title: Add /api/reflect endpoint for KB synthesis suggestions
description:
Surface reflect-suggestions.json data in web UI.

Data source: ~/.orch/reflect-suggestions.json
- Generated by 'orch daemon reflect' or 'kb reflect'
- Shows topics with multiple investigations needing consolidation

Current data shows:
- 'orch' topic: 29 investigations (!)
- Other topics with 3+ investigations each

Endpoint should return:
- synthesis: topics needing consolidation (3+ investigations)
- promote: kn entries ready for kb promotion
- stale: decisions with 0 citations, >7 days old
- timestamp: when suggestions were generated

UI usage:
- 'Knowledge Debt' panel showing topics needing synthesis
- Badge count of actionable items
- Links to specific investigations

This surfaces 'what knowledge work is piling up' - helps prioritize documentation work.
close_reason: Verified /api/reflect endpoint already implemented in commit 88b71037. Endpoint returns reflect-suggestions.json data with timestamp and synthesis array. Tests pass, documentation updated.

title: Add kb reflect --type open to surface investigations with unimplemented recommendations
description:
kb reflect currently finds clusters by topic prefix but misses open action items.

**Problem:** Investigations with actionable 'Next:' recommendations get buried. Example: FAILURE_REPORT.md template recommended in inv-failure-mode-artifacts but never implemented.

**Solution:** Add --type open mode that:
1. Finds investigations where Status ≠ Complete  
2. Extracts the 'Next:' field
3. Filters out close/none/complete/implemented
4. Shows what's actionable

**Why not LLM:** D.E.K.N. structure already standardizes where recommendations live. Pattern matching on Next: field is fast, free, and deterministic. Template is the forcing function.

**Test:** rg '\*\*Next:\*\*' .kb/investigations/*.md | grep -vi 'close|none|complete|implemented'
Currently returns 18 open items that kb reflect doesn't surface.

title: [orch-go] investigation: Test headless spawn with directory fix - just s...
Add outputs.required to artifact-producing skills
description:
## Problem

Only `investigation` skill has `outputs.required` defined. Other artifact-producing skills have no verification gates.

## Skills to Update

| Skill | Artifact Pattern | Structure Checks |
|-------|------------------|------------------|
| systematic-debugging | `.kb/investigations/{date}-*debug*.md` | D.E.K.N., self-review |
| reliability-testing | `.kb/investigations/{date}-*reliability*.md` | D.E.K.N., go/no-go section |
| research | `.kb/investigations/{date}-*research*.md` | D.E.K.N., recommendation section |
| architect | `.kb/decisions/{date}-*.md` OR `.kb/investigations/{date}-*.md` | D.E.K.N. |
| codebase-audit | `.kb/investigations/{date}-*audit*.md` | D.E.K.N., findings section |

## Implementation

Add to each skill's `.skillc/skill.yaml`:

```yaml
outputs:
  required:
    - pattern: ".kb/investigations/{date}-*[pattern]*.md"
      description: "[Skill] file with findings"
  optional:
    - pattern: ".kb/decisions/{date}-*.md"
      description: "Promoted decision (if recommendation accepted)"
```

## Not Covered (different verification needed)

- **feature-impl** - Output is commits, not files
- **issue-creation** - Output is beads issue
- **design-session** - Output varies (epic/investigation/decision)
- **kb-reflect** - Output is kb actions

These need verification in `orch complete`, not `skillc verify`.

## Success Criteria

- [ ] systematic-debugging has outputs.required
- [ ] reliability-testing has outputs.required  
- [ ] research has outputs.required
- [ ] architect has outputs.required
- [ ] codebase-audit has outputs.required
- [ ] All skills rebuild with `skillc build`
- [ ] `skillc verify` works on each skill
close_reason: Added outputs.required to 4 artifact-producing skills (codebase-audit, architect, research, reliability-testing). All verify tests pass. Committed to ~/.claude/skills and orch-go.

title: Implement skill size reduction and compact output flags
description:
Investigation: 2026-01-16-inv-analyze-orchestration-session-hit-context.md

Context: Orchestration sessions hit context limits prematurely because 67% of context consumed by setup (orchestrator skill = 2,385 lines, ~28K tokens).

Recommendation: Create 'core' (500-800 lines) and 'reference' (2,385 lines) skill variants. Add --compact flag to orch status (show only running/recent agents, not all 182).

Key insight: Skill content + tool output must fit within context budget alongside actual work.

Evidence: Session consumed 56K tokens before work began - skill embedding alone was ~50% of setup context.

title: Orchestrators should know registered projects via kb projects list
description:
## Problem

Meta-orchestrators and orchestrators don't know where orch-managed projects are. When asked to spawn work in another project (e.g., price-watch), they have to search for paths.

## Solution

Include `kb projects list` output (or summary) in orchestrator context so they know:
- What projects exist
- Where they're located
- Can spawn cross-project work without path hunting

## Evidence

Meta-orchestrator couldn't find price-watch path until running `kb projects list`.

## Implementation Options

1. Add to ORCHESTRATOR_CONTEXT.md template
2. Add to meta-orchestrator skill context
3. Create `orch projects` command that wraps kb projects list
close_reason: (light tier - no synthesis by design)

title: Auto-switch accounts before hitting rate limits
description: Before orch spawn, check current account usage. If 5-hour session >80% OR weekly >90%, auto-switch to alternate account (if available and has capacity). Prevents friction from hitting limits mid-work. Should check both accounts and pick the one with most headroom.
close_reason: Implemented auto-account-switching with configurable thresholds (5h >80%, weekly >90%). Switches to alternate account with better headroom before spawn. All tests pass, committed as 6f9539c.

title: Add --workdir flag to orch tail for cross-project agent tailing
description:
**Symptom:** Cannot tail cross-project agents - `orch tail pw-6j8t` fails with 'no agent found' because it only checks current project.

**Expected:** `orch tail pw-6j8t --workdir /path/to/price-watch` should work like other cross-project commands.

**Related:** Similar to --workdir support in orch complete. Need to pass x-opencode-directory header when querying OpenCode API for session messages.
close_reason: Added --workdir flag to orch tail for cross-project agent tailing. Flag uses target project directory for workspace search and OpenCode API x-opencode-directory header. Includes helpful error messages suggesting --workdir when issue ID appears to belong to different project.

title: Dashboard: Debounce gold 'processing' border on agent cards
description: UX issue: Gold processing border toggles rapidly on/off as agent switches between active tool calls and waiting. Visually distracting. Fix: Debounce the 'processing' state - stay gold for 5-10 seconds after last activity before transitioning to idle appearance. Could also add CSS transition for smoother visual change.
close_reason: Debounced gold processing border from 1s to 5s delay, added 500ms CSS transitions for smooth fades. Removed animate-pulse (solid gold sufficient). Commit: de002c60. Leave it Better: kn-a9e5ef.

title: Add orch doctor --sessions for workspace/session cross-reference
description:
## Problem
No way to detect:
- Workspaces whose sessions were garbage-collected
- Sessions without corresponding workspaces
- Zombie sessions that appear active but are stuck

## Impact
No systematic cleanup or zombie detection.

## Solution
Add `orch doctor --sessions` that cross-references:
1. Workspaces in `.orch/workspace/` with their `.session_id` files
2. Active sessions from OpenCode API (`GET /session`)
3. Registry entries in `~/.orch/registry.json`

## Output
```
orch doctor --sessions
Workspaces: 288
Sessions: 15 active
Orphaned workspaces: 3 (session deleted)
Orphaned sessions: 1 (no workspace)
Zombie sessions: 0
```

## References
- Investigation: .kb/investigations/2026-01-06-inv-workspace-session-architecture.md
close_reason: Enhanced orch doctor --sessions to cross-reference workspaces, OpenCode sessions, and registry with zombie detection. Output matches spec. All tests passing.

title: Fix tmux socket path for orch serve running inside overmind
description:
**Problem:** Dashboard context API and follow mode fail because orch serve connects to wrong tmux server.

**Root cause:** Overmind runs services inside its own tmux server with socket at /var/folders/.../overmind-orch-go-*. When orch serve runs tmux commands without specifying socket, it connects to overmind's tmux (no orchestrator session) instead of main tmux at /tmp/tmux-501/default.

**Evidence:**
- `tmux display-message -t orchestrator -p '#{window_index}'` works from shell (main tmux)
- Same command fails from orch serve (overmind's tmux)
- Two tmux servers visible in `ps aux | grep tmux`

**Solution:** Add `-S /tmp/tmux-$(id -u)/default` to tmux commands in pkg/tmux/tmux.go, or detect the correct socket path.

**Affected functionality:**
- /api/context endpoint
- Dashboard 'Follow Orchestrator' mode  
- Tmux auto-switch (sync-workers-session.sh works because it runs from shell)

**Location:** pkg/tmux/tmux.go - tmuxCommand() and findTmux() functions
close_reason: Phase: Complete - Fixed Dashboard /api/context by detecting main tmux socket when running inside overmind. Tests: go test ./pkg/tmux/... - all passing. API endpoint verified returning orchestrator cwd.

title: Real-time agent activity display in dashboard
description:
Dashboard should show live agent activity for headless spawns:
1. Filter active vs completed sessions (currently shows stale sessions)
2. Show current activity per agent (last message, current tool, tokens)
3. Live updates via SSE as agents work

Current gap: /api/agents returns all sessions including completed ones from days ago. Need to detect which sessions are actively running vs idle/completed.

Related: orch-go-36b (dashboard visibility investigation)
close_reason: Implemented real-time agent activity display with live SSE updates. Added activity tracking to agent cards, message.part event parsing, Active Only filter, and granular store updates. All code committed and builds successfully.

title: orch complete fails for orchestrator sessions - beads lookup before workspace detection
description:
orch complete for orchestrator sessions (which don't have beads tracking) tries beads lookup first, failing before it can detect the workspace is an orchestrator session.

The complete flow should:
1. Check if workspace exists and has ORCHESTRATOR_CONTEXT.md or .tier=orchestrator
2. If orchestrator session, use registry/workspace-based completion
3. Only fall back to beads lookup for worker sessions

Current behavior: beads lookup first, fails for orchestrator sessions.

title: feat-037: Show orchestrator sessions in orch status
description:
Add orchestrator sessions from registry to orch status output.

## Context
Decision 2026-01-05-orchestrator-lifecycle-without-beads.md establishes session registry for orchestrators. Status command needs to show these alongside worker agents.

## Implementation
- In cmd/orch/status_cmd.go
- Read from session registry (feat-035)
- Display orchestrator sessions in a separate section or with distinct indicator
- Show: workspace name, goal, duration, project

## Display Format (suggested)
```
ORCHESTRATOR SESSIONS
  WORKSPACE                          GOAL                    DURATION  PROJECT
  og-orch-ship-feature-05jan         Ship the feature        2h 15m    orch-go
  meta-work-review-patterns-05jan    Review patterns         45m       -

AGENTS
  BEADS ID    STATUS    PHASE      ...
```

## Success Criteria
- Active orchestrator sessions shown in status
- Distinct from worker agents (different section or indicator)
- Shows useful info: workspace, goal, duration
- Empty state handled gracefully

## Dependencies
- feat-035 (session registry) must be implemented first

## References
- Decision: .kb/decisions/2026-01-05-orchestrator-lifecycle-without-beads.md
close_reason: Added ORCHESTRATOR SESSIONS section to orch status output. Shows workspace, goal, duration, and project from session registry. Supports --project filtering, adaptive terminal formatting (wide/narrow/card), and --json output. Empty state handled gracefully.

title: Add OpenCode launchd plist for auto-start and KeepAlive
description:
OpenCode serve is the foundation that orch daemon and orch serve depend on, but it has no auto-start mechanism.

Current state:
- com.orch.daemon.plist exists (orch daemon run)
- com.orch-go.serve.plist exists (orch serve)  
- NO opencode launchd plist - manual start required

Problem: When OpenCode crashes or returns errors (like 'redirected too many times'), the entire orchestration system stalls. Daemon can't spawn, orch serve can't query sessions.

Deliverable:
1. Create ~/Library/LaunchAgents/com.opencode.serve.plist
   - ProgramArguments: opencode serve --port 4096
   - RunAtLoad: true
   - KeepAlive: true (restart on crash)
   - StandardOutPath: ~/.orch/logs/opencode.log
   - StandardErrorPath: ~/.orch/logs/opencode.err
   - EnvironmentVariables: PATH with node/bun locations

2. Test: launchctl bootstrap, verify auto-restart on kill

3. Document in orchestrator skill under 'System Maintenance'

Reference: ~/Library/LaunchAgents/com.orch.daemon.plist for pattern
close_reason: Implemented directly: Created ~/Library/LaunchAgents/com.opencode.serve.plist with RunAtLoad and KeepAlive. Verified auto-restart works.

title: Phase 1: Service monitoring daemon with crash notifications
description: Background goroutine in orch serve that polls overmind status every 10s, tracks PIDs to detect crashes, and emits desktop notifications. Solves silent service failures (e.g., opencode crash discovered only when orch status fails). Implementation follows architecture from orch-go-e79ao. Success criteria: kill service process → notification within 15s, CPU overhead <0.5%. See .orch/workspace/og-arch-design-observability-infrastructure-09jan-3e5e/SYNTHESIS.md for full design.
close_reason: Service monitoring daemon implemented and validated. Detects crashes in 2-3s (target 15s), auto-restarts services, logs events to events.jsonl, CPU overhead 0.0% (target <0.5%). Evidence: opencode crashed twice and auto-restarted successfully. Code committed in 9f249e88. Agent stuck at completion phase - orchestrator validated manually.

title: Dashboard shows 'Waiting for activity' - OpenCode API returns 0 messages despite messages existing on disk

title: Track fix attempts on issues - surface retry patterns
description:
When an issue takes multiple attempts to fix (like orch-go-k5lp streaming dedup - 3 attempts), that's a process signal we should capture and learn from.

Possible implementation:
1. Add 'attempts' field to beads issues (increment on reopen or related spawn)
2. Track which issues get reopened or spawn follow-up fixes
3. Surface patterns: which skills/problem-types have high retry rates?
4. Feed into orch learn - 'this type of bug often takes multiple attempts'

This connects to the System Learning Loop - we track context gaps, we should also track fix reliability gaps.

Related: orch-go-k5lp (3 attempts), possibly others we haven't noticed.
close_reason: Added fix attempt tracking: pkg/verify/attempts.go with FixAttemptStats, spawn warning for retry patterns, orch retries command. All tests passing.

title: triage:ready on epic should cascade to children or daemon should infer
description:
**Problem:** When labeling an epic `triage:ready`, the intent is 'process this epic now'. But the daemon only spawns individual issues, not epics. The label doesn't propagate to children.

**Current behavior:**
```
bd label add epic-xyz triage:ready
orch daemon preview
# Shows: epic-xyz type 'epic' not spawnable
# Children still show: missing label 'triage:ready'
```

**Expected behavior (option A - cascade):**
```
bd label add epic-xyz triage:ready --cascade
# Adds triage:ready to all unblocked children
```

**Expected behavior (option B - daemon infers):**
```
bd label add epic-xyz triage:ready
orch daemon preview
# Daemon sees epic is triage:ready
# Auto-includes unblocked children in spawn queue
```

**User mental model:**
'Label the epic ready for the daemon' means 'ensure the entire epic is processed now' - not 'label this one non-spawnable thing'.

**Related:**
- orch-go-tuofe: Children shouldn't have blocking dep on parent (separate bug)
- kb-8294e7: Constraint about --parent creating blocking deps

**Design question:**
Should this be a beads feature (--cascade flag) or daemon behavior (infer from epic label)? Probably daemon - beads shouldn't know about triage workflow.
close_reason: Implemented daemon epic child inference - children of triage:ready epics are now auto-included in spawn queue

title: Implement session end validation + interactive completion (Capture at Context)
description:
Implement validation gate and interactive completion for orch session end per Capture at Context principle.

## Context
- Decision: .kb/decisions/2026-01-14-capture-at-context.md
- Problem: Handoffs incomplete because gate fires at wrong moment (end vs throughout)
- Current: promptForSessionSummary only asks Outcome + Brief Summary
- Need: Validate all sections, prompt for unfilled ones

## Design (approved by Dylan)

### Sections to validate (with placeholder patterns):
1. Outcome - {success | partial | blocked | failed} (required)
2. TLDR - [Fill within first 5 tool calls:...] (required)
3. Where We Ended - {state of focus goal now} (required)  
4. Next Recommendation - {continue-focus | shift-focus | escalate | pause} (required)
5. Evidence - [Pattern 1: e.g., (optional, allow 'nothing notable')
6. Knowledge - {topic}: (optional, allow 'none')
7. Friction - [Tool gap or UX issue] (optional, allow 'smooth')

### Flow:
1. validateHandoff() - check which sections have placeholders
2. promptForUnfilledSections() - interactive prompts for each
3. updateHandoffWithResponses() - replace placeholders
4. Archive only when complete

### Files to modify:
- cmd/orch/session.go - replace promptForSessionSummary with new validation system
- Remove old updateHandoffTemplate function
- Update runSessionEnd to use new completeAndArchiveHandoff function

## Tests needed:
- Test validation detects unfilled sections
- Test interactive completion flow
- Test placeholder replacement
close_reason: Session end validation implemented. Tests verified: TestValidateHandoff*, TestUpdateHandoffWithResponses, TestHandoffSectionsDefinition - all PASS

title: [orch-go] investigation: test third concurrent spawn
Consolidate session-context.js and orch-session-autostart.ts into single plugin
description:
## Problem

Two separate OpenCode plugins with overlapping concerns:

| Plugin | Purpose | Event |
|--------|---------|-------|
| session-context.js | Loads orchestrator skill | config hook |
| orch-session-autostart.ts | Runs orch session start | session.created |

Both:
- Detect workers (same logic, duplicated)
- Run at session start
- Are orchestrator-specific
- Live in ~/.config/opencode/plugin/

## Why Consolidate

1. **DRY** - isWorker() logic duplicated across both files
2. **Consistency** - Same detection should use same code
3. **Maintenance** - Single point of change for orchestrator session setup
4. **Clarity** - One 'orchestrator session plugin' vs two partial plugins

## Proposed Solution

Create single `orchestrator-session.ts` plugin that:
1. Uses `config` hook to inject orchestrator skill (from session-context.js)
2. Uses `session.created` to auto-start orch session (from orch-session-autostart.ts)
3. Shares isWorker() detection logic
4. Removes the two old plugins

## Implementation

```typescript
export const OrchestratorSessionPlugin: Plugin = async ({ directory, $ }) => {
  // Shared worker detection
  const worker = await isWorker(directory)
  
  return {
    config: async (config) => {
      if (worker) return
      // Inject orchestrator skill
      config.instructions.push(skillPath)
    },
    event: async ({ event }) => {
      if (event.type !== 'session.created') return
      if (worker) return
      // Auto-start orch session
      await $\`orch session start\`
    }
  }
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Single orchestrator-session.ts plugin created
- [ ] session-context.js removed
- [ ] orch-session-autostart.ts removed
- [ ] Both behaviors preserved (skill injection + session auto-start)
- [ ] Worker detection shared, not duplicated

## References

- ~/.config/opencode/plugin/session-context.js
- ~/.config/opencode/plugin/orch-session-autostart.ts
- .kb/investigations/2025-12-29-inv-create-opencode-plugin-orch-session.md
close_reason: Consolidated session-context.js and orch-session-autostart.ts into single orchestrator-session.ts plugin at ~/.config/opencode/plugin/. Shared isWorker() detection cached at init, both config (skill injection) and event (session auto-start) hooks preserved. Investigation files updated with lineage. Leave it Better: kn command not available, but learning noted in investigation history (cache detection at init for efficiency).

title: Daemon blocks child tasks when parent epic is in_progress
description:
## Problem

Child tasks with `triage:ready` label are blocked by daemon when their parent epic is `in_progress`. This is backwards - `in_progress` parent should **unblock** children, not block them.

## Expected Behavior

- Parent `open` → children blocked (epic not started)
- Parent `in_progress` → children **unblocked** (epic is active, work should proceed)
- Parent `closed` → children unblocked (or auto-closed?)

## Workaround

Remove parent relationship: `bd update pw-xxxx.1 --parent ""`

## Context

Discovered in price-watch project when creating epic pw-u8th with 4 child tasks. All tasks had `triage:ready` but daemon wouldn't pick them up until parent relationship was removed.
close_reason: Root cause: GetBlockingDependencies treated all deps as blocking when status != closed. Fix: parent-child deps now only block when parent is 'open', not 'in_progress'. Tests: go test ./pkg/beads/... -run TestGetBlockingDependencies - 11 passed (6 original + 5 new parent-child tests). Commit: 5538c022

title: Usage stats in navbar unclear - '40% 5h 25% wk' not immediately understandable
description:
## Problem

The usage stats shown as '40% 5h 25% wk personal' are cryptic. Users need to decipher what each number means.

## Evidence

Screenshot shows: '40% 5h 25% wk personal • connected'

Meaning (if you know):
- 40% = hourly usage
- 5h = hours until reset
- 25% = weekly usage  
- wk = weekly indicator
- personal = account name

## Expected

Make usage immediately understandable:
- Add labels: '40% hourly | 25% weekly'
- Or use icons with tooltips
- Or restructure: '25% weekly (40% this hour) • resets in 5h'
- Show which limit is constraining (hourly vs weekly)

## Files
- web/src/routes/+page.svelte - navbar usage display
- cmd/orch/serve.go - usage API response
close_reason: Added tooltips and clearer labels to navbar usage stats. Changes: 5h→5hr, wk→week, added | separator, tooltips explain rolling window and weekly reset. Commit 28fe665.

title: Consolidate artifact templates into kb-cli
description:
## Problem

Artifact templates are fragmented across multiple locations:
- orch-knowledge/skills/src/worker/{skill}/templates/
- ~/.claude/skills/worker/{skill}/templates/
- ~/.kb/templates/
- ~/.orch/templates/
- kb-cli hardcoded defaults

This causes:
- Drift between copies (e.g., investigation template has D.E.K.N. in some places, not others)
- Confusion about which is authoritative
- Templates in skill directories that skillc doesn't manage

## Solution

1. **kb-cli owns artifact templates** (investigation, decision, guide, research)
   - Update hardcoded defaults in kb-cli with current best versions
   - User overrides at ~/.kb/templates/
   - Add `kb create research` command if missing

2. **Remove templates from skill directories**
   - Delete orch-knowledge/skills/src/worker/investigation/templates/
   - Delete orch-knowledge/skills/src/worker/research/templates/
   - Delete ~/.claude/skills/worker/*/templates/

3. **orch-go owns spawn-time templates** (already correct)
   - SYNTHESIS.md, SPAWN_CONTEXT.md, etc. stay in .orch/templates/

4. **Sync ~/.kb/templates/ with kb-cli defaults**
   - Or remove user overrides if they match defaults

## Scope

| Template | Current Locations | Target |
|----------|------------------|--------|
| investigation.md | 5 places | kb-cli only |
| research.md | 2 places | kb-cli only |
| decision | 2 places | kb-cli only |
| guide | 1 place | kb-cli only |

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] kb-cli hardcoded templates updated with D.E.K.N. + structured uncertainty
- [ ] `kb create research` works (add if missing)
- [ ] Templates removed from skill directories
- [ ] Skills reference `kb create` instead of embedding templates
- [ ] ~/.kb/templates/ cleaned up or synced

title: Implement auto-switch account when capacity is low
description: Before spawning, check current account capacity. If <20% remaining on 5-hour limit, automatically switch to healthier account. Account package has capacity functions but no auto-switch orchestration.

title: Epic: Dashboard 'Needs Attention' Redesign
description:
## Problem

The dashboard 'Needs Attention' section is broken in multiple ways:
- Shows noise, not actionable items
- 'Pending Reviews' with 72 items creates anxiety, not action
- 'Blocked issues' surfaces expected behavior (sequential deps) as problems
- No clear actions - items shown without verbs/commands

## Goal

Transform 'Needs Attention' from overwhelming backlog into focused decision surface.

## Success Criteria

- [ ] Small actionable count (3-5 items max normally)
- [ ] Each item has a clear action verb
- [ ] Only surfaces items requiring human intervention
- [ ] Dismissed items don't resurface inappropriately

## Child Issues

Consolidates:
- orch-go-fqb8: Redesign for actionable decisions (broad scope)
- orch-go-t404: Blocked issues misleading (specific fix)
- orch-go-va5v: Replace 'Pending Reviews' with 'Recent Knowledge'

## Design Direction

From orch-go-fqb8:
```
Needs Attention (3)

🔴 BLOCKING: 2 agents at Phase: Complete need review
   → Click to complete

⚠️  DECISION NEEDED: 1 issue needs triage
   → Click to triage

📊 PATTERN: 'chrome launch' gap recurring (5x)
   → Consider: kn constrain
```

Categories prioritized: BLOCKING > DECISION NEEDED > PATTERNS

title: Standardize binary installation location across CLI tools
description: Pattern: binaries get installed to both ~/bin/ and ~/go/bin/, causing PATH shadowing where stale binaries mask new ones. Hit with orch (stale binary), kb (stale binary). Investigate: standardize on one location, add staleness check, or make install scripts consistent across orch-go, kb-cli, bd.
close_reason: Investigation complete. Root cause: 3 of 6 CLI tools use go install (→ ~/go/bin) while PATH gives ~/go/bin priority over ~/bin. Recommendation: Standardize on orch-go Makefile pattern (build → copy to ~/bin → codesign). Follow-up issues needed for beads, skillc, agentlog. Constraint recorded via kn.

title: Epic: Split serve_agents.go into focused handler files
description:
## Problem

serve_agents.go (1399 lines) is part of serve.go hotspot cluster (34 fix commits in 28 days). 

## Goal

Split serve_agents.go into focused files by endpoint domain, similar to how serve_beads.go, serve_errors.go, serve_learn.go were already extracted.

## Approach

1. Spawn architect to analyze and design split
2. Extract by endpoint groupings
3. Verify tests pass after each extraction

## Success Criteria

- serve_agents.go reduced to routing + shared utilities
- Each handler domain in its own file
- All tests pass
- Fix commit density decreases

title: Fix guarded-files plugin false positives on documentation
description:
Guarded-files plugin triggers on files containing 'AUTO-GENERATED' anywhere in content, not just file headers.

**Fix approach:**
- Check if AUTO-GENERATED appears in first N lines (header area)
- Or check if it's in a comment block at the start of the file
- Not just `content.includes('AUTO-GENERATED')`

**Files:**

title: Active agent titles sometimes unhelpful (e.g. 'Orchestrator skill says')
description:
## Problem

Active agent cards show titles that aren't meaningful. Example: 'Orchestrator skill says' doesn't tell the user what the agent is actually doing.

## Evidence

Screenshot shows an active agent with title 'Orchestrator skill says' - this is the start of the task description but truncated unhelpfully.

## Root Cause

Title is derived from task description which may start with context rather than the actual task. For investigations spawned with questions, the question text becomes the title.

## Expected

Show a more useful title:
- Use beads issue title if available
- Fall back to skill + short task summary
- Consider extracting the actual task from longer descriptions

## Files
- web/src/routes/+page.svelte - AgentCard title display
- cmd/orch/serve.go - agent data sent to UI
close_reason: Added collapsed section preview showing agent task summaries. When sections are collapsed, header now displays first 1-2 agent tasks with +N suffix. All tests pass, commit 185e07b.

title: Trial: One week daemon-first workflow (Jan 6-13)
description:
## Purpose

Build trust in daemon through direct experience, not theory.

## Rules

- Issues via `bd create` → daemon picks them up
- No `orch spawn` for workers (unless true emergency)
- Meta-orchestrator produces issues, not spawns
- `--bypass-triage` only for orchestrator sessions or emergencies

## Observe

- Skill inference accuracy
- Context quality
- Timing (30-60s delay)
- Completion quality

## Capture

- Every friction point

title: action-log.ts plugin logs duplicate entries - same timestamp appearing twice
description: Observed duplicate entries in ~/.orch/action-log.jsonl with identical timestamps. Plugin has deduplication logic (loggedCalls Set) but it's not preventing duplicates. Suggests either: 1) tool.execute.after hook called twice per action, 2) multiple plugin instances, or 3) callID not unique across calls. Evidence: tail -100 ~/.orch/action-log.jsonl shows many entries appearing exactly twice with same timestamp.
close_reason: Fixed duplicate action-log entries by implementing file-based lock deduplication with atomic operations. Changes already committed in 4641be0b. Requires OpenCode restart to take effect.

title: Dashboard doesn't detect completion for untracked agents
description:
## Problem

Dashboard shows untracked agents (--no-track) as 'active' indefinitely because:
1. No beads issue exists → no Phase: Complete comment to check
2. Falls back to 10-minute activity timeout only

## Evidence

Agent orch-knowledge-untracked-1766690045 completed its work (committed to repo) but dashboard shows it as Active at 7m 25s.

## Solution

For agents without beads tracking, check alternative completion signals:
1. SYNTHESIS.md exists in workspace → completed (full tier)
2. .tier file says 'light' AND commits exist → completed (light tier)
3. OpenCode session is_processing=false for >2 minutes → idle

## Related

Fix orch-go-k08g only addressed tracked agents with beads Phase: Complete comments.
close_reason: Added workspace SYNTHESIS.md check as fallback for untracked agent completion detection. Tests pass, implementation follows existing patterns.

title: Add dedup check before spawn to prevent duplicate sessions for same beads issue
description:
**Problem:** Daemon spawns new sessions for the same beads issue instead of resuming existing ones.

**Impact:**
- Multiple OpenCode sessions for same issue (e.g., 12+ sessions for orch-go-nqgjr)
- All recent sessions count toward concurrency limit
- Concurrency limit hit even though only 2 agents actually running

**Evidence (Jan 15):**
- orch-go-nqgjr: 12+ sessions all within 2 hours
- price-watch 'investigate run 93': 4 duplicate sessions

**Solution:** Check for existing session with same beads ID before spawn. If found and recent, resume instead of spawn fresh.

**Related:**
- Stuck agent recovery design
- Ghost filtering decision
close_reason: Phase: Complete - Design validation complete: two-layer dedup (session-level + TTL backup) is substrate-aligned, observability gap identified, feat-048 added for telemetry implementation

title: Bug: Worker agents cause tmux session switch from orchestrator to workers-orch-go
description:
When headless worker agents run, something causes the tmux client to switch from the 'orchestrator' session to 'workers-orch-go' session, disrupting the orchestrator's workflow.

## Symptoms
- Dylan is in 'orchestrator' tmux session
- Spawns headless agents (confirmed: spawn_mode=headless in events)
- tmux suddenly switches to 'workers-orch-go' session
- Has to manually switch back

## Investigation needed
1. What triggers the switch? (agents all ran headless, not tmux mode)
2. Is it orch servers, daemon, or something else?
3. Could be glass tools or playwright triggering something?

## Constraint
Headless spawns should NEVER affect the orchestrator's tmux session.
close_reason: Investigation complete: orch-go headless spawn code does NOT interact with tmux. Bug is external. See .kb/investigations/2026-01-08-inv-bug-worker-agents-cause-tmux.md

title: Registry file needs self-describing header
description:
## Problem

When orchestrator tried to manually edit `~/.orch/registry.json` to clean up untracked agents, the structure was unexpected. Multiple jq attempts failed because the format wasn't documented.

## Evidence (session ses_474f)

Lines 230-265: Orchestrator tried various jq queries assuming different structures, all failed.

## Solution

Add a comment header or companion `.schema.json` explaining:
- What each field means
- Valid values for status/phase
- How entries are keyed
- What operations are safe

Example header approach (JSON5 or separate file):
```
// Registry Schema:
// - Key: session_id (from OpenCode)
// - Fields: workspace, issue_id, status, phase, started_at, updated_at
// - Status values: active, idle, completed, abandoned
// - Safe operations: read-only; use orch commands to modify
```

## Principle

**Self-Describing Artifacts** - 'If an agent encounters this file with no memory of how it was created, can they modify it correctly?' Currently: no.
close_reason: Zombie reconciled

title: Daemon doesn't see issues with newly added labels
description:
## Problem

Daemon polling doesn't pick up issues when labels are added after creation. orch-go-7rgz has `triage:ready` and `skill:feature-impl` labels but daemon log shows it's not in the 10 issues found.

## Evidence

```
bd list --status open --limit 0 | grep 7rgz
orch-go-7rgz [P1] [feature] open [skill:feature-impl triage:ready] - Add periodic kb reflect to daemon

tail ~/.orch/daemon.log
DEBUG: Found 10 open issues
# 7rgz not listed, not skipped - simply not found
```

Labels were added via `bd update --add-label` after issue creation. Daemon restart didn't help.

## Likely Cause

`ListReadyIssues()` in `pkg/daemon/issue_adapter.go` may be caching or using a stale query.

## Success Criteria

- Issues with newly added labels appear in daemon's next poll cycle
- No restart required
close_reason: Root cause: bd ready defaults to limit 10. Fix: added --limit 0 to both RPC and CLI paths. Tests: go build ./... - success, test-limit-fix.go - 20 issues found (was 10). Commits: e6aeb559 (fix), 21ce441e (docs). Note: Restart daemon to apply fix: launchctl kickstart -k gui/$(id -u)/com.orch.daemon

title: Document decision authority criteria: when agents can decide vs escalate to human
description:
Currently there's no clear documented criteria for when agents/architects can make decisions autonomously vs when to escalate to Dylan.

## The Gap
- spawn.md mentions 'Authority levels (what agent can decide vs escalate)' but doesn't define criteria
- Orchestrator skill mentions escalation but no concrete rules
- Result: orchestrators are overly cautious, asking Dylan about things agents could handle

## Observed Pattern (implicit, needs documenting)

| Escalate to Dylan | Agent can decide |
|-------------------|------------------|
| Strategic direction changes | Implementation details |
| User-facing behavior changes | Internal conventions |
| Trade-offs with unclear values | Trade-offs with clear criteria |
| Irreversible decisions | Reversible decisions |
| Cross-project impact | Single-project scope |
| New capabilities/features | Refinements to existing features |
| Resource allocation (cost, time) | Technical approach within budget |

## Examples
- Workspace naming convention → Agent can decide (implementation detail, reversible)
- Whether to use beads for orchestrators → Dylan decides (strategic, changes mental model)
- Which test framework to use → Agent can decide (technical, reversible)
- Whether to support a new platform → Dylan decides (strategic, resource commitment)

## Deliverables
1. Add 'Decision Authority' section to .kb/guides/ (new file or extend existing)
2. Update orchestrator skill to reference the criteria
3. Update SPAWN_CONTEXT.md template to include authority level for the specific task

## References
- .kb/guides/spawn.md mentions authority but doesn't define
- Orchestrator skill has implicit patterns
close_reason: Created .kb/guides/decision-authority.md with six-axis decision framework, decision tree, and examples. Updated spawn.md and SPAWN_CONTEXT.md template to reference the guide.

title: Extend daemon to run open reflection with issue creation
description:
## Problem

Daemon only runs synthesis reflection with issue creation. Open type (investigations with forgotten Next: actions) should also auto-create issues.

## Solution

1. Add ReflectOpenEnabled config (default: true when ReflectCreateIssues is true)
2. Add open reflection to daemon run loop (hourly, same as synthesis)
3. Call kb reflect --type open --create-issue

## Blocked By

**kb-cli-538**: Add --create-issue support for open type in kb reflect

This issue cannot be implemented until kb-cli supports `kb reflect --type open --create-issue`.

## Success Criteria

- Investigations >3 days with unimplemented Next: actions get triage:review issues
- No false positives from paused or just-created investigations
close_reason: No longer relevant per decision 2026-01-07-synthesis-is-strategic-orchestrator-work. Auto-issue creation caused duplicate noise. Reflection opportunities now surfaced via orch status instead.

title: Interactive orchestrator sessions don't create workspaces or SESSION_HANDOFF.md
description:
## Problem
Interactive orchestrator sessions (opened directly via opencode/oc) don't get workspaces like spawned sessions do. This means:
- No SESSION_HANDOFF.md on exit
- No .session_id file linking to OpenCode session
- Session context lost on exit

## Impact
Session amnesia - next orchestrator has no context about what was done.

## Contrast
Spawned orchestrators via `orch spawn orchestrator` DO get workspaces with ORCHESTRATOR_CONTEXT.md and produce SESSION_HANDOFF.md.

## Potential Solutions
1. OpenCode plugin that creates workspace on session.created for orchestrator sessions
2. `orch session start` creates workspace (already has some of this?)
3. Manual discipline (current workaround)

## Reference
Discovered in session 2026-01-06 while wrapping up.
close_reason: Fixed interactive orchestrator sessions not creating workspaces. Added createSessionWorkspace() to session.go that creates ~/.orch/session/{date}/SESSION_HANDOFF.md on orch session start. Tests: go test ./pkg/session/... ./pkg/spawn/... - all 178 tests passing. Smoke test verified workspace creation works.

title: Replace duplicate agent counts with usage progress bars in stats bar
description:
## Problem

Stats bar shows active/recent/archive counts that are already visible in Swarm Map section headers. This wastes horizontal space (critical at 666px width constraint).

## Solution

Replace agent count section with usage progress bars:
- Before: `1 active | 25 recent | 184 archive | 0 errors`
- After: `[████░░] 64% hr ↻5hr | [██░░░░] 28% wk | 0 errors`

## Design

Progress bars for hourly and weekly usage:
- Green (<50%), Yellow (50-80%), Red (>80%)
- Show reset time for hourly (the constraining limit)
- Compact format to fit 666px width

## Keep
- 0 errors (not duplicated)
- focused indicator
- beads ready/blocked  
- servers status
- @personal account indicator

## Remove
- active count (shown in Active section header)
- recent count (shown in Recent section header)
- archive count (shown in Archive section header)

## Files
- web/src/routes/+page.svelte - stats bar layout
close_reason: Replaced duplicate active/recent/archive counts with usage progress bars showing hourly and weekly limits with reset times. Extended /api/usage to include reset times. Commit e708c66.

title: Fix session discovery to scan all windows when current window has no history
description: discoverSessionHandoff() needs cross-window scan between current-window check and legacy fallback. Scan all .orch/session/*/latest, pick most recent by timestamp. Enables: same window = window history (isolation), new window = most recent across all (convenience).
close_reason: Root cause: missing cross-window scan in discovery flow. Fix: added scanAllWindowsForMostRecent() helper and inserted call between current-window check and legacy fallback. Tests: go test ./cmd/orch -run 'Session|Handoff' - 10 passed, 0 failed (includes new TestDiscoverSessionHandoff_CrossWindowScan)

title: Add workspace_path tracking to session.json
description:
## Enhancement

Optional improvement to session tracking:

- Add workspace_path field to Session struct in pkg/session/session.go
- Update createSessionWorkspace() to save workspace path to session.json
- Display workspace location in 'orch session status' output
- Enable 'orch session end' to easily find/validate workspace

## Context

Current implementation creates workspaces at ~/.orch/session/{date}/ but doesn't track the path in session.json. This works fine but could be more discoverable.

## Priority

P3 - Nice to have, not blocking. Current implementation is functional without this.

title: Knowledge System Cleanup - Reduce Before Adding
description:
Strategic cleanup of knowledge system before adding more instrumentation. Goal: complete the synthesis loop and reduce noise so future tooling has clean data to work with.

## Context
- 629 investigations accumulating (not archived after synthesis)
- orch patterns showing noise (sleep, generic globs)
- kb reflect duplicating orch's synthesis detection
- Meta-topics (investigation, synthesis) polluting suggestions

## Phases

### Phase 1: Reduce Noise
- [ ] feat-043: Add meta-topic exclusions to synthesis detection
- [ ] Fix kb reflect to align with orch (or dedupe)
- [ ] Prune orch patterns noise

### Phase 2: Complete Synthesis Loop  
- [ ] feat-042: Implement kb archive --synthesized-into {guide}
- [ ] Batch archive existing synthesized investigations (dashboard, spawn, daemon)

### Phase 3: Instrument (post-cleanup)
- [ ] Consider kb stats command
- [ ] Consider read tracking for guides

## Success Criteria
- Synthesis opportunities show only actionable topics
- Investigations archived after synthesis (count stabilizes)
- orch patterns shows actionable signals, not noise
- One source of truth for synthesis suggestions
close_reason: Phase 1 complete, future phases can be separate epics

title: Add meta-topic exclusions to synthesis detection (feat-043)
description:
Quick win (~30 min): Exclude meta-topics from synthesis opportunity detection.

## Problem
Topics like 'investigation' (35 files), 'synthesis', 'artifact', 'skill' pollute synthesis suggestions. These are about the system itself, not domain topics worth synthesizing.

## Solution
Update pkg/verify/synthesis_opportunities.go to exclude meta-topics from TopicKeywords or add an exclusion list.

## Acceptance
- orch status no longer shows 'investigation' as synthesis opportunity
- kb reflect (if it uses same logic) also excludes meta-topics

## Context
Part of epic orch-go-3puvy (Knowledge System Cleanup)
Architect design: .kb/investigations/2026-01-07-design-post-synthesis-investigation-archival.md
close_reason: Excluded meta-topics (investigation, synthesis, artifact, skill) from TopicKeywords. All tests pass. SYNTHESIS.md created.

title: Fix kb reflect synthesis detection alignment
description:
Fix kb reflect to align with orch's synthesis detection (or dedupe).

## Problem
kb reflect shows 'dashboard (54 investigations)' as needing synthesis even though dashboard.md guide exists. orch status correctly excludes it. Two tools, inconsistent results.

## Options
1. Fix kb reflect to use same logic as orch (check for existing guides)
2. Have kb reflect call orch's detection (single source of truth)
3. Remove synthesis detection from kb reflect (let orch own it)

## Acceptance
- kb reflect and orch status show consistent synthesis opportunities
- No duplicate/conflicting suggestions

## Context
Part of epic orch-go-3puvy (Knowledge System Cleanup)
close_reason: Fix spawned as orch-go-k63qu - adding guide existence check to kb reflect synthesis detection

title: Prune orch patterns noise
description:
Filter out noise from orch patterns output.

## Problem
orch patterns shows 31 'critical' patterns that aren't actionable:
- 'Bash on *.' 354 times (pattern extraction bug)
- 'sleep 3' empty results (expected behavior)
- Generic globs returning empty (normal exploration)

## Solution
- Fix pattern extraction to get meaningful command summaries
- Filter out known-benign patterns (sleep, empty globs)
- Consider severity recalibration

## Acceptance
- orch patterns shows actionable signals
- Critical patterns are actually critical
- Noise reduced by >80%

## Context
Part of epic orch-go-3puvy (Knowledge System Cleanup)
close_reason: 87% noise reduction verified in SYNTHESIS: 23 critical patterns reduced to 3, all tests pass

title: Completed agents shouldn't count against concurrency limit
description: Bug: orch spawn hits concurrency limit (5) even when 3 of those agents are idle at Phase: Complete. Completed agents should be excluded from the active count since they're not consuming resources. Current behavior forces --max-agents workaround or manual cleanup of sessions that are already done.
close_reason: Fixed concurrency limit to exclude Phase: Complete agents. Added check in checkConcurrencyLimit() that calls verify.IsPhaseComplete() to skip counting completed sessions. All tests pass, committed as d0eae36.

title: Dashboard shows stale/dead agents after OpenCode restart - doesn't clear old sessions
description: After restarting OpenCode, the dashboard continues showing old dead agents as 'Active Agents' with 'Dead - needs attention' status. The CLI (orch status) shows correct data, but the dashboard at localhost:5188 shows 4 stale agents that should have been cleared. This appears to be a sync issue between the dashboard's SSE connection and the registry state after OpenCode restarts.
close_reason: Investigation found threshold already aligned (3 min). The 'stale agents' shown on dashboard are from OpenCode's session persistence - dead sessions are intentionally shown in Active section for user attention. Not a bug, working as designed.

title: Research OpenCode Zen/Black architecture and economics
description:
Investigate how OpenCode Zen/Black actually works:

Questions to answer:
1. Architecture - Just a proxy or running compute?
2. Economics - How do they make /mo flat rate work with Opus //MTok pricing?
3. Bulk deals - Do providers (Anthropic, OpenAI, Google) give resellers cheaper rates?
4. Usage limits - What caps exist (if any) on the /mo tier?
5. Sustainability - Is this viable long-term or subsidized land-grab?
6. Infrastructure - What compute/serving do they actually run?

Context:
- OpenCode Black launched Jan 6, sold out in 30s
- /mo for access to claude-opus-4-5 and ~20 other models
- No usage meters disclosed yet
- Community skeptical about economics

Sources to check:
- OpenCode docs/GitHub for Zen architecture
- Twitter posts from @opencode about Black
- Community discussions on pricing/limits
- Any technical docs on how gateway works
close_reason: Research investigation complete. OpenCode Zen = potentially viable cooperative (pending transparency), OpenCode Black = temporary drama. Recommendation: maintain status quo. Investigation: .kb/investigations/2026-01-13-research-opencode-zen-black-architecture-economics.md

title: Clean up stale beadsClient when daemon socket disappears
description:
## Problem
When beads daemon stops or crashes, the beadsClient reference in orch serve becomes stale. Subsequent RPC attempts may hang or timeout before falling back to CLI.

## Current Behavior
- Socket existence check added to short-circuit when daemon not running
- But beadsClient itself may still hold stale connection state
- Reconnect logic has exponential backoff adding latency

## Expected Behavior
- Detect when daemon socket disappears
- Clear/reset beadsClient state
- Next request should immediately fall back to CLI or attempt fresh connection

## Reference
- .kb/investigations/2026-01-07-inv-api-beads-endpoint-takes-5s.md
- pkg/beads/client.go (RPC client with auto-reconnect)
- cmd/orch/serve_beads.go (socket existence check)
close_reason: Fixed stale beadsClient with socket-aware lifecycle management (cleanup on disappear + reinit on reappear) and mutex protection. Tests: go build (success), go test ./... (2 pre-existing failures unrelated - TestIntegration_ChildID_Comments, TestResolve_Aliases). Commit: 4735e16c. Manual verification needed: start orch serve, stop/start beads daemon, verify no hangs.

Human installs Chrome extension, agent connects to their browser session. Full analysis with 6 findings in .kb/investigations/2025-12-26-inv-shared-browser-experience-orch-ecosystem.md

title: Dashboard: collapsible sections should persist state across refreshes
description:
## Problem

Collapsible sections (Active, Recent, Archive) reset to default state on page refresh. User has to re-expand/collapse every time.

## Solution

Store collapsed/expanded state in localStorage keyed by section name. Restore on page load.

## Implementation

```typescript
// On toggle
localStorage.setItem('dashboard-section-active', collapsed ? 'collapsed' : 'expanded')

// On load
const state = localStorage.getItem('dashboard-section-active') ?? 'expanded'
```
close_reason: Fixed dashboard collapsible section state not persisting across page refresh. Root cause was reactive statement firing during initialization, overwriting stored preferences. Added sectionStateLoaded flag to prevent saves until after initial load. 3 new Playwright tests confirm fix works.

title: Phase 3.5: Bidirectional Pattern Detection (Dylan patterns)
description: Extend coaching.ts to detect Dylan's behavioral patterns in addition to orchestrator patterns. Coach watches BOTH sides of conversation. MVP scope: explicit prefixes (compensation:, frame-collapse:, focus:, step-back:), priority uncertainty ("what's next?" 2+ times), compensation pattern (repeated context via keyword similarity). Defer complex patterns (deference, tool experience gap, frustration peak timing) to later iteration. title: Dashboard shows wrong investigation artifact for cross-project agents
description:
**Pattern:** Second occurrence of this bug - cross-project artifact path confusion.

close_reason: Phase: Complete - Fixed cross-project investigation tab bug by guarding auto-discovery with hasReliableProjectDir flag. Root cause: session directory unreliable for --workdir spawns.

title: Dashboard artifact viewer doesn't find cross-project investigation files
description:
When viewing a completed agent from another project (e.g., pw-4014 from price-watch while dashboard runs from orch-go), the Investigation tab fails to load with 404.

## Root Cause
Artifact API only searches current project's directories:
- .orch/workspace/{name}/SYNTHESIS.md
- .kb/investigations/

But cross-project agents have their artifacts in the source project's directories.

## Example
Agent: og-inv-kb-setup-audit-30dec [pw-4014]
Investigation at: price-watch/.kb/investigations/2025-12-30-inv-kb-setup-audit-price-watch.md
API searches: orch-go/.kb/investigations/ (wrong project)

## Possible Solutions
1. Store PROJECT_DIR in agent metadata, use it to resolve artifact paths
2. Parse beads ID prefix (pw- vs orch-go-) to infer project
3. Search all known project directories for artifacts

## Related
- Fixed workspace ID suffix bug: 32cf0792
- Cross-project agent visibility already works for status/phase
close_reason: Fixed cross-project investigation/decision file discovery

title: Epic: Amnesia-Resilient Artifact Architecture
description:
Design and implement coherent artifact architecture across orchestration ecosystem. Goal: Any fresh Claude can resume work with zero context loss.

Themes identified:
1. Workspace lifecycle (archival, retention, cleanup)
2. Knowledge flow (project → global promotion, investigation → decision)
3. Orchestrator session boundaries (handoffs, synthesis timing)
4. Cross-artifact relationships (beads ↔ kb ↔ workspace)
5. Multi-agent coordination (synthesis, conflict detection)
6. Failure mode capture (post-mortems, structured failure analysis)

Success criteria: Minimal artifact set with explicit relationships that fully captures system state.

Foundation: .kb/investigations/2025-12-21-design-deep-pattern-analysis-orchestration-artifacts.md

title: Investigation: Workspace lifecycle and archival strategy
description:
Questions to answer:
- When workspaces are cleaned, should SYNTHESIS.md be archived?
- Should there be .orch/archive/ for completed agent syntheses?
- What's the retention policy - keep all? successful only? time-based?
- How does archival interact with kb context discovery?

Context: Currently SYNTHESIS.md is deleted with workspace cleanup, losing session summaries. This may violate session amnesia principle - future Claude can't learn from past agent work patterns.

Parent: orch-go-4kwt

title: Design: Reflection checkpoint pattern for agent sessions
description:
Pattern observed: Agent's most valuable output came from interactive follow-up ('what else do you see?') not autonomous completion.

The pattern: Autonomous work → Human probe → Deeper synthesis → Richer output

Encode via:
1. Skill-level: Add reflection phase before completion
2. Spawn-level: --interactive flag for pause-before-complete
3. Protocol-level: SPAWN_CONTEXT template update
4. Artifact-level: Require 'unexplored questions' section in SYNTHESIS.md

Key insight: Agents completing autonomously is efficient but may leave value on the table. Most generative moments happen at boundary between agent work and human judgment.

Related: SESSION_HANDOFF.md pattern captures 'what next session should explore'

Parent: orch-go-4kwt

title: Active agents constantly reorder as they update, causing visual instability
description:
## Problem

Active agents in the Swarm Map fight for position as they update, causing jarring visual jumps. The gold-highlighted active cards rapidly swap positions.

## Evidence

Screenshot shows 4 active agents. As each updates (every few seconds), they jump around in the grid.

## Expected

Active agents should maintain stable positions. Either:
- Sort once on initial render, then maintain position
- Sort by a stable key (beads ID, spawn time) rather than last update time

## Files
- web/src/routes/+page.svelte - agent grid rendering and sorting logic
close_reason: Fixed active agents grid reordering by using spawned_at (stable) instead of updated_at (volatile) for secondary sort. Removed dead code (unused filteredAgents). All tests pass.

title: [orch-go] investigation: concurrent test beta
Epic: Orchestrator behavioral self-correction
description:
Enable orchestrator to detect and correct repeated behavioral mistakes without human intervention.

Root cause: Tool outcomes are ephemeral - no feedback loop exists for 'you tried X 3 times with empty result'.

Current mechanisms track knowledge state (kn, kb, orch learn) but not action outcomes. Self-correction requires: action → outcome → pattern detection → adjustment.

Implementation sequence:
1. Action logging subsystem (~/.orch/action-log.jsonl)
2. Pattern analyzer (extend learning.go)
3. Surfacing mechanism (orch patterns or SessionStart injection)

See investigation: .kb/investigations/2025-12-27-inv-orchestrator-self-correction-mechanisms-problem.md

title: skillc should include dependencies in deployed SKILL.md frontmatter
description:
## Problem

When skillc deploys skills, it doesn't include the `dependencies` field from skill.yaml in the generated SKILL.md frontmatter.

## Current Behavior

skill.yaml has dependencies, but deployed SKILL.md doesn't include them.

## Expected Behavior

Deployed SKILL.md should include dependencies from skill.yaml.

## Impact

orch-go's LoadSkillWithDependencies() reads dependencies from SKILL.md frontmatter. Without the dependencies field, dependencies aren't loaded at spawn time.

## Workaround

Manually edit deployed SKILL.md files to add dependencies field after each skillc deploy.

title: Epic: Model & System Efficiency
description:
## Understanding

Problem: We make model decisions based on vibes and external benchmarks, not evidence from our own system. We don't know our actual failure distribution, success rate by model/skill, or scaling limits. Previous approach: reactive model switching when costs/limits hit. Constraints: need telemetry before we can measure, need definitions before we can track. Risks: optimizing model choice when real problem is skill/context quality. Done: evidence-based model selection, measurable system efficiency.

---

Parent epic for understanding model selection, system efficiency, and scaling constraints. Core question: Are we optimizing the right things?
close_reason: Epic Complete: Evidence-based model selection delivered.

title: Investigate actual failure distribution across spawns
description: What causes spawn failures? Hypotheses: context too long, skill instructions ambiguous, tool schema unclear, verification gates missing. Goal: determine if model choice is the real variable or downstream of other problems. May require manual review of failed spawns to categorize.
close_reason: Investigated failure distribution: model restrictions top cause, skill errors second, 4.8M daemon spawn failures. Investigation file: .kb/investigations/2026-01-09-inv-investigate-actual-failure-distribution-across.md

title: Define 'success' for spawn telemetry
description: What does success actually mean? Options: agent completes + commits, agent completes + code works, agent completes + human verified. The Verification Bottleneck principle says we can't know success without human observation. Need to decide: track claimed success or verified success? This definition shapes the telemetry schema.
close_reason: Defined success as 'verification_passed && !forced'. Telemetry schema recommendations documented in investigation file.

title: Analyze spawn-to-value ratio
description: What's the ratio of spawns to actual lasting value? If we spawn 50 agents/week but only 10 produce lasting value, that's a system smell. Could indicate: wrong granularity, premature spawning, model mismatch. Requires telemetry (orch-go-x67lc) plus qualitative assessment.
close_reason: Completed spawn-to-value ratio investigation synthesis: D.E.K.N. summary shows 86.7% completion rate (contradicts 'spawn 50, get 10' hypothesis), but January abandonment spiked 4.8x (4.4% → 21.0%) requiring immediate investigation. Deliverables: investigation file with full synthesis, SYNTHESIS.md, kb decision kb-de30d9, git commit 6e0b28a0. Recommendation: spawn investigation for January abandonment root cause.

title: Measure context preparation vs execution cost
description: What ratio of tokens go to SPAWN_CONTEXT.md, skill embedding, kb context vs actual work? If 60% is setup, that's an optimization lever. Could inform: context compression, selective inclusion, caching strategies.
close_reason: Creating SYNTHESIS.md with findings and recommendations

title: Identify orchestrator value-add vs routing overhead
description: When does orchestrator judgment actually matter vs when is it just routing? If daemon could handle 80% of spawns correctly, orchestrator should focus on the 20%. Informs: daemon autonomy expansion, orchestrator focus areas.
close_reason: Investigation complete: orchestrator value-add is synthesis/triage judgment (20%); routing is daemon-automated (80%); overhead is workflow debt. SYNTHESIS.md created with findings and 4 follow-up issue recommendations.

title: Stress test: what breaks at 10x spawn volume
description: Current: ~5-15 agents/day. What breaks at 50? 150? Candidates: registry scaling, beads issue volume, human verification bottleneck, context staleness, OpenCode server limits. Goal: know limits before hitting them.
close_reason: Phase: Complete - Investigation complete. Found human verification is the binding constraint before technical limits. Ranked bottlenecks: (1) human review (2) registry locking (3) KB context growth (4) OpenCode stability (5) beads queries. Recommends batch review workflow with automated gates to achieve 5-10x review capacity. Deliverables: investigation file and SYNTHESIS.md committed.

title: Configure OpenAI and DeepSeek API credentials in OpenCode
description: The model aliases for GPT-5 and DeepSeek were added to orch (pkg/model/model.go) but OpenCode needs provider credentials configured to actually use them. Setup required: OpenAI API key, DeepSeek API key. Verify with test spawns using --model gpt-5 and --model deepseek-r1.
close_reason: Configured OpenAI and DeepSeek API keys in launchd plist and opencode.jsonc. Fixed model IDs to match models.dev (deepseek-chat, deepseek-reasoner, gpt-5, gpt-5-mini). Verified working with test spawns.

title: Add orch account add command for OAuth flow
description: Currently accounts must be manually added to ~/.orch/accounts.yaml. Need 'orch account add <name>' command that: (1) Opens browser for Claude OAuth, (2) Captures refresh token, (3) Saves to accounts.yaml. This would fix the workflow when refresh tokens expire - currently no way to re-auth without manual token extraction.
close_reason: Implemented orch account add command with OAuth authorization code flow using PKCE. Added pkg/account/oauth.go, pkg/account/oauth_test.go, and account add command in main.go. All 19 account tests pass.

title: Epic: Migrate skills to skillc-managed structure
description:
## Goal

Migrate ~/.claude/skills/ from manual SKILL.md files to skillc-managed .skillc/ sources, enabling modular skill development with dependency resolution and automated compilation.

## Context

- skillc exists at ~/Documents/personal/skillc with production-ready CLAUDE.md compilation
- orch-knowledge repo no longer exists (confirmed)
- Current skills have mixed structures: src/ (modular, 2 skills) vs manual SKILL.md (rest)
- skillc already supports custom output filenames (can output SKILL.md)

## Scope

**In scope:**
- Enhance skillc manifest for SKILL.md-specific patterns (frontmatter handling)
- Pilot migration of one skill to validate the pattern
- Convert existing modular skills (feature-impl, codebase-audit) to .skillc/
- Documentation of the migration pattern
- Verification that orch spawn works with compiled SKILL.md

**Out of scope:**
- Converting all monolithic skills (optional, low-value)
- Changes to orch-go pkg/skills/loader.go (read-only, no changes needed)
- Recursive skill dependencies (future enhancement)

## Success Criteria

- [ ] skillc can compile .skillc/ → SKILL.md with valid YAML frontmatter
- [ ] orch spawn works with skillc-compiled SKILL.md files
- [ ] At least 3 skills migrated (investigation, feature-impl, codebase-audit)
- [ ] Migration pattern documented for future skill authors

## Investigation Reference

See: orch-go/.kb/investigations/2025-12-22-inv-epic-replace-orch-knowledge-skillc.md
close_reason:
Epic complete. All success criteria met:
- skillc compiles .skillc/ → SKILL.md with valid frontmatter (type: skill support added)
- orch spawn works with skillc-compiled SKILL.md files (verified)
- 3 skills migrated: investigation, feature-impl, codebase-audit
- Migration pattern documented at ~/orch-knowledge/docs/skillc-skill-migration.md

Sources now in orch-knowledge, deployed to ~/.claude/skills/. Old src/ structures removed.

title: Enhance skillc for SKILL.md frontmatter handling
description:
## Context

Part of orch-go-4ztg: Epic: Migrate skills to skillc-managed structure

## Problem

skillc currently adds header comments at the start of compiled output:
```markdown
<!-- AUTO-GENERATED by skillc -->
<!-- Source: .skillc -->
...
```

SKILL.md files require YAML frontmatter at line 1:
```yaml
---
name: investigation
skill-type: procedure
...
---
```

If skillc header appears first, YAML parsing fails.

## Task

Modify skillc to handle SKILL.md compilation:
1. Add `type: skill` option in skill.yaml manifest
2. When type=skill: emit frontmatter first, header comments after
3. Support explicit frontmatter source file (e.g., frontmatter.yaml → merged into output)

## Alternative Approaches

- Option A: type field switches header placement
- Option B: Explicit frontmatter.yaml file in sources
- Option C: Detect output filename ends with SKILL.md, auto-adjust

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] skill.yaml can specify type: skill
- [ ] Compiled SKILL.md has frontmatter at line 1
- [ ] orch-go ParseSkillMetadata() parses compiled output correctly
- [ ] Existing CLAUDE.md compilation unchanged (backward compatible)

## Technical Notes

- See skillc/pkg/compiler/compiler.go:91 for header generation
- See orch-go/pkg/skills/loader.go:103-125 for ParseSkillMetadata
close_reason: Batch close from orch review

- Medium complexity (has templates/ subdirectory)
- Well-understood skill with clear structure

title: [orch-go] investigation: timing validation run 3
orch status still slow: getCompletionsForReview runs VerifyCompletionFull on 303 workspaces
description:
## Problem

`orch status` is still 1m26s despite workspace cache fix (c4d21d8d). JSON mode is fast (1s) but text output is slow.

## Root Cause

`getCompletionsForReview()` in cmd/orch/review.go:138 runs `verify.VerifyCompletionFull()` for EVERY workspace with SYNTHESIS.md:
- 303 workspaces with SYNTHESIS.md
- Each VerifyCompletionFull does beads lookups, file reads, etc.
- Called from `GetArchitectRecommendationsSurface()` which is called in text output mode

## Evidence

```
time orch status --json  # 1.0s (fast)
time orch status         # 1m26s (slow)
find .orch/workspace -name 'SYNTHESIS.md' | wc -l  # 303
```

## Fix Options

1. **Cache completions** - Don't re-verify on every status call
2. **Lazy load** - Only verify when explicitly requested 
3. **Skip old workspaces** - Only verify recent (e.g., <24h old)
4. **Move to API** - Have orch serve cache this data
close_reason: Zombie issue reconciled - no active agent found

title: Explore tradeoffs for orch-go opencode integration.
description: Explore tradeoffs for orch-go opencode integration. Current problem: orch-go uses 'opencode run --attach {server} --title {name} {prompt}' which doesn't render TUI correctly in tmux. Python orch-cli uses standalone mode: 'opencode {project_dir}' then waits for TUI ready, then types prompt via send-keys. Options to explore: 1) Match Python's standalone approach, 2) Fix attach mode to work with TUI, 3) Hybrid approach, 4) Other alternatives. Consider: session management, server coordination, TUI rendering, complexity, reliability, feature parity with Python orch-cli.

title: Regression: agent cards jostling for first position on SSE updates
description: Agent cards in dashboard are fighting for first position again when SSE updates come in. This was previously fixed with stable sort by spawned_at. Likely regression from recent SSE/CPU fix commits (ed772bac or 04defd83). Check web/src/lib/stores/ and sorting logic - may have reverted to updated_at sort or lost the stable sort key.
close_reason: No regression found. Stable sort fix from dfd61e05 is intact. Commits ed772bac/04defd83 did not touch sorting logic. Issue was false positive.

title: Update tmux spawn to use attach mode for dual TUI+API access
description:
## Problem

Currently `orch spawn --tmux` uses standalone mode (`opencode /path --model X`) which creates an ephemeral server on a random port. Sessions created in standalone mode are NOT visible to the main OpenCode server at `:4096`, so:

- No `session_id` captured in registry
- Cannot interact via HTTP API (`orch send`, `orch tail` fall back to tmux capture)
- No structured message access
- Monitoring/status checks require tmux scraping

This means tmux spawns are "human-friendly" (TUI visible) but NOT "AI-friendly" (no API access).

## Solution

Switch from standalone mode to attach mode:

```go
// OLD (standalone - ephemeral server)
opencode /path/to/project --model X

// NEW (attach - shared server)  
opencode attach http://127.0.0.1:4096 --dir /path/to/project --model X
```

With attach mode + the OpenCode fix (already applied locally):
1. TUI connects to shared server at `:4096`
2. Sessions created with correct `directory` (via `x-opencode-directory` header)
3. Sessions visible in API immediately
4. Can capture `session_id` and store in registry
5. Full API access: messages, status, send prompts

## Implementation Details

### 1. Update `pkg/tmux/tmux.go`

Change `BuildStandaloneCommand()` to `BuildAttachCommand()`:

```go
type AttachConfig struct {
    ServerURL  string // http://127.0.0.1:4096
    ProjectDir string
    Model      string
    SessionID  string // optional: continue existing session
}

func BuildAttachCommand(cfg *AttachConfig) string {
    cmd := fmt.Sprintf("opencode attach %s --dir %s", 
        cfg.ServerURL, 
        shlex.Quote(cfg.ProjectDir))
    if cfg.Model != "" {
        cmd += fmt.Sprintf(" --model %s", shlex.Quote(cfg.Model))
    }
    if cfg.SessionID != "" {
        cmd += fmt.Sprintf(" --session %s", cfg.SessionID)
    }
    return cmd
}
```

### 2. Update `cmd/orch/main.go` `runSpawnTmux()`

After TUI is ready and prompt is sent:
1. Query `/session` API with `x-opencode-directory` header
2. Find most recent session for this project
3. Store `session_id` in registry

```go
// After SendPromptAfterReady succeeds
sessionID, err := findRecentSession(serverURL, cfg.ProjectDir)
if err != nil {
    // Log warning but don't fail - tmux fallback still works
    log.Printf("Warning: could not capture session_id: %v", err)
}

// Update registry with session_id
agent.SessionID = sessionID
```

### 3. Update registry usage

Commands that currently fall back to tmux when `session_id` is missing can now use API:
- `orch tail` - use `/session/{id}/message` 
- `orch send` - use `/session/{id}/prompt`
- `orch question` - parse from API messages

## Dependencies

**OpenCode fix required:** The SDK must pass `directory: process.cwd()` to API calls.

Fix already applied locally at:
`opencode/packages/opencode/src/cli/cmd/tui/context/sdk.tsx`

```typescript
const sdk = createOpencodeClient({
  baseUrl: props.url,
  signal: abort.signal,
  directory: process.cwd(),  // THIS LINE ADDED
})
```

This fix needs to be:
1. Committed to opencode repo
2. Released as new version
3. Or: use locally built opencode binary

## Testing

1. `orch spawn --tmux investigation "test"` creates session
2. Session visible via: `curl http://127.0.0.1:4096/session -H "x-opencode-directory: $PWD"`
3. `session_id` present in registry: `cat ~/.orch/agent-registry.json | jq '.agents[-1].session_id'`
4. `orch tail <id>` uses API (not tmux fallback)
5. `orch send <id> "message"` uses API

## Evidence

Tested attach mode with fixed OpenCode locally:

```bash
# Session created with correct directory
curl -s http://127.0.0.1:4096/session \
  -H "x-opencode-directory: /Users/dylanconlin/Documents/personal/orch-go" \
  | jq 'sort_by(.time.created) | last'

# Output:
{
  "id": "ses_4bf7f116bffeaVAetTEdKTtsD4",
  "title": "New session - 2025-12-21T10:42:30.164Z",
  "dir": "/Users/dylanconlin/Documents/personal/orch-go",
  ...
}

# Can fetch messages via API
curl -s "http://127.0.0.1:4096/session/ses_4bf7f116bffeaVAetTEdKTtsD4/message" \
  -H "x-opencode-directory: /Users/dylanconlin/Documents/personal/orch-go"
# Returns structured message history
```

## Related

- Investigation: `.kb/investigations/2025-12-21-inv-tmux-spawn-killed.md`
- Python orch-cli gave up on attach mode due to this bug: `src/orch/spawn.py:959-960`
- OpenCode source: `packages/opencode/src/cli/cmd/tui/context/sdk.tsx`

title: orch next: Synthesize prioritized work recommendations
description:
Combine multiple signals into ranked 'what to work on next' recommendations.

Current state: Orchestrator must manually run and synthesize:
- bd ready (available work)
- orch patterns (problem areas)
- orch focus (strategic alignment)
- orch learn (knowledge gaps)

Desired state: `orch next` synthesizes these into prioritized recommendations:
1. Critical blockers (patterns showing active failures)
2. Focus-aligned ready work
3. High-value maintenance (recurring patterns worth fixing)
4. Strategic backlog

Example output:
```
orch next

RECOMMENDED NEXT ACTIONS (aligned with focus: 'Ship snap MVP')

1. [BLOCKER] orch-go-xyz has failed 3x in last 24h - spawn systematic-debugging
2. [FOCUS] orch-go-abc ready - implements snap auth (P1)
3. [FOCUS] orch-go-def ready - snap API endpoint (P2)
4. [MAINTENANCE] 'chrome launch' gap occurred 5x - consider kn constrain
5. [BACKLOG] orch-go-ghi ready - consolidation phase 1 (P2, not focus-aligned)
```

Key behaviors:
- Filter patterns to active/open issues only
- Weight by focus alignment
- Surface blockers before new work
- Show reasoning for prioritization

title: orch complete --force bypasses repro verification for bugs
description:
--force flag skips ALL verification including repro check for bugs. This defeats the Bug Reproducibility Gates epic (jjd4).

## Root cause
--force sets a boolean that skips the entire verification flow, not granular checks.

## Solution
--force should only skip the blocking check (test evidence), not subsequent gates (repro verification). Or rename to --skip-test-evidence and keep repro gate mandatory.
close_reason: Fixed --force to only skip phase/liveness verification, not repro verification. Repro gate now always runs for bugs. Updated help text and error messages. Tests passing.

title: Daemon capacity count goes stale after initial spawn cycle
description: Daemon correctly counts on startup but goes stale after spawning. Requires launchctl restart to pick up new work. Prior fix (orch-go-s2j7) filtered sessions by 30-min update time, but issue persists. Suspect: Reconcile() not being called on each poll, or untracked agents counting toward capacity incorrectly.
close_reason: Fixed daemon capacity count to exclude untracked agents. Added extractBeadsIDFromSessionTitle() and isUntrackedBeadsID() functions to filter out sessions with -untracked- in beads ID. All tests pass.

title: Fix: tmux spawn should not use --format json
description:
## Problem

When spawning in tmux mode, the agent runs with `--format json` which outputs raw JSON instead of OpenCode's interactive TUI.

## Current Behavior
```
opencode run --attach ... --format json --title ... "prompt"
# Outputs raw JSON lines to terminal
```

## Expected Behavior
```
opencode run --attach ... --title ... "prompt"
# Shows OpenCode's normal TUI
```

## Root Cause
`pkg/tmux/tmux.go:BuildSpawnCommand()` hardcodes `--format json`.

## Fix
- Remove `--format json` from tmux spawn command
- Keep `--format json` only for inline mode (needed to parse session ID)

## Files
- pkg/tmux/tmux.go line ~10

title: Daemon should auto-complete agents that report Phase: Complete
description:
## Problem

Agents that report 'Phase: Complete' sit idle and block capacity slots until manually completed via `orch review done`.

Just observed:
- 2 agents idle for 10+ minutes at 'Phase: Complete'
- Dashboard showed 3/3 slots full, but only 1 was actually running
- P1 issue couldn't spawn because slots were blocked by completed agents
- Had to manually run `orch review done orch-go` to free capacity

## Expected Behavior

Daemon should:
1. Detect agents at 'Phase: Complete'
2. Run completion verification (same as `orch review`)
3. Auto-complete if verification passes
4. Free the slot for new work

## Why P1

- Blocks new work from spawning
- Defeats purpose of autonomous daemon
- Requires human intervention to keep system flowing
- Directly caused friction today (P1 session cleanup issue blocked)

## Evidence

```
orch status
# Active: 3 (running: 1, idle: 2)
# orch-go-8a03c idle Phase: Complete 10m AT-RISK
# orch-go-91qze idle Phase: Complete 10m AT-RISK

orch review
# 2 completions ready, both rec=close

orch review done orch-go
# Completed 2/2, freed slots
```

## Implementation

Daemon polling loop should:
1. Check for idle agents with 'Phase: Complete'
2. Run `orch complete` verification
3. If passes, complete and close beads issue
4. If fails, mark for manual review

title: Move orch learn to kb learn as part of Phase 1 consolidation
description: orch learn conceptually belongs in kb - it's about knowledge gaps, suggests kn/kb artifacts, and aligns with kb reflect. Current placement is artifact of building it inside spawn workflow. When Phase 1 (kb absorbs kn) happens, migrate: 1) Move pkg/spawn/learning.go to kb-cli, 2) orch spawn emits gap events, kb consumes them, 3) Rename to 'kb learn'. Gap tracker file stays at ~/.orch/gap-tracker.json or moves to ~/.kb/.
close_reason: orch learn successfully migrated to kb learn. All code committed (orch-go: 17638c33, kb-cli: 90fce23). Builds and tests pass. kb binary installed with learn command. orch learn delegates correctly when kb is in PATH.

title: Add semantic pattern matching to gap normalizeQuery
description: The normalizeQuery function at pkg/spawn/learning.go:365-371 only does lowercase + whitespace normalization. Semantically related queries like 'synthesize orchestrator investigations' and 'synthesize spawn investigations' are treated as separate patterns, preventing recurrence detection. Implement template-based pattern matching to group related queries. See .kb/investigations/2026-01-07-inv-audit-recurring-gap-patterns-semantic.md for detailed findings.
close_reason: Implemented semantic pattern matching in normalizeQuery with 12 patterns using 1-3 word wildcards. Related queries like 'synthesize X investigations' now group as single pattern. All tests pass. Leave it Better: kn-2b13d0 (decision on template-based approach).

title: Glass: Add hover capability and investigate other important interaction modes
description: Glass currently supports click, type, navigate, screenshot. Missing hover which is important for: tooltips, dropdown menus, hover states. Should investigate what other interaction modes are commonly needed (drag-drop, right-click, keyboard shortcuts, scroll-into-view, wait-for-element, etc.)
