title: Dashboard: fallback to beads close reason when synthesis is null
description:
## Problem

Light-tier feature-impl agents don't create SYNTHESIS.md (by design), so dashboard shows no TLDR for completed agents.

## Solution

When synthesis is null, fall back to:
1. Beads issue close reason (from bd show --json)
2. Task description from spawn context
3. Empty state with 'No summary available'

## Implementation

The /api/agents endpoint already has beads data. Need to:
1. Include close_reason in agent response when synthesis is null
2. Update agent-card.svelte to display close_reason as fallback

## Files
- cmd/orch/serve.go - add close_reason to agent response
- web/src/lib/components/agent-card/agent-card.svelte - fallback display logic
close_reason: Added close_reason fallback for light-tier agents in dashboard. When synthesis is null, shows beads close_reason. Changes: Issue struct, API, detail panel, collapsible section. Commit c89a55f.

title: Infrastructure: Daemon-managed development servers per project
description:
Development servers are a recurring friction point across the orch ecosystem. Orchestrators start servers in-session, sessions die, servers die, next session is confused.

**Problems observed:**
- price-watch: Docker services (make up) not running, orchestrator keeps asking Dylan to restart
- orch-go: vite dev server on wrong port (5188 vs 5173), duplicate processes
- Servers started in background during session are lost when session ends
- No visibility into which servers are running for which projects

**Proposed solution:**

1. **Project server manifest** - Each project declares its servers in .orch/servers.yaml:
```yaml
servers:
  - name: docker
    command: make up
    health: docker compose ps --format json
    ports: [3000, 5432, 6379]
  - name: frontend
    command: npm run dev
    workdir: frontend
    ports: [5173]
```

2. **orch serve manages project servers** - Not just the dashboard, but also project dev servers
   - Starts declared servers when entering project
   - Health checks and auto-restarts
   - Surfaces status in dashboard (/api/servers endpoint already exists)

3. **SessionStart hook** - Verifies servers are healthy before orchestration begins

**Success criteria:**
- Orchestrator never asks Dylan to start servers
- Servers survive session death
- Dashboard shows server health per project
- `orch servers status` shows all managed servers

**Related:**
- orch-go-tiav: glass daemon-based shared browser control (similar pattern)
- Price-watch CLAUDE.md now has 'Session Start' section (manual workaround)
close_reason: Designed on-demand health checks with servers.yaml schema and SessionStart integration. 3 approaches analyzed, 5 implementation features added to backlog (feat-024 through feat-028).

title: Test OpenCode plugin event reliability and timing
description:
Investigation 2026-01-08-inv-epic-mechanize-principles-via-opencode.md identified untested assumptions about plugin behavior.

**Items to test:**

1. **`file.edited` event reliability**
   - Does it fire reliably for all Edit tool calls?
   - What data is in the event payload (file path, content)?
   - Any edge cases (large files, binary files)?

2. **`session.idle` timing**
   - How long until session.idle fires?
   - Is it configurable?
   - Does it fire on every idle or just first?

3. **Plugin interaction**
   - What happens when multiple plugins hook the same event?
   - Order of execution?
   - Can one plugin block another?

**Deliverable:** Investigation documenting actual behavior, update plugins if assumptions were wrong.
close_reason: Investigation-only work - no code changes requiring tests. Created .kb/investigations/ artifact.

title: Worker skills should depend on worker-base instead of duplicating patterns
description:
**Problem:** worker-base skill exists with common patterns (authority delegation, beads tracking, phase reporting, exit protocol) but only investigation uses it. Other worker skills (systematic-debugging, feature-impl, etc.) have these patterns duplicated inline.

**Evidence:**
- worker-base at ~/orch-knowledge/skills/src/shared/worker-base/
- investigation/.skillc/skill.yaml has 'dependencies: - worker-base'
- systematic-debugging and feature-impl have NO worker-base dependency
- grep shows bd comment / Phase: patterns duplicated in each skill

**Maintenance burden:** Updates to common patterns (like 'how to report phase') must be made in multiple skills instead of one place.

**Fix:** Add worker-base as dependency to:
- systematic-debugging
- feature-impl  
- architect
- research
- design-session
- Any other worker skills

Then remove duplicated sections from each skill that worker-base already provides.

**Note:** This is refactoring, not behavior change. Skills should work the same after consolidation.
close_reason: Already completed: Investigation revealed all 10 worker skills already had worker-base dependency. Added explanatory co

title: Customize OpenCode fork to prevent Task tool confusion in orchestrator context
description:
## Problem Observed

Orchestrator agent in OpenCode repeatedly tried to use the Task tool to spawn workers instead of `orch spawn`. Required multiple user corrections before the agent used the correct method.

**Sequence of failures:**
1. User: "spawn 5 with an escape hatch opus"
2. Agent: Uses Task tool with `investigation` subagent type → User aborts
3. Agent: Uses Task tool with `explore` subagent type → User aborts  
4. User: "are you not aware of orch spawn?"
5. Agent: Finally uses `orch spawn` but with wrong syntax
6. Agent: Corrects syntax, succeeds

**Root cause:** The Task tool is prominently documented in OpenCode's system prompt. The `orch spawn` guidance is in the orchestrator skill but gets overshadowed.

## Skill Fix Already Applied

Added "Spawn method gate" to Pre-Response Gates in orchestrator skill:
- Commit: `2f7324e` in orch-knowledge
- Now checks "Am I about to use Task tool to delegate?" before every response

## Proposed OpenCode Customizations

Since we maintain an OpenCode fork (`~/Documents/personal/opencode`), we can customize the system prompt or add plugins:

### Option A: Conditional System Prompt (Recommended)

When orchestrator skill is loaded, modify the Task tool description:

```
**Note for orchestrators:** In projects using orch-go, use `orch spawn SKILL "task"` 
instead of the Task tool. The Task tool lacks beads tracking, workspaces, and 
completion workflow that orch spawn provides.
```

Detection: Check if `SPAWN_CONTEXT.md` is absent AND orchestrator skill is in context.

### Option B: Plugin that Intercepts Task Tool

Create plugin at `~/.config/opencode/plugin/orchestrator-spawn-guard.js`:
- Hook: `tool.pre` for Task tool
- Check: Is this an orchestrator context (no SPAWN_CONTEXT.md)?
- Action: Warn or suggest `orch spawn` instead

### Option C: Remove/Hide Task Tool for Orchestrators

More aggressive - conditionally remove Task tool from available tools when in orchestrator mode.

**Risk:** May break legitimate Task tool uses (e.g., slash commands).

## Context Detection

Orchestrator context indicators:
- No `SPAWN_CONTEXT.md` in workspace
- Working directory is project root
- Orchestrator skill loaded (check skill name in context)

Worker context indicators:
- `SPAWN_CONTEXT.md` exists
- Has `TASK:` in initial prompt
- Spawned via `orch spawn`

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Orchestrator agents default to `orch spawn` without user correction
- [ ] Task tool remains available for legitimate uses (slash commands, etc.)
- [ ] Fix works in both OpenCode TUI and Claude Code (if applicable)

## References

- OpenCode fork: `~/Documents/personal/opencode`
- Orchestrator skill: `~/orch-knowledge/skills/src/meta/orchestrator/`
- Skill fix commit: `2f7324e` (orch-knowledge)
- Original issue transcript: User conversation 2026-01-16
close_reason: Created task-tool-gate plugin that blocks Task tool for orchestrators, forcing use of orch spawn. Implemented using tool.execute.before hook + inverse worker detection pattern. Commit: 709fa74bc

title: orch handoff generates stale/incorrect data
description:
Discovered this session - orch handoff output doesn't reflect current state.

**Problems observed:**
1. 'Agents Still Running' lists completed agents (bd-ef1a, orch-go-26lo, etc.)
2. 'Work Completed' section shows garbled data ('ready] - Implement Synthesis...')
3. Doesn't reflect what actually happened this session

**Root cause hypotheses:**
- Reading from stale registry/tmux data
- Not filtering completed agents
- 'Work Completed' parsing is broken

- Work Completed should pull from closed beads issues this session
- Session summary should be accurate

**Workaround:** Manual handoff writing (what we did this session)

title: orch complete doesn't set OpenCode session status to 'done'
description:
After orch complete successfully verifies and closes an agent, the OpenCode session status remains 'null' instead of being set to 'done'. This causes completed agents to appear as 'running' in orch status output.

Root cause: cmd/orch/complete_cmd.go doesn't call OpenCode API to update session status after completion.

Impact:
- Completed agents appear in 'orch status' as running
- Ghost agents accumulate in the active list
- Token counters remain visible for completed work
- Confusing user experience (like pw-oicj appearing with 1.1M tokens after completion)

Expected behavior:
- After orch complete succeeds, call OpenCode API to set session status to 'done'
- orch status should then filter out completed sessions

Investigation: .kb/investigations/2026-01-09-inv-pw-oicj-ghost-agent-postmortem.md
close_reason: Fixed bug by adding client.DeleteSession() after beads closure. Tests: go test ./cmd/orch -run TestComplete - PASS. Root cause: complete_cmd.go didn't delete OpenCode sessions. Solution: Added session deletion at line 565-580, reads from workspace .session_id file. Commit: 5d26e1f5

title: Dashboard: redesign agent detail pane with tabbed interface
description:
## Problem

Current agent detail slide-out is too narrow, shows low-value info (quick copy for workspace/session), and doesn't provide access to the full agent lifecycle (input → messages → output).

## Design

### Layout
- **Width:** 80-85% of viewport
- **Tabs:** Input | Messages | Investigation | Synthesis | Files | Meta

### Tab Behavior by Agent State

**Active (processing):**
- Default to **Messages** tab
- Live message feed filling vertical space
- New messages appear at bottom (like Claude Code)
- Filter out tool call noise - show clear signal only
- Input/Output tabs available but not default

**Completed:**
- Default to **Investigation** tab (for investigation skill) or **Synthesis** tab
- Shows rendered markdown from workspace artifacts
- Messages tab shows full history (filtered)

### Tab Contents

| Tab | Content |
|-----|---------|
| **Input** | SPAWN_CONTEXT.md rendered |
| **Messages** | Filtered conversation (no tool call spam) |
| **Investigation** | .kb/investigations/*.md if created |
| **Synthesis** | SYNTHESIS.md or SESSION_HANDOFF.md |
| **Files** | Summary only: '5 files, +146/-3' |
| **Meta** | Beads ID [copy], tier, runtime, tokens |

### What to Remove
- Quick copy buttons for workspace/session (move to Meta tab)
- Quick Commands section at bottom
- Raw tool calls in message feed

### Data Sources
- Workspace artifacts: .orch/workspace/{name}/*.md
- Messages: GET /session/{id}/messages (filter tool calls)
- Files: session.summary.additions/deletions
- Metadata: .tier, .beads_id, .spawn_time, .session_id

## Evidence

Audit of 175 workspaces shows:
- 153 SPAWN_CONTEXT.md (input)
- 54 SYNTHESIS.md (output)
- 16 ORCHESTRATOR_CONTEXT.md
- 15 SESSION_HANDOFF.md
- 4 FAILURE_REPORT.md

Messages API exists: GET /session/{id}/messages
close_reason: Created design spec (.kb/investigations/2026-01-06-inv-orch-go-hmj61-dashboard-agent.md) and Epic orch-go-akhff with 5 implementation tasks for tabbed agent detail pane interface.

title: Add capability skill type to skill system - start with document-handling
description:
New skill type: 'capability' - cross-cutting domain knowledge that composes with workflow skills.

**Background:**
- Workflow skills (investigation, feature-impl) = how to do a type of work
- Capability skills = how to use a domain effectively (tools, limits, patterns)
- Composition: spawn = workflow skill + 0-N capability skills

**First capability: document-handling**
Based on investigations:
- .kb/investigations/2025-12-30-inv-research-anthropic-official-document-skills.md (API capabilities)
- .kb/investigations/2025-12-30-inv-pre-process-documents-locally-vs-api.md (local-first patterns)

Content:
- When to use local tools vs API
- Available tools (poppler: pdftotext, pdfseparate, pdfinfo)
- Decision tree (triage-first pattern)
- Limits (100-page, 32MB, token costs)
- NOT phases/checkpoints (that's workflow skill territory)

**Implementation:**
1. Add skill-type: capability to skill metadata schema
2. Create document-handling skill in orch-knowledge/skills/src/capability/
3. Update orch spawn to auto-detect (task mentions PDF/document → inject)
4. Update SPAWN_CONTEXT template with capabilities section
5. Add --capability flag for explicit loading

**Constraints:**
- Keep capabilities lean (~500 lines max)
- Auto-detect with explicit override
- Prove pattern with one skill before expanding
close_reason: Stale 15 days - capability skill type can be revisited when needed

title: Add model→mode auto-selection to orch spawn
description:
## Problem

Orchestrators don't know which model/mode combinations work. Invalid combinations create zombie agents or hit rate limits silently.

## Current Reality (Jan 2026)

Only two viable spawn paths:
- `claude + opus` - Max subscription via claude CLI
- `opencode + sonnet` - Pay-per-token API

Invalid combinations:
- `opencode + opus` → zombie agent (auth blocked)
- `opencode + flash` → TPM rate limit exceeded
- `claude + flash` → claude CLI doesn't support Gemini

## Solution

1. **Auto-select mode based on model:**
   - `--model opus` → automatically use `--mode claude`
   - `--model sonnet` → automatically use `--mode opencode`
   - `--model flash` → error with explanation

2. **Update orchestrator skill** with clear guidance:
   - Document the two viable paths
   - Remove stale model selection guidance that implies flash works

3. **Add validation in orch spawn:**
   - Warn/error on invalid combinations before creating zombie agents

## Evidence

- kb-a906ec: Constraint captured
- kb-5896e3: Open question about auto-selection
- Session friction: orchestrator didn't know how to spawn via claude code

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `orch spawn --model opus` auto-selects claude mode
- [ ] `orch spawn --model sonnet` auto-selects opencode mode  
- [ ] `orch spawn --model flash` errors with explanation
- [ ] Orchestrator skill updated with two-path guidance
- [ ] Invalid mode+model combo warns before spawning
close_reason: Phase: Complete - Added model→mode auto-selection (opus→claude, sonnet→opencode, flash→error), validation for invalid combos, orchestrator skill updated, all tests passing

title: [orch-go] investigation: test fire-and-forget timing
ORCHESTRATOR_CONTEXT.md contradicts decision on session end
description:
ORCHESTRATOR_CONTEXT.md tells spawned orchestrators to run 'orch session end' when complete. But decision 2026-01-04-orchestrator-session-lifecycle.md says:

'Orchestrators and meta-orchestrators don't try to end their own sessions (no orch session end). They write SESSION_HANDOFF.md when goal reached, then wait. Session end happens when the level above runs orch complete.'

The template and skill need to be updated to match the decision:
1. Remove 'orch session end' instruction from completion protocol
2. Clarify that spawned orchestrators write SESSION_HANDOFF.md and WAIT
3. Remove references to --skip-reflection --reason flags (don't exist)

- cmd/orch/spawn.go (ORCHESTRATOR_CONTEXT.md template)
- ~/.claude/skills/meta/orchestrator/SKILL.md (skill references)

title: Dashboard needs Recent/Completed agents section
description:
Dashboard shows Active Agents and Ready Queue, but no visibility into recently completed work.

After completing multiple agents (bn50.2, bn50.3, 6vmr, 0xc1, etc.), there's nowhere to see:
- What was just completed
- The synthesis/outcome of recent work

Should add a 'Recent' or 'Completed' section showing:
- Agents completed in last 24h (configurable)
- Their outcome (success/failed)
- Quick link to SYNTHESIS.md / deliverables

This completes the agent lifecycle visibility: Ready Queue → Active → Recent/Completed

title: Consider auto-starting beads daemon with orch serve
description:
## Question
Should beads daemon auto-start when `orch serve` starts?

## Context
From investigation 2026-01-07-inv-api-beads-endpoint-takes-5s.md:
- First API request takes 6.5s due to RPC client reconnect overhead when daemon not running
- Subsequent cached requests are ~15ms
- Cold start penalty is significant for dashboard UX

## Considerations
- Pro: Eliminates cold start penalty
- Pro: Dashboard always has fast beads access
- Con: Another process to manage/monitor
- Con: May not be needed if user runs bd daemon separately

## Options
1. Auto-start beads daemon in orch serve
2. Document that bd daemon should be running for best performance
3. Add health check that warns if daemon not running

## Reference
- .kb/investigations/2026-01-07-inv-api-beads-endpoint-takes-5s.md
close_reason: Investigation complete: No auto-start needed - caching already solves cold-start, per-project daemon architecture makes centralized management inappropriate

title: Experiment: two-model architecture session with shared principles
description:
**Hypothesis:** Two models (Claude + Gemini), grounded in same principles, designing together produces better architecture than single-model.

**Setup:**
- Pick a real design question
- Give both models ~/.kb/principles.md
- Relay between them for 3-4 rounds
- Capture what emerges

**Prior evidence:** Dylan saw promising results with Claude+Gemini 'auto-architecting' together (pre-principles era). Worth testing if shared principles + model diversity = better outcomes.

**Success criteria:** Did cross-pollination surface something neither would alone? Was it worth the overhead?

**Note:** Experiment first. If valuable, then consider systematizing.
close_reason: Experiment complete. Two-model (Claude+Gemini) architecture session on kb ask design produced better outcome than single-model would have. Gemini caught principle violation (no-context case) and made sharper argument against --force (tool conflation, not just principle violation). Friction: had to ask Gemini to write to files, no easy way to see conversational output. Verdict: worth doing for design questions with real tradeoffs. Systematization would be separate work.

title: Investigate orch status observability gaps and escape hatch tracking
Epic: System Self-Evaluation - Teaching the system to prioritize, not just surface
description:
The system has perception (orch patterns, orch learn, orch next, kb reflect) but not judgment. It surfaces 11 patterns, 9 gaps, 6 issues - all presented as equally worthy of attention. The orchestrator must evaluate manually.

**The Question:** What grounds 'this matters'? The chain must terminate in something verifiable, not feeling.

**Hypotheses to test:**
- Blocking count (does this gate other work?)
- Cost (time/tokens wasted per attempt)
- Trend (accelerating vs stable?)
- Resolution (has this been addressed?)

**Two-layer model:**
- Layer 1: Objective filtering (remove resolved, demote stale, mark blocked)
- Layer 2: Contextual weighting (blocking, cost, trend, alignment)

**Phases:**
1. Instrument what's missing (resolution tracking, cost tracking, blocking graph)
2. Collect data (run system, track what gets acted on vs ignored)
3. Test hypotheses (which signals predict action?)
4. Implement evaluation layer (build what works)

**Success:** The system curates so you can prioritize. Less noise, richer signal.
close_reason: Replaced with single investigation per design review - epic structure too speculative

title: Phase 1: Instrument missing signals
description:
Add instrumentation to collect signals that might predict importance:

**Resolution tracking:**
- Connect gaps to commits/issue closures
- When a gap is addressed, mark it resolved
- Stop surfacing resolved gaps

**Cost tracking:**
- Log duration per spawn (start → complete/abandon)
- Log tokens consumed per spawn
- Associate cost with issue/gap

**Blocking graph:**
- Surface implicit blocking (not just beads dependencies)
- Count downstream blockers per issue/gap

**Deliverables:**
- Extended event logging in agentlog or orch events
- Resolution detection logic
- Cost aggregation queries
- Blocking count in orch patterns output
close_reason: Parent epic cancelled - research approach changed

title: Phase 2: Collect evaluation data
description:
Run the instrumented system and collect data on what gets acted on.

**Track:**
- Which patterns/gaps/issues surfaced
- Which ones you acted on (spawned, closed, addressed)
- Which ones you ignored
- Time between surfacing and action

**Duration:** 1-2 weeks of normal usage

**Deliverable:** Dataset of (surfaced, acted_on, time_to_action, outcome)
close_reason: Parent epic cancelled - research approach changed

title: Phase 3: Test prioritization hypotheses
description:
Analyze collected data to test which signals predict action.

**Hypotheses:**
1. Blocking count predicts action (high blockers → acted on sooner)
2. Cost predicts action (expensive failures → acted on sooner)
3. Trend predicts action (accelerating gaps → acted on sooner)
4. Resolution filtering reduces noise (resolved items were correctly ignored)

**Analysis:**
- Correlation between each signal and time_to_action
- False positive rate (surfaced but never acted on)
- False negative rate (acted on but wasn't prominently surfaced)

**Deliverable:** Investigation with findings on which signals work
close_reason: Parent epic cancelled - research approach changed

title: Phase 4: Implement evaluation layer
description:
Build the evaluation layer based on Phase 3 findings.

**Layer 1: Objective Filtering**
- Filter resolved gaps/patterns
- Demote stale items (no activity in N days, not blocking)
- Mark externally-blocked items as 'waiting'

**Layer 2: Contextual Weighting**
- Weight by signals that Phase 3 validated
- Expose weights in orch next output
- Allow override/tuning

**Deliverable:** Enhanced orch next that curates, not just surfaces
close_reason: Parent epic cancelled - research approach changed

title: Headless spawn registers wrong project directory
description:
When spawning with workdir parameter, OpenCode session registers the original cwd instead of the target directory.

**Repro:**
1. From orch-go directory, run: `orch spawn feature-impl 'task' --workdir /path/to/other-project`
2. Check session: `curl http://127.0.0.1:4096/session/<id>`
3. Session shows `directory: /path/to/orch-go` instead of `/path/to/other-project`

**Impact:**
- Dashboard filters by project - spawns don't appear in correct project view
- `orch status` may not show agent in expected project context

**Root cause (suspected):**
OpenCode CreateSession API uses the cwd of the orch process, not the directory where SPAWN_CONTEXT.md was created.

**Evidence:**
Session ses_4b62a9e92ffeRGwLY9Oo0OsdVV spawned for orch-knowledge but registered with directory: /Users/dylanconlin/Documents/personal/orch-go
close_reason: Batch close from orch review

title: test-no-daemon-close
close_reason: Testing close

title: Require test execution evidence for feature-impl completion
description:
Currently verification checks ceremony (files exist, Phase: Complete reported) not behavior (tests actually pass). The 4026cb69 case shows an agent claiming 'tests pass' with no actual test output - reverted in 18 minutes.

Fix: Before accepting Phase: Complete for feature-impl:
1. Parse beads comments for test execution evidence (command output, pass/fail counts)
2. Pattern match for actual test output: 'go test ./... - PASS', 'npm test', pytest output, etc.
3. Block completion (EscalationBlock) if code changes exist but no test evidence found

Example valid evidence:
```
bd comment <id> 'Tests: go test ./pkg/state/... - PASS (12 tests in 0.8s)'
```

Reference: .kb/investigations/2025-12-28-inv-verification-system-audit-verification-theater.md
close_reason: Added test execution evidence verification that blocks feature-impl completion when code modified without documented test output. Includes 22 test output patterns (Go/npm/pytest/cargo/playwright), false positive detection, and skill-based gating. 47 test cases pass.

title: Research current open source LLM landscape (LocalLLaMA, HN)
description:
## Problem

Need to stay current on open source LLM developments to inform model arbitrage strategy (orch-go-6x36). The space moves fast - new models, inference optimizations, and hosting options emerge weekly.

## Solution

Research investigation covering:

### Sources
- r/LocalLLaMA - practitioner experiences, model comparisons, quantization tips
- Hacker News - new releases, benchmarks, infrastructure discussions

### Questions to Answer
1. What models are practitioners actually using for coding/agents? (not just benchmarks)
2. What's the current state of local inference? (vLLM, llama.cpp, Ollama performance)
3. Any new providers challenging Groq/Together/Fireworks?
4. Quantization sweet spots (Q4 vs Q8 vs FP16 for agent tasks)
5. What's shipping in Q1 2025? (Llama 4 full release, DeepSeek V4, etc.)

### Deliverable
Investigation file with:
- Current model rankings (practitioner consensus, not benchmarks)
- Provider comparison (cost/speed/reliability)
- Recommendations for orch-go integration priorities

## Evidence

- Parent issue: orch-go-6x36 (multi-provider routing)
- Context: overnight swarm runs successful, preparing for scale

title: Consolidate ~/.kb and ~/orch-knowledge/kb into single repo
description:
Currently have two separate knowledge bases:
- ~/.kb (git repo: dylan-conlin/kb) - system-wide knowledge, 49KB principles.md
- ~/orch-knowledge/.kb - older knowledge, 7.1KB principles.md

Goal: Merge into single repo at ~/orch-knowledge/kb with symlink ~/.kb → ~/orch-knowledge/kb

Steps:
1. Backup both repos
2. Compare and reconcile differences (principles.md differs)
3. Merge ~/.kb git history into ~/orch-knowledge
4. Create symlink ~/.kb → ~/orch-knowledge/kb
5. Verify all tools still work (kb, orch, etc.)
6. Update any hardcoded paths
7. Archive old dylan-conlin/kb repo

title: Dashboard progressive disclosure - Active/Recent/Archive sections
description:

Implement collapsible sections for session display:
- **Active** (expanded by default): Sessions with recent activity (<10 min)
- **Recent** (collapsed): Idle sessions from last 24h  
- **Archive** (collapsed): Older sessions

**Why:** Problem is presentation, not persistence. Sessions are valuable for debugging. Current 6h threshold is arbitrary and hides valuable completions.

**Also consider:**
- Add session count badges to collapsed headers
- Add `--delete-sessions` flag to `orch clean` for explicit cleanup

title: Add orch doctor command to check and restart services
description:
Diagnostic and recovery command to check health of all orch infrastructure services.

Use case: When things aren't working, user runs 'orch doctor' to diagnose and fix.

Checks:
1. OpenCode serve - is it running? responding? auth valid?
2. orch serve - is it running? correct port?
3. orch daemon - is it running? launchd status?
4. beads - is database accessible?

Actions:
- orch doctor (check all, report status)
- orch doctor --fix (attempt to restart failed services)
- orch doctor opencode (check specific service)

Output example:
  ✓ OpenCode serve    running (port 4096, 3 sessions)
  ✗ orch serve        not running
    → Run: launchctl kickstart gui/$(id -u)/com.orch-go.serve
  ✓ orch daemon       running (last poll 30s ago)
  ✓ beads             accessible (567 issues)

Depends on: orch-go-0x12 (OpenCode launchd plist)
close_reason: Implemented orch doctor command with --fix and --verbose flags. Checks OpenCode (4096), orch serve (3348), and beads daemon. All tests pass.

title: Rate limit account switch kills in-flight agents with no recovery
description:
**Failure mode discovered 2026-01-06:**

Root cause: Agents are tied to the account that spawned them. When that account hits limits, those agents can't continue. No mechanism to detect, migrate, pause, or resume.

Impact: Complete loss of work context - had to restart meta-orchestrator from scratch.

Potential solutions:
1. Proactive rate limit monitoring (warn at 80%, pause spawning at 90%)
2. Agent session persistence that survives account switches  
3. Graceful degradation - queue new work instead of failing
4. orch clean --preserve-orchestrator flag to protect meta sessions
close_reason: Added --preserve-orchestrator flag to orch clean. Protects orchestrator/meta-orchestrator workspaces and sessions from cleanup during rate limit events. Tests: go test ./cmd/orch/... -run Test.*Orchestrator - 12/12 passed, full suite passes.

title: Session start should auto-name tmux window as {project}-{count}
description:
Currently orchestrator session names are inherited from ad-hoc tmux window names ("session", "test-session", "zsh"). This creates no discoverability or pattern.

Desired behavior:
- orch session start "goal" should auto-generate name: {project}-{count}
- Count is daily (resets each day, based on today's session directories)
- Automatically renames tmux window to match
- Example: orch-go-1, orch-go-2, kb-cli-1

Changes needed:
1. Get project name from current directory
2. Count today's sessions (scan .orch/session/ for YYYY-MM-DD prefix)
3. Generate name: {project}-{count}
4. Create .orch/session/{project}-{count}/active/SESSION_HANDOFF.md
5. Rename tmux window via tmux rename-window

Benefits:
- Consistent naming across tmux, session handoffs, discovery
- Easy to reference ("see orch-go-2 for context")
- Chronological ordering within a day
- Pattern supports multiple projects
close_reason: Implemented {project}-{count} naming with auto tmux rename. All tests passing, committed.

title: Cross-project agents show wrong project_dir - workspaces spawned with --workdir have session.directory set to orchestrator cwd instead of target
description:
## Root Cause

When spawning with --workdir, the OpenCode session is created with the orchestrator's current directory (where orch spawn runs) instead of the target --workdir.

The workspace's SPAWN_CONTEXT.md has correct PROJECT_DIR, but:
1. Workspace cache is built from session directories only
2. Sessions all point to orch-go
3. price-watch workspaces never get scanned
4. lookupProjectDir returns empty for cross-project agents

## Attempted Fixes (partial)

- 5148c2d2: Set ProjectDir from s.Directory (helps same-project, not cross-project)
- Moved workspace lookup outside !isStale (still doesn't help - cache never has cross-project data)

## Proposed Fix

Option A: Fix spawn to pass correct directory to OpenCode session creation
Option B: Scan workspaces from ALL project directories (config-based or discovered)
Option C: Use workspace PROJECT_DIR directly instead of session.directory

## Files Involved

- cmd/orch/spawn_cmd.go - session creation
- cmd/orch/serve_agents_cache.go - workspace cache building
- pkg/opencode/client.go - x-opencode-directory header
close_reason: Implementation complete - kb projects integration added to extractUniqueProjectDirs, verified 17 projects now scanned including price-watch

title: orch learn: resolved gaps still appear in suggestions
description:

Fix: Filter gaps from suggestions when an ImprovementRecord exists for that query.
close_reason: Fix already implemented in commit 11b1aea3. RecordResolution marks ALL matching events (not just one), and FindRecurringGaps filters out resolved events. Tests pass.

title: Add 'orch review done' command to mark items as reviewed
description:
## Problem

orch review shows 215+ completions indefinitely. No way to mark items as 'reviewed and understood'. We keep re-reading the same completions every session.

## The Gap (from completion lifecycle investigation)

Completion has 4 phases:
1. Verification (automated) - did agent produce deliverables? ✅ exists
2. Synthesis Review - orchestrator reads SYNTHESIS.md ✅ exists  
3. **Mental Model Sync - Dylan confirms understanding** ❌ MISSING
4. Follow-up Triage - create issues for discovered work ✅ manual

Phase 3 is the gap. Only Dylan can say 'I understand what this agent did and its implications.'

## Solution

Add `orch review done <workspace|beads-id>` command that:
1. Marks workspace as 'reviewed' (touch .reviewed or similar)
2. Removes from default `orch review` output
3. Still accessible via `orch review --all` or `orch review --archived`

Batch variant: `orch review done --project orch-go` to mark all reviewed for a project.

## Key Insight

This is a **human-in-the-loop** checkpoint. The orchestrator presents synthesis, Dylan confirms understanding, then it's archived. Without this, completions accumulate forever.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `orch review done <id>` marks single item reviewed
- [ ] `orch review done --project <name>` marks project batch
- [ ] `orch review` hides reviewed items by default
- [ ] `orch review --all` shows everything including reviewed
- [ ] Reviewed state persists in workspace (not beads)
close_reason: Implemented 'orch review done' to actually process completions: closes beads issues, cleans up tmux windows, logs events. Added -y/--yes flag for automation. All tests pass.

title: Document template ownership model

- skillc owns CLAUDE.md/SKILL.md compilation only - NOT artifact templates

This prevents future drift by establishing clear ownership boundaries.

title: Epic: Root Cause Thinking - Teaching the system to question before solving
description:
**Problem:** Agents consistently fix symptoms rather than investigating root causes. The system produces working fixes that don't address underlying design issues.

**Evidence (from today's session):**
1. Untracked visibility - Added .phase file workaround instead of questioning why untracked agents exist in this broken state
2. Agent error reporting - Recommended 'Immediate Checkpoint' skill changes instead of asking 'why do agents die without writing anything?'
3. Stale architect recommendations - Added filter call, didn't ask 'why is stale data in the system at all?'
4. Headless spawn race condition - Added message verification retry, didn't ask 'why does the API accept messages before ready?'

**Why this happens (hypotheses):**
- Skill design rewards completion, not depth
- Issue descriptions frame as symptoms ('Dashboard shows X' not 'Why does X happen?')
- No '5 whys' forcing function in workflow
- Completion pressure - root cause takes longer, feels less 'done'
- Orchestrator accepts symptom fixes without pushback

**Why epic-worthy:**
- Affects all skills (investigation, systematic-debugging, feature-impl)
- Compounds over time - every symptom fix is future tech debt
- Hard to fix incrementally - needs design thinking
- High leverage - if agents find root causes, we build better systems

**Phases:**
1. Investigation - Why does this happen? Analyze skill design, issue framing, incentives, completion criteria
2. Design - What would root-cause-first look like? (5 whys gate? reframing prompts? different success criteria?)
3. Implementation - Changes to skills/templates/process
4. Validation - Do agents actually find deeper problems now?

**Key uncertainty:** Is this solvable with process/skill changes, or is it a fundamental limitation of how we prompt agents?
close_reason: Investigation identified 4 root causes why orch system fixes symptoms: (1) completion gates reward correctness not depth, (2) design questioning triggers only on failure, (3) first-time fixes skip architectural review, (4) issue framing is symptom-level. Recommended fix: Add 'Design Implications' phase gate to systematic-debugging skill. See investigation file for full analysis and implementation recommendations.

title: Proactive rate limit monitoring in spawn
description:
**From investigation:** 2026-01-06-inv-diagnose-overall-66-completion-rate.md

Rate limiting causes 14-21% of all abandonments. Examples:
- 'Stalled due to rate limiting at 97% 5h usage'
- 'Hit rate limit on personal account mid-implementation'
- 'Orphaned from rate limit crash'

**Implementation:**
1. Check usage % before spawn
2. Warn at 80% usage
3. Block spawn at 95% usage (or auto-switch account)
4. Add usage to spawn telemetry events

**Success criteria:** Rate limit abandonments decrease by 50%+

title: Audit and fix invalid Svelte 5 event modifier syntax across web/
description:
Recurring pattern: code using Svelte 4 syntax like `onclick|stopPropagation` which is invalid in Svelte 5.

**Pattern found in:** screenshots-tab.svelte (fixed manually)

**Correct Svelte 5 syntax:**
- `onclick={(e) => { e.stopPropagation(); doThing(); }}`
- Or use Svelte's event modifiers properly

**Task:** Grep for `|stopPropagation`, `|preventDefault`, and similar patterns across web/src/ and fix all instances.
close_reason: Phase: Complete - Fixed 2 instances of invalid Svelte 4 event modifiers in service-log-viewer.svelte. Converted to Svelte 5 inline syntax. Build succeeded, no remaining invalid patterns found. Investigation and SYNTHESIS.md committed.

title: Gate orch spawn on beads dependency check
description:
orch spawn should refuse to spawn blocked issues.

## Problem

Orchestrator spawned jjd4.2 while jjd4.1 was still in progress, despite jjd4.2 depending on jjd4.1. The dependency was displayed—orchestrator read it—and still spawned both in parallel.

Mental checklists don't work under cognitive load. This needs to be a gate.

## Behavior

```
$ orch spawn feature-impl --issue orch-go-jjd4.2
Error: orch-go-jjd4.2 is blocked by orch-go-jjd4.1 (open)
Use --force to override
```

## Implementation

Before spawning with --issue:
1. Run `bd show <id>` or equivalent to get dependencies
2. Check if any 'Depends on' issues are still open
3. If blocked: error with message showing what's blocking
4. --force flag bypasses (for cases where orchestrator knows better)

## Also applies to daemon

Daemon should skip blocked issues when processing triage:ready queue. Don't spawn, don't error—just skip and log.

## Evidence

This conversation: orchestrator displayed dependency info and still spawned both P1s in parallel, optimizing for throughput over correctness.
close_reason: Implemented dependency gating for orch spawn --issue. Added --force flag to bypass. Daemon skips blocked issues silently. All tests pass.

title: Set beads issue status to in_progress on spawn
description: Python orch-cli calls 'bd update <id> --status in_progress' when spawning. orch-go is missing this - issues stay 'open' instead of 'in_progress'.

title: Disable Playwright MCP for orchestrator sessions
description: Orchestrator has Playwright MCP enabled which causes confusion with Glass browser tools. Orchestrator doesn't need browser automation - it delegates that work. Having both creates ambiguity about which tool to use. Disable Playwright MCP in orchestrator's opencode config.
close_reason: Not a code fix - MCP is session-scoped. Solution: start orchestrator sessions from orch-go, not glass. Recorded as constraint kn-1d1830.

title: Implement kb extract command for cross-project artifact migration
description:
From architect design session (orch-go-8sa5). When extracting a component to a new repo, knowledge artifacts need to follow.

Command: kb extract <artifact> --to <project>

Behavior:
- Copies artifact to target project's .kb/ directory
- Adds inline lineage header to copied artifact: 'extracted-from: <original-path>'
- Adds 'extracted-to: <new-path>' reference in original (optional, for discoverability)
- Preserves original (copy not move) for historical reference

Aligns with Session Amnesia principle - artifacts should be self-describing, not rely on external registry.

See: .kb/investigations/2025-12-22-inv-design-knowledge-system-project-extraction.md
close_reason: Implemented kb extract command with lineage tracking (extracted-from, source-project, extraction-date metadata), optional --update-source back-reference, and full test coverage

title: Context Injection: Pilot Progressive Disclosure for Investigation Skill

close_reason: Designed Core vs Reference split with four-dimension test (Frequency/Stage/Type/Complexity). Investigation file and SYNTHESIS.md committed. Recommend promoting four-dimension test to decision for future skill optimization.

title: Bug Reproducibility Gates
description:
Require reproduction evidence at bug creation and verification at completion.

## Problem

The Dec 27-Jan 2 spiral showed that bugs can be 'fixed' repeatedly without the symptom ever being verified as resolved. Agents fixed proximate causes while the root symptom persisted. 347 commits, 40 fix commits, 6 days lost, full rollback required.

Root cause: No gate requiring (1) reproduction before issue creation, or (2) reproduction check before closure.

## Solution

1. Bug issues require --repro flag with reproduction steps/evidence
2. orch complete for bugs gates on demonstrating repro no longer reproduces
3. 'Could not reproduce' becomes explicit outcome distinct from 'fixed'

## Evidence

Post-mortem: .kb/post-mortems/2026-01-02-system-spiral-dec27-jan02.md
Principle: Gate Over Remind (reminders fail under cognitive load)

## Success Criteria

- Cannot create bug issue without reproduction evidence
- Cannot close bug issue without reproduction verification
- 'Could not reproduce' tracked separately from 'fixed'
close_reason: All children complete: --repro gate at creation, repro verification at completion, CNR tracking, skill update, template update

title: Add --repro flag to bd create for bug type
description:
Add required --repro flag when creating bug-type issues.

## Behavior

- `bd create --type bug 'title'` without --repro: error with message explaining requirement
- `bd create --type bug 'title' --repro 'steps or evidence'`: creates issue with repro in metadata
- Repro stored in issue, visible in `bd show`, included in SPAWN_CONTEXT.md for agents

## Repro format

Free-form text. Could be:
- Command that demonstrates bug: `--repro 'orch status shows 27 active when only 4 running'`
- Steps: `--repro '1. Open dashboard 2. Check Active count 3. Compare to tmux windows'`
- Reference: `--repro 'See screenshot in /tmp/dead-agents.png'`

## Schema change

Add `repro` field to issue schema. Display in bd show under 'Reproduction:' section.

## Skip flag

`--no-repro --reason 'why'` allows bypass but records the skip reason. For cases where reproduction is genuinely not possible (e.g., one-time crash with no logs).
close_reason: Implemented --repro flag for bd create --type bug. Added Repro/NoReproReason to Issue schema, CLI validation, RPC support, database migration 033, and display in bd show. All tests pass.

title: Gate orch complete on reproduction verification for bugs
description:
Add reproduction verification gate to orch complete for bug-type issues.

## Behavior

When completing a bug issue:
1. orch complete reads the --repro from issue metadata
2. Prompts: 'Original reproduction: [repro]. Is this resolved? [y/n/could-not-reproduce]'
3. If 'y': proceeds with normal completion
4. If 'n': blocks completion, suggests respawn or re-diagnosis
5. If 'could-not-reproduce': closes with distinct status (not 'fixed')

## Agent workflow impact

Agents completing bug fixes must:
1. Run the original reproduction steps
2. Include result in completion evidence
3. Explicitly confirm symptom is gone

## Integration with SPAWN_CONTEXT

Reproduction steps already visible to agent (from issue 1). Agent knows upfront what 'fixed' means.

## Skip flag

`orch complete --skip-repro-check --reason 'why'` for edge cases. Logged.
close_reason: Feature already implemented by prior agent (commit 47dc6cfb). All tests pass. No additional work required.

title: Track 'could not reproduce' as distinct outcome
description:
Add explicit 'could-not-reproduce' close status distinct from 'fixed'.

## Problem

Currently 'already fixed' and 'could not reproduce' both result in closing the issue. But they mean different things:
- 'Fixed': symptom was present, now resolved
- 'Could not reproduce': symptom was never confirmed

## Solution

- Add `--outcome could-not-reproduce` to bd close (or through orch complete flow)
- Track in issue metadata
- Surface in bd stats: 'X bugs fixed, Y could not reproduce'

## Value

- Surfaces flaky/transient bugs explicitly
- Prevents false 'fixed' counts
- Helps identify issues that were created without proper reproduction
close_reason: Added could-not-reproduce as distinct close outcome (commit f88e94df in beads repo)

title: Update issue-creation skill to require reproduction
description:
Update the issue-creation skill to include reproduction attempt.

## Current behavior

issue-creation investigates the symptom and creates a typed issue with problem/solution/evidence structure.

## New behavior

For bug-type issues, skill must:
1. Attempt to reproduce the reported symptom
2. Record reproduction steps that work
3. Include --repro flag in the bd create call
4. If cannot reproduce: create issue with type 'investigation' instead, or flag for human review

## Skill file

~/.claude/skills/meta/issue-creation/SKILL.md (or wherever issue-creation lives)

## Dependency

Requires orch-go-jjd4.1 (--repro flag exists)
close_reason: Updated issue-creation skill to require reproduction for bugs. Skill now uses --repro flag, has decision tree for non-reproducible issues, and can fallback to investigation type. Deployed to ~/.claude/skills/.

title: Add reproduction to SPAWN_CONTEXT template for bugs
description:
Include reproduction steps in SPAWN_CONTEXT.md when spawning for bug issues.

## Current state

SPAWN_CONTEXT.md includes issue title, description, acceptance criteria, but not structured reproduction steps.

## Change

Add 'Reproduction' section to SPAWN_CONTEXT.md template when issue has --repro metadata:

```
## Reproduction

The following reproduces this bug:
[repro from issue]

Your fix is verified when these steps NO LONGER reproduce the symptom.
```

## Location

Likely in orch spawn template generation (cmd/orch/spawn.go or similar).

## Dependency

Requires orch-go-jjd4.1 (--repro field exists in issue schema)
close_reason: Added REPRODUCTION section to SPAWN_CONTEXT.md template that is conditionally included for bug issues. Extracts repro steps from beads issue and instructs agent to verify fix by confirming repro steps no longer produce bug. All tests pass.

title: Codebase cleanup: Remove deprecated root artifacts
description:
## Problem

The orch-go codebase has accumulated organizational debt at the project root:
- Root main.go (519 lines) is byte-for-byte identical to legacy/main.go
- ~75MB of uncommitted executables (orch, orch-new, orch-test, etc.)
- .bak files in cmd/orch/
- debug_sse.go with build ignore tag adding clutter

## Context

From audit: .kb/investigations/2025-12-23-audit-organizational-orch-go-codebase-structure.md

README already states: 'The legacy monolithic main.go at project root is deprecated'
The real CLI is cmd/orch/main.go (3,221 lines).

## Solution

### Phase 1: Update .gitignore (safe, immediate)
- Add patterns: `orch-*`, `test-*` (for build artifacts)
- Already have `orch-go` and `build/`

### Phase 2: Delete stale artifacts
```bash
rm cmd/orch/main.go.bak cmd/orch/wait_test.go.bak
rm orch orch-new orch-test orch-test-serve test-orch-go  # if untracked
```

### Phase 3: Remove deprecated root files
```bash
rm main.go main_test.go
# Keep debug_sse.go (has build ignore tag, harmless) or move to tools/
```

### Phase 4: Optional Makefile enhancement
- Add `clean-all` target to remove root executables

## Trade-offs

- `go build .` from root will fail (desirable - forces correct usage via cmd/orch/)
- legacy/ preserved for reference

## Success Criteria

- [ ] `ls *.go` at root shows only debug_sse.go (or nothing)
- [ ] `go build ./cmd/orch/` continues to work
- [ ] No .bak files in codebase
- [ ] .gitignore prevents future artifact accumulation
close_reason: Verified root cleanup already 90% done (no .go or .bak files); removed ~50MB gitignored executables; all success criteria verified (no .go at root, build works, no .bak, .gitignore configured)

title: [orch-go] investigation: timing test 2
Add orchestrator/worker distinction to action-log.jsonl entries
description:
**Goal:** Enable analysis of orchestrator vs worker session patterns by tagging action log entries.

**Current state:** action-log.jsonl has session_id but no way to distinguish orchestrator from worker sessions.

**Distinguishing signals available:**
- Session title pattern: workers have `og-feat-xxx [beads-id]`, orchestrators are untitled
- Workspace location: workers run from `.orch/workspace/`, orchestrators from project root
- SPAWN_CONTEXT.md exists: workers have it, orchestrators don't
- `orch session start` called: orchestrators (if using new unified flow)
- Runs `orch spawn`: almost always orchestrators

**Implementation options:**
1. Add `is_orchestrator: bool` field to ActionEvent struct
2. Detect at log time: check session title pattern OR cwd contains `.orch/workspace/`
3. Could also add `beads_id` field for workers (extract from session title)

**Value:** Enables pattern analysis like 'what do orchestrators do vs workers', friction detection per role, autonomy grant patterns.

**Related:** orch-go-65tv investigated orchestrator patterns, this enables deeper analysis.
close_reason: Added is_orchestrator and beads_id fields to ActionEvent with auto-detection. Detection uses session title pattern [beads-id] and .orch/workspace/ path. 11 new test functions, all 26 action tests passing. Committed as 28be81c6.

title: Dashboard shows completed agents as active until 10min timeout
description:
## Problem
Dashboard 'Active' section shows agents that have completed their work but OpenCode session is still open. Currently using 10-minute activity timeout to determine active vs idle.

## Evidence
Agent og-feat-revise-investigation-2025-25dec completed its work (committed investigation revision) but dashboard showed it as 'active' at 4m 45s runtime.

## Solution
Check beads phase status (Phase: Complete) in addition to activity timeout. If agent has reported Phase: Complete via beads comment, move to Recent immediately regardless of session activity.

## Implementation
1. In serve.go handleAgents(), check if agent has Phase: Complete beads comment
2. If Phase: Complete, set status='completed' instead of 'active'
3. Dashboard will then show it in Recent section
close_reason: Batch close from orch review

title: Separate orch serve status from orch servers
description:
From architect investigation orch-go-lb61.

orch serve is infrastructure (global monitoring API), not a project server. Should not be in project port allocations.

**Implementation:**
1. Remove API server (port 3348) from project port registry
2. Add dedicated status command: `orch api status` or fold into `orch daemon status`
3. Show: running/stopped, port, uptime, connected clients

**Rationale:** orch servers = project dev infrastructure (tmux-managed, ephemeral). orch serve = orchestrator infrastructure (standalone, persistent). Different operational patterns.

See: .kb/investigations/2025-12-23-inv-design-question-should-orch-servers.md

title: Epic: Spawnable orchestrator sessions
description:
Infrastructure changes to support spawning orchestrator-type skills with appropriate context, visibility, and verification.

**Frame shift context:** Meta-orchestrator manages orchestrator sessions like orchestrators manage workers. This epic enables that by making orchestrator sessions first-class spawnable entities.

**Key differences from worker spawns:**
- Context: ORCHESTRATOR_CONTEXT.md (not SPAWN_CONTEXT.md)
- Visibility: Tmux default (not headless)
- Artifact: SESSION_HANDOFF.md (not SYNTHESIS.md)
- Tracking: Session-level (not issue-level beads)

**Reference:** .kb/investigations/2026-01-04-inv-spawnable-orchestrator-sessions-infrastructure-changes.md

```
Now I have a clear picture. Let me design the system learning loop

Now I have a clear picture. Let me design the
Now I have a clear picture. Let me design
Now I have a clear picture. Let
```

This is the same message at different points in generation. Should either:
1. Only show final message (dedupe by replacing previous with longer version)
2. Show streaming for CURRENT message only, collapse to final when complete
3. Dedupe consecutive messages where one is a prefix of another

The issue persists despite orch-go-j5ph claiming to fix it.

title: Post provenance system - trace artifacts and prompts that produced output
description:
Posts (and other synthesized artifacts) should be able to include their provenance chain - which sources were consulted, which prompts shaped the output, how the thread evolved.

**Why this matters:**
- Validation loop resistance: provenance is external verification, not 'I thought about it and this felt true'
- Session amnesia compensation: next Claude can understand not just what was written but how it emerged
- Distinguishes synthesis from generation: shows assembly from grounded sources, not confabulation

**What it might look like:**
```markdown
<!-- PROVENANCE -->
<!-- Session: 2025-12-24-blog-amnesia-post -->
<!-- Thread: SESSION_HANDOFF.md → Karpathy discussion → outline → draft -->
<!-- Sources consulted: 
<!--   - ~/.kb/principles.md
<!--   - sources/2025-12-19-karpathy-year-in-review-2025.md
<!-- Prompts: .orch/workspace/blog-amnesia-post/conversation.jsonl -->
<!-- /PROVENANCE -->
```

**Would require:**
- Conversation capture (OpenCode API could provide this)
- Source tracking (which files were read, when)
- Prompt lineage (how the task evolved)

**Origin:** Discussion during blog post outlining session. Recognized as logical endpoint of evidence hierarchy + self-describing artifacts principles, but explicitly deferred to ship the post first.

**Principle:** Reflection before action - worth building eventually, but not instead of the immediate work.
close_reason: Designed 3-tier provenance system (session ID → source refs → thread lineage). Investigation file at .kb/investigations/2025-12-27-inv-post-provenance-system-trace-artifacts.md. Key insight: OpenCode API already captures raw data via GetMessages(), we just need extraction/formatting. Next: add provenance block to templates, create orch provenance command.

title: Investigate question-asking process - validate premises before execution
description:
**Pattern observed:** We asked 'how do we evolve skills to be where value resides?' and spawned an epic before asking 'should we do this?'

The architect review caught the flawed premise and saved wasted work. This suggests a process gap.

**Questions to answer:**
1. What's the right sequence for strategic questions? (should → how → execute)
2. How does this relate to existing principles? (Reflection Before Action, Evolve by Distinction)
3. Should design-session skill include premise validation as a phase?
4. Should orchestrator skill have guidance on question sequencing?
5. Is this worth promoting to principles.md?

**Evidence:**
- Epic orch-go-erdw created assuming direction was correct
- Architect orch-go-fwmw found premise was wrong (current separation is intentional)
- Constraint captured: kn-c12998

**Potential outcomes:**
- Update design-session skill with premise validation phase
- Add question sequencing guidance to orchestrator skill
- Promote to principles if pattern recurs

title: Fix headless spawn model selection - pass model to message, not session
description:
Root cause: OpenCode uses per-message model selection (by design), but orch-go passes model to CreateSession (ignored) instead of SendMessageAsync.

**The learning:** OpenCode model selection is per-message, not per-session. This enables mid-conversation model switching, cost optimization, and flexibility. Not a bug - intentional design.

**Fix needed in orch-go:**
1. Update SendMessageAsync to accept model parameter
2. Pass model in payload: `"model": { "providerID": "anthropic", "modelID": "..." }`
3. Remove Model from CreateSessionRequest (it's ignored anyway)
4. Thread model from spawn flags through to first message

**Also consider:**
- Add --model flag to `orch send` for follow-up messages
- Store intended model in workspace/.model for session consistency

**Evidence:** 
- OpenCode run.ts:233-238 passes model to sdk.session.prompt(), not create()
- OpenCode session/index.ts:124-139 create schema has no model field
- Investigation: .kb/investigations/2025-12-23-inv-model-selection-issue-architect-agent.md

**Decision to record:** OpenCode model selection is per-message by design
close_reason: Batch close from orch review

title: Update meta-orchestrator skill with session learnings from 2026-01-05
description:
Add five new sections to meta-orchestrator skill based on learnings from meta-orchestrator session 2026-01-05:

## Sections to Add

1. **The Conversational Frame** - Meta-orchestrator is a thinking partner with Dylan, not another autonomous execution layer. Primary value: post-mortem perspective, pattern recognition, real-time frame correction.

2. **Goal Refinement Before Spawn** - Core responsibility: translate strategic intent into actionable orchestrator goals. Workflow: understand intent → check project state → identify specific goal → propose with success criteria → spawn on confirmation.

3. **Vague Goals Cause Frame Collapse** - Pattern: vague goal → exploration → investigation → debugging (frame collapsed). Fix: specific goals with action verbs (triage, ship, complete), concrete deliverables, success criteria.

4. **The Spawn Improvement Loop** - Meta-orchestrator's primary workflow: spawn → observe/review handoff → diagnose friction → improve next spawn.

5. **Post-Mortem Perspective** - The core unlock: meta-orchestrator can see orchestrator failure patterns that orchestrators can't see about themselves (same as orchestrators seeing worker patterns).

## Also Add

- New failure mode to Orchestrator Failure Modes table: 'Frame collapse from vague goal'

## Source

Conversation from meta-orchestrator session og-work-meta-orchestrator-session-05jan (2026-01-05). Draft content available in session transcript.

## Files

- Skill source: ~/orch-knowledge/skills/src/meta/meta-orchestrator/.skillc/
- Deploy with: skillc deploy meta-orchestrator
close_reason: Added 5 new sections to meta-orchestrator skill: The Conversational Frame, Post-Mortem Perspective, Goal Refinement Before Spawn, Vague Goals Cause Frame Collapse, The Spawn Improvement Loop. Also added frame collapse failure mode to table. Deployed via skillc.

title: Update model template with enable/constrain pattern + consider kb create model tooling
description:
Current TEMPLATE.md Constraints section (lines 39-44) is vague. Doesn't specify enable/constrain pattern.

**Problem:** 
- Morning agents (N=4) independently discovered pattern
- Afternoon agents (N=6) didn't use it
- Template doesn't enforce consistency

**Solution options:**
1. **Update template** - Add explicit enable/constrain format to Constraints section
2. **Create tooling** - 'kb create model' that scaffolds from template with guidance
3. **Both** - Template + tooling for consistency

**Current template vagueness:**
```markdown
## Constraints
{What limits exist? Why can't we "just do X"? Make implicit constraints explicit.}

**Why {constraint}:**
{Explanation - this is where you create surface area for strategic questions}
```

**Should be:**
```markdown
## Constraints

### Why {Constraint Question}?

**Constraint:** {What's the limitation}

**Implication:** {What this means in practice}

**This enables:** {What becomes possible}
**This constrains:** {What's no longer allowed/possible}
```

**Consider:** Does `kb create model` tooling add value or is template update sufficient?

**Deliverable:** Template updated + decision on tooling
close_reason: Updated TEMPLATE.md Constraints section with explicit enable/constrain pattern. Decided kb create model tooling not needed (models are synthesized artifacts - template structure sufficient). Committed: 53f332c3

title: Epic readiness gate: Understanding section for epics
description:
Enhance beads to prompt for Understanding section when creating epics.

## Context
Strategic Orchestrator Model (2026-01-07) defines epic readiness as model completeness, not task list completeness.

## The 5 questions (from decision)
An epic is 'ready' when you can explain:
1. What problem we're solving (not symptoms)
2. Why previous approaches failed
3. What the key constraints are
4. Where the risks live
5. What 'done' looks like

## Implementation
- `bd create --type epic` prompts for Understanding section with these 5 questions
- Light touch: `bd ready` warns if epic missing Understanding section (surfacing, not blocking)

## Principle
Surfacing Over Browsing - understanding should be IN the issue, not a separate file

## Location
beads repo

## Validation
- `bd create --type epic` prompts for the 5 questions
- `bd ready` shows warning for epics without Understanding section
close_reason: Implemented epic understanding gate: bd create --type epic now requires --understanding or --no-understanding --reason. bd ready shows warnings for epics missing Understanding section. Tests pass. Changes in beads repo at ~/Documents/personal/beads (not yet committed there).

title: Context Injection: Probe 4 - Usage Analysis
description: Analyze recent orchestrator and worker session transcripts to determine which injected context (from hooks and SPAWN_CONTEXT.md) is actually referenced or utilized. Identify redundant or ignored content to optimize the 25K token startup overhead.
close_reason: Investigation complete: 68% of SPAWN_CONTEXT is skill content (70% reference material), recommend progressive disclosure for 33-40% token reduction. Investigation file at .kb/investigations/2026-01-17-inv-analyze-recent-session-transcripts-action.md

title: Remove beads tracking from orchestrator/meta-orchestrator spawns
description:
Orchestrators are interactive sessions with Dylan, not autonomous tasks. Beads tracking (issue creation, Phase comments) doesn't fit the model.

**Why remove beads for orchestrators:**
- Orchestrators aren't discovered/triaged - Dylan spawns them and interacts directly
- No async coordination needed - Dylan is right there
- SESSION_HANDOFF.md is richer than beads comments could be
- Phase: Complete doesn't fit (orchestrators don't have phases)
- Current model forces worker patterns onto a different thing

**Changes needed:**
1. `orch spawn orchestrator`: skip `bd create`, don't write `.beads_id`
2. `orch complete`: accept workspace name for orchestrators, check SESSION_HANDOFF.md as completion signal
3. `orch status`: show orchestrators separately (from registry, filter by `.orchestrator` marker)
4. Remove Phase: Complete requirement for orchestrator completion

**Completion signal:** SESSION_HANDOFF.md exists in workspace

**Registry already tracks enough:**
- session_id, workspace path, project - sufficient for lookup and completion

Related: orch-go-i3ro (ORCHESTRATOR_CONTEXT.md contradicts decision on session end)
close_reason: Tests verified by orchestrator: go test ./cmd/orch/... -run TestOrchestrator - all 4 tests PASS

title: Agents claim file modifications without actual changes (hallucinated success)
description: Multiple agents in session wrote SYNTHESIS.md claiming they modified files (e.g., cmd/orch/main.go, pkg/daemon/daemon.go), but git diff showed no changes. They hallucinated their own success. Need stricter completion verification that checks git diff output before accepting 'Phase: Complete'.

title: orch serve cache not invalidated on agent completion
description:
The TTL cache added in orch-go-ez2f caches agent status but doesn't invalidate when agents complete. Dashboard shows stale 'active' status after orch complete.

## Root cause
Cache TTL (probably 30s-60s) holds stale data. No cache invalidation on completion.

## Solution
Either:
1. Invalidate cache entry on orch complete
2. Check beads status directly for active agents (only cache historical)
3. Shorter TTL for active agents vs completed
close_reason: Fixed cache invalidation by adding POST /api/cache/invalidate endpoint and calling it from orch complete. Tests: go test ./cmd/orch/... - ok 36.817s

See .kb/investigations/2025-12-22-inv-de-bloat-feature-impl-skill.md

title: Agents should report Phase: Complete BEFORE final commit
description:
**Problem:** Agents do their work, commit code, but die before reporting Phase: Complete via bd comment. The work is done but the system doesn't know.

**Evidence this session:** ys34 agent wrote complete investigation file with 'Phase: Complete' in it, committed code, but never ran bd comment. Had to manually close.

**Root cause:** No forcing function between 'work done' and 'phase reported'. Agent can commit and then die (rate limit, context exhaustion).

**Possible solutions:**
1. Skill phase ordering: report Phase BEFORE final commit (gate commit on reporting)
2. Post-commit hook that checks for Phase: Complete comment
3. orch complete could detect commits exist but no Phase comment and prompt

**Impact:** Orchestrator has to manually track which agents actually finished vs just died.
close_reason: Updated skill templates for phase-first reporting. No web/ UI changes - template docs only. Tests pass.

title: Detect stale/zombie sessions after OpenCode restart
description:
After OpenCode server restart, sessions with pending tool calls appear 'active' in dashboard but are actually dead (tool will never complete).

**Symptom:** Dashboard shows 'Waiting for activity...' indefinitely for agents that were killed mid-execution.

**Root cause:** OpenCode persists session state but doesn't mark interrupted sessions. Sessions with `tool.state.status: 'pending'` appear as 'processing' even when stale.

**Fix:** In `handleAgents()` and `runStatus()`, detect stale sessions:
- If tool pending for >5 min with no message updates → mark as 'stale' or 'interrupted'
- Show in dashboard with different status (not 'active')

**Files:**
- cmd/orch/serve.go:handleAgents()
- cmd/orch/main.go:runStatus()
- pkg/opencode/client.go (may need helper)

**Investigation:** .kb/investigations/2025-12-28-inv-stale-sessions-after-opencode-restart.md
close_reason: Dead/stalled agent frontend handling

title: Implement session resume protocol (automatic handoff injection)
description:
Implement the hybrid session resume protocol designed in .kb/investigations/2026-01-11-design-session-resume-protocol.md

**Goal:** Zero cognitive load for Dylan - fresh Claude instances automatically receive prior session context via handoff injection.

**Approach:** Hybrid (automated hooks + manual fallback)
- Primary: OpenCode plugin + Claude Code hook auto-inject
- Fallback: `orch resume` CLI command for manual use
- Project-specific handoffs via .orch/session/latest symlink

**Implementation phases:**
1. Core command (`orch resume` with discovery + formatting)
2. Session end integration (update latest symlink)
3. Hook integration (OpenCode plugin + Claude Code hook)
4. Handoff format optimization (condensed for injection)

**Design:** .kb/investigations/2026-01-11-design-session-resume-protocol.md

title: Add rate limiting to daemon agent spawning
description:
## Problem

Daemon spawns agents for all `triage:ready` issues without limit. With aggressive batch-labeling, this caused 32+ concurrent agents and 193 commits in 2 days.

## Solution

Add configurable rate limiting to daemon:
1. Max concurrent agents per project (e.g., 5)
2. Max spawns per hour (e.g., 20)
3. Backoff when hitting limits

## Implementation

Location: `pkg/daemon/daemon.go`

```go
type RateLimiter struct {
    MaxConcurrent int           // e.g., 5
    MaxPerHour    int           // e.g., 20
    SpawnHistory  []time.Time
}

func (d *Daemon) canSpawn() bool {
    // Check concurrent limit
    // Check hourly limit
    // Return false if either exceeded
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Daemon respects MaxConcurrent setting
- [ ] Daemon respects MaxPerHour setting
- [ ] Config in `~/.orch/config.yaml` or `.orch/config.yaml`
- [ ] `orch daemon preview` shows "rate limited" when applicable
- [ ] Logs when rate limit hit
close_reason: Added rate limiting to daemon (20/hour default)

title: Beads type field shows null in JSON but displays correctly in bd show
description:
## Problem

`bd show` displays 'Type: task' but JSON serialization shows `"type": null`. This inconsistency caused daemon to silently reject issues that appeared correctly configured.

## Evidence (session ses_474f)

Lines 540-545:
> 'The type IS task in `bd show` output but the JSON shows null. There might be a JSON serialization issue in bd.'

Orchestrator spent ~10 turns debugging this, eventually the daemon worked anyway (likely after a retry that fixed the race condition).

## Impact

- Daemon silently rejects issues with null type
- Orchestrator can't diagnose because `bd show` looks correct
- Wastes debugging time on phantom issues

## Solution

Fix JSON serialization to match display output. If type is set, it should serialize correctly.

## Note

This may be a beads-cli issue rather than orch-go. Cross-reference with beads repo if needed.
close_reason: Root cause: JSON field is 'issue_type' not 'type'. Querying .type returns null because field doesn't exist. This is could-not-reproduce - no code bug found. Tests: verified bd list --json | jq '.[0].issue_type' returns correct value 'task'

title: Integrate skillc verify into orch complete
description:
## Problem

`skillc verify` exists but nothing invokes it automatically. Agents can complete without running verification. The gate exists but isn't installed in the doorway.

## Solution

When `orch complete` runs, invoke `skillc verify` against the agent's workspace:

1. Look up which skill was used (from beads issue metadata or SPAWN_CONTEXT.md)
2. Find the skill's .skillc manifest (skill registry at ~/.claude/skills/ or embedded in spawn context)
3. Run `skillc verify <skill-path> --workdir <workspace>`
4. Fail completion if verify fails (exit code 1)

## Implementation

In `pkg/verify/check.go`:

```go
// After existing checks (phase, deliverables, commits)
if skill := getSkillFromSpawnContext(workspacePath); skill != "" {
    if err := runSkillcVerify(skill, workspacePath); err != nil {
        return fmt.Errorf("skill verification failed: %w", err)
    }
}
```

## Considerations

- Need to locate skill manifest from skill name (registry lookup)
- Some skills may not have outputs.required defined (skip gracefully)
- `skillc` binary must be available (check in orch doctor?)
- JSON output mode for parsing results

## Success Criteria

- [ ] `orch complete` runs `skillc verify` when skill has outputs defined
- [ ] Completion blocked if verify fails
- [ ] Clear error message showing what's missing
- [ ] Graceful skip if skill has no outputs.required
- [ ] Works with investigation, feature-impl skills
close_reason: Integrated skill output verification into orch complete. Added pkg/verify/skill_outputs.go with 16 tests. VerifyCompletionFull now checks skill.yaml outputs.required patterns. Graceful skip when skill has no outputs defined.

title: Add verification metrics to orch stats
description:
## Problem
Verification failures are being logged to events.jsonl (17 events so far) but orch stats doesn't surface this data. We can't see:
- Which verification gates fail most often
- Verification pass/fail rate
- Common failure patterns by skill

## Evidence
- Logging implemented: pkg/events/logger.go LogVerificationFailed()
- Events exist: grep -c verification.failed ~/.orch/events.jsonl → 17
- Stats doesn't read them: grep verification cmd/orch/stats_cmd.go → no results

## Solution
Add verification section to orch stats output:
```
📋 VERIFICATION METRICS
  Total attempts:     295
  Passed:            278 (94.2%)
  Failed:             17 (5.8%)
  
  Gates failed (top 5):
    test_evidence:    8 (47%)
    phase_complete:   4 (24%)
    git_diff:         3 (18%)
    visual_evidence:  2 (12%)
```

## Acceptance
- orch stats shows verification pass/fail rates
- Shows breakdown by gate type
- Optionally shows by skill
close_reason: Added verification metrics to orch stats showing pass/fail/bypass rates with gate-type breakdown. Tests pass. Commit 07795e75.

title: Epic: orch init and Project Standardization
description:
## Goal

Provide a single command (`orch init`) that bootstraps all project components needed for the orch ecosystem, with proper port allocation to prevent conflicts.

## Scope

- Add `orch init` command to orchestrate project setup
- Implement port allocation registry to prevent conflicts
- Enhance tmuxinator config generation with project-specific settings
- Create CLAUDE.md template system for consistent project context

## Out of Scope

- Changes to beads (bd init) or kb-cli (kb init) - we shell out to these
- Migration of existing projects (manual or separate script)
- Web UI for port management

## Success Criteria

- [ ] `orch init` creates .beads/, .kb/, .orch/, CLAUDE.md in one command
- [ ] Port conflicts are prevented via ~/.orch/ports.yaml registry
- [ ] Tmuxinator configs include dev server commands with allocated ports
- [ ] CLAUDE.md templates exist for go-cli, svelte-app, python-cli types

## Investigation

See: .kb/investigations/2025-12-21-inv-scope-orch-init-project-standardization.md
close_reason: Batch close from orch review

title: Add orch init command
description:
## Context

Part of orch-go-lqll: Epic: orch init and Project Standardization

## Task

Implement `orch init` command that bootstraps all project components:

```bash
orch init [--type go-cli|svelte-app|python-cli] [--port NNNN] [--yes]
```

**Behavior:**
1. Runs `bd init` if .beads/ doesn't exist
2. Runs `kb init` if .kb/ doesn't exist
3. Creates `.orch/workspace/` and `.orch/templates/`
4. Generates CLAUDE.md from template (based on --type or auto-detected)
5. Allocates dev port via port registry (Task 2)
6. Creates/updates tmuxinator config with allocated port (Task 3)

**Flags:**
- `--type`: Project type for template selection (auto-detect if not specified)
- `--port`: Explicit port allocation (otherwise auto-allocate)
- `--yes`: Non-interactive mode

**Auto-detection logic:**
- go.mod exists → go-cli
- package.json with svelte → svelte-app
- pyproject.toml exists → python-cli
- Otherwise → minimal

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `orch init` creates .beads/ via bd init
- [ ] `orch init` creates .kb/ via kb init
- [ ] `orch init` creates .orch/workspace/ directory
- [ ] `orch init` generates CLAUDE.md from template
- [ ] Auto-detection works for go, svelte, python projects
- [ ] `--yes` flag skips all prompts
- [ ] Idempotent - safe to run multiple times
