title: Complete Phase 4: Remove agent registry file
description:
## Problem

The agent registry (~/.orch/agent-registry.json) was necessary when tmux was the only way to manage agents. Now that OpenCode provides REST API + SSE, the registry causes more problems than it solves:
- Drift between registry, tmux, OpenCode, beads (four-layer reconciliation)
- Ghost agents from stale entries
- 500+ lines of merge conflict, tombstone, locking logic

## Context

From synthesis: .kb/investigations/2025-12-21-synthesis-registry-evolution-and-orch-identity.md

The Dec 6 investigation (orch-cli) proposed phased migration:
- Phase 1: ✅ Store agent metadata in beads comments
- Phase 2: ✅ Beads-first lookup with registry fallback  
- Phase 3: ✅ Registry stripped to minimal tmux mapping
- Phase 4: ❌ Remove registry file entirely

## Solution

1. Update `orch status` to query OpenCode API + tmux directly (no registry read)
2. Update `orch complete` to work without registry lookup
3. Remove registry file creation from spawn flow
4. Delete registry reconciliation code
5. Remove `~/.orch/agent-registry.json` management entirely

## Trade-offs

- `orch status` will be ~100-300ms slower (acceptable for interactive command)
- Fulfills Dec 1 architectural vision: 'orch orchestrates, but doesn't own state'

## Success Criteria

- [ ] `orch status` works without reading registry file
- [ ] `orch complete` works without registry lookup
- [ ] No registry file created on spawn
- [ ] Registry-related code removed from pkg/
- [ ] No ghost agent issues
close_reason: Removed agent registry references from documentation and tests. Updated CLAUDE.md architecture section, gendoc command docs, fixed stale comments in focus.go and test files. All tests pass.

title: GetIssuesBatch needs to resolve short IDs to full IDs before List call
description:
Follow-up from nfrr fix:

The fix used ListArgs{IDs: beadsIDs} but beadsIDs contains short IDs (e.g., '51jz') extracted from OpenCode session titles. Beads List expects full IDs (e.g., 'orch-go-51jz').

Fix options:
1. Resolve short IDs to full IDs using client.ResolveID() before List call
2. Use individual Show() calls which support short ID resolution
3. Update extractBeadsIDFromTitle to include project prefix

Option 2 is simplest since Show already handles short IDs.
close_reason: Fixed short ID resolution - now uses Show() calls which handle ID resolution

title: Verification: Exempt non-code work from test_evidence gate
description:
## Problem
test_evidence gate blocks completion for work that has no tests to run:
- Synthesis/consolidation (produces guides, not code)
- Documentation updates
- Plugin configuration (TypeScript in ~/.config, no test harness)
- Skill updates (markdown files)

14 test_evidence failures in events - likely many are false requirements.

## Evidence
- 14 test_evidence failures (most common verification failure)
- Synthesis agents blocked despite producing valid guides

## Solution
Detect work type from:
1. Skill type (synthesis work via kb-reflect skill)
2. Files modified (only .md files, only plugin .ts in ~/.config)
3. SYNTHESIS.md outcome field

Exempt appropriate work types from test_evidence requirement.

## Acceptance
- Synthesis/documentation work completes without test evidence demand
- Code-modifying work still requires test evidence
- Clear in verification output why gate was skipped
close_reason: Added markdown-only and outside-project exemptions to test_evidence gate. Synthesis/doc work now completes without test evidence demand. All 53 verify tests pass.

title: Dashboard connection pool exhaustion: SSE connections blocking API fetches
description: Root cause: Two SSE connections (events and agentlog) consume 2 of the 6 available HTTP/1.1 connections per origin. When all connections are busy with SSE or slow responses, new fetch requests queue up as pending. Quick fix: Don't auto-connect agentlog SSE on page load - it's already deferred but still connects. Long-term: HTTP/2 or single multiplexed SSE.

title: Implement kb ask command for inline mini-investigations
description:
## Problem

Every investigation spawn does semantic query answering (kb context → read → synthesize), but with 30+ second overhead. Dylan's 'how would the system recommend...' pattern revealed the desire for SYNCHRONOUS/INLINE answers without spawn overhead.

## Proposed Solution

Add `kb ask "question"` command that:
1. Runs kb context with question keywords
2. Reads top N matching artifacts (investigations, decisions, kn entries)
3. Sends to LLM with synthesis prompt
4. Returns answer inline (~5-10 seconds)

## Key Trade-off

| Mode | Latency | Artifact | Use When |
|------|---------|----------|----------|
| `kb ask` | ~5s | None (ephemeral) | Quick questions, exploratory |
| `orch spawn investigation` | ~30s+ | Yes (.kb/investigations/) | Questions worth preserving |

## Implementation Notes

- Optional `--save` flag to create investigation artifact
- LLM integration: use same model as opencode default
- Cost consideration: every ask is an LLM call (unlike kb context which is grep)

## Example Usage

```bash
# Quick inline answer
kb ask "how should we sort the swarm map?"

# Save as investigation if answer is valuable
kb ask "how should we sort the swarm map?" --save
```

## Success Criteria

- [ ] Returns useful synthesis in <10 seconds
- [ ] Clear guidance on ask vs spawn decision
- [ ] Optional --save for questions worth preserving

## References

- Investigation: .kb/investigations/2025-12-24-inv-investigate-emerging-pattern-how-would.md
- kn decisions: kn-38b772, kn-3e8bd6
close_reason: Implemented orch kb ask command for inline mini-investigations. Runs kb context, reads top N artifacts, synthesizes via OpenCode (~10-20s). Supports --save for investigation artifacts, --global for cross-project search, --limit for artifact count. Tests pass, committed.

title: Dashboard: Playwright tests should run headless
description:
## Problem

When spawning an agent with `--mcp playwright`, the Playwright browser opens visibly instead of running headless. This is disruptive during development.

## Example

```bash
orch spawn --mcp playwright feature-impl "test UI"
# Browser window pops up visibly
```

## Expected Behavior

Playwright MCP server should run browsers in headless mode by default.

## Investigation Needed

- Check how --mcp playwright configures the browser
- May need to pass headless option to Playwright MCP server
- Check if there's an environment variable or config option
close_reason: Fixed: Added --headless flag to Playwright MCP server config in dotfiles

title: [stabilization] Deep investigation: registry vs OpenCode state
description: Two sources of truth are diverging: orch registry (~/.orch/agent-registry.json) and OpenCode sessions (~/.local/share/opencode/storage/session/). When they get out of sync, orch status shows ghost data. Questions: Should orch even have its own registry? How should reconciliation work? What's the intended session lifecycle in OpenCode?

title: Fix synthesis completion recognition to prevent false spawns
description:
Investigation: 2026-01-08-inv-synthesize-model-investigations-11-synthesis-work.md

Context: Fourth spawn for same synthesis task in one day - work was completed Jan 6, yet daemon keeps spawning. Each wasted spawn costs ~$0.50-2.00 + 30-60 minutes agent time.

Root cause: Three compounding failures:
1. Dedup JSON parse failure returns 'no duplicate' on error
2. No recognition of COMPLETED synthesis
3. Polysemous 'model' keyword matches 5+ unrelated topics

Recommendation: Mark synthesis as 'COMPLETED' to prevent future spawns. Escalate kb-cli dedup fix as urgent. Add completion recognition pattern.

Evidence: Guide at .kb/guides/model-selection.md (326 lines, Jan 6). Three beads issues: orch-go-bn6io, orch-go-p1mxh, orch-go-goiq9.

title: Implement orch servers command for centralized server management
description:
Add CLI commands to manage dev servers across all projects from a single place.

**Commands to implement (in order):**
1. `orch servers` / `orch servers list` - Show all projects with port allocations and running status (check lsof for actual binding)
2. `orch servers start <project>` - Start servers via tmuxinator (tmuxinator start workers-{project})
3. `orch servers stop <project>` - Stop servers pane (send Ctrl+C to servers window)
4. `orch servers attach <project>` - Switch to servers pane in tmux
5. `orch servers open <project>` - Open localhost:{web_port} in browser
6. `orch servers status` - Summary: X running, Y stopped, Z total

**Existing infrastructure to use:**
- Port registry: ~/.orch/ports.yaml (pkg/port)
- Tmuxinator configs: ~/.tmuxinator/workers-{project}.yml (pkg/tmux)
- Tmux sessions: workers-{project} with 'servers' window

**Constraints:**
- Must detect actual port binding (lsof -i :{port}) not just allocation
- Don't kill agent windows when stopping servers
- Handle missing tmuxinator configs gracefully

**Reference:** .kb/investigations/2025-12-23-inv-explore-options-centralized-server-management.md
close_reason: Implemented orch servers command with 6 subcommands (list, start, stop, attach, open, status) for centralized server management across projects. All tests passing, integration with port registry and tmux verified.

title: Add orch clean --phantoms to cleanup phantom agents
description: From og-feat-implement-islive-beadsid-22dec: Phantom agents are those where LivenessResult.IsPhantom() is true (no tmux, no opencode session, but workspace exists). Add --phantoms flag to orch clean to identify and remove these.

title: Add SESSION_HANDOFF.md template for orchestrator context preservation
description:
From design-minimal-artifact-taxonomy investigation:

**Problem:** Orchestrator sessions end without structured handoff. Next session starts cold, loses context about active agents, pending work, blocking issues.

**Solution:** SESSION_HANDOFF.md template that captures:
- Active agents and their status
- Blocking issues (PRs waiting, bugs, external deps)
- Key decisions made this session
- Recommended next actions

**Template:**
```markdown
# Session Handoff - {date}

## TLDR
[1-2 sentence summary]

## Active Agents
| Agent | Task | Status |
|-------|------|--------|

## Blocking Issues
- [issue]: [why blocked]

## Decisions Made
- [decision]: [rationale]

## Next Session
1. [priority action]
2. [follow-up]
```

**Integration:**
- `orch handoff` command to generate from current state
- Or manual creation in .orch/SESSION_HANDOFF.md

Ref: .kb/investigations/2025-12-21-design-minimal-artifact-taxonomy.md

title: Restore discovered_work.go from pre-spiral (c4e117a7)
description:
## Problem

Current follow-up handling in `orch complete` (lines 280-355) prompts for each item but allows quitting early with 'q' - items silently drop.

The lost `discovered_work.go` from c4e117a7 had a **gate** that required disposition of ALL items, with skip-all requiring a documented reason.

## Current State

`complete_cmd.go:280-355` has:
- Parses NextActions, AreasToExplore, Uncertainties from SYNTHESIS.md
- Prompts `[y/N/q to quit]` for each
- Creates beads issues for 'y' responses
- **Problem:** 'q' exits silently, 'N' skips silently - no forcing function

## What Was Lost (c4e117a7)

`pkg/verify/discovered_work.go` had:
- `CollectDiscoveredWork()` - same parsing
- `PromptDiscoveredWorkDisposition()` - y/n/skip-all prompts
- **Gate:** skip-all required documented reason
- **Gate:** completion blocked until all items dispositioned

## Implementation

Restore the gating behavior from discovered_work.go:
1. Restore `pkg/verify/discovered_work.go` from c4e117a7
2. Restore `pkg/verify/discovered_work_test.go` from c4e117a7
3. Integrate gate into complete_cmd.go (replace current loose prompting)
4. Respect --force to bypass

## Source

```bash
git show c4e117a7:pkg/verify/discovered_work.go
git show c4e117a7:pkg/verify/discovered_work_test.go
```

## Success Criteria

- `orch complete` blocks until all discovered items dispositioned
- skip-all requires documented reason (prevents lazy dismissal)
- --force bypasses the gate
- Tests pass

title: Detect orchestrator frame collapse (doing worker work)
description:
## Problem

Orchestrators sometimes drop into worker-level work without realizing it. The price-watch orchestrator on Jan 6 spent hours manually fixing CSS bugs after agents failed, instead of escalating or trying different spawn strategies (like --mcp playwright).

The handoff documented 'Manual fixes by orchestrator' as a section - that's frame collapse.

## Why This Matters

- Orchestrator time is expensive (coordination, not implementation)
- Frame collapse compounds - once you start doing worker work, you keep doing it
- Orchestrators can't see their own frame collapse (that's why meta-orchestrator exists)

## Possible Solutions

1. **Detection in skill**: Add to orchestrator skill - 'If you're editing code files for >15 min, you've frame collapsed'
2. **Beads comment pattern**: Detect 'Phase: Manual fix' or similar patterns
3. **File edit heuristic**: If orchestrator session has >N lines of code changes, flag it
4. **Explicit escalation prompt**: After 3 failed agent spawns for same issue, prompt escalation

## Evidence

- pw-orch-resume-price-watch-06jan-bcd7 SESSION_HANDOFF.md shows manual fixes
- Meta-orchestrator reflection identified this pattern
close_reason: Investigated frame collapse detection. Key finding: self-detection unreliable, need multi-layer approach (skill guidance + SESSION_HANDOFF.md check + potential plugin). Created investigation with 5 findings and implementation recommendations.

title: Analyze understanding artifact architecture: Epic Model vs Understanding sections vs Models
description:
Strategic question: Are we creating redundant artifact types for forcing understanding before action?

Three artifacts exist:
1. Epic Model template (~/.orch/templates/epic-model.md) - Working document for probing problems (Probing → Forming → Ready)
2. Understanding sections (beads epic --understanding flag) - Required problem statement when creating epics
3. Models (.kb/models/) - General system understanding synthesized from investigation clusters

Questions to investigate:
- Are these manifestations of same 'artifact as gate' forcing function pattern?
- Is there redundancy? (Epic Model 1-page brief vs Understanding section)
- Should they be unified or kept distinct?
- Is Epic Model PROCESS and Understanding section OUTPUT?
- Are we layering artifacts when they might be different views of same thing?

Context:
- Jan 7 Strategic Orchestrator Model: synthesis is orchestrator work, epic readiness = can explain problem
- Jan 7 Epic readiness gate: bd create --type epic requires --understanding flag
- Jan 12 Models decision: created .kb/models/ for general system understanding
- Price-watch orchestrator noted missing Understanding sections in epics

Goal: Understand if current architecture is coherent or if we're creating artifact proliferation/debt

References:
- .kb/investigations/2026-01-07-inv-epic-readiness-gate-understanding-section.md
- .kb/decisions/2026-01-12-models-as-understanding-artifacts.md
- ~/.orch/templates/epic-model.md
close_reason: Analysis complete. Three artifacts (Epic Model template, Understanding sections, Models) are coherent lifecycle progression representing different temporal scopes (session → epic → domain), not redundancy. Perceived redundancy stems from implicit lifecycle documentation. Recommendation: Document lifecycle explicitly (.kb/guides/understanding-artifact-lifecycle.md), update orchestrator skill, keep current architecture. See investigation: .kb/investigations/2026-01-13-inv-analyze-understanding-artifact-architecture-epic.md

title: Epic: Pressure Visibility System - Operationalize Pressure Over Compensation
description:
## Goal

Operationalize the Pressure Over Compensation principle by building three layers that make surfacing failures visible and create pressure for system improvement.

## Context

From investigation `.kb/investigations/2025-12-25-inv-pressure-over-compensation-surfacing-mechanisms.md`:
- 8 surfacing mechanisms exist (kb context, hooks, daemon, beads, spawn context, orch status, synthesis, completion)
- 4/8 are passive (require human to invoke)
- Compensation is currently frictionless - human can paste context without friction
- The gap is a 'Pressure Visibility Layer' that detects, surfaces, and learns from gaps

## Scope

**In Scope:**
- Gap Detection: Detect when orchestrator/agent lacks context it should have
- Failure Surfacing: Make gaps visible and painful to ignore
- System Learning Loop: Convert gap observations into mechanism improvements

**Out of Scope:**
- Changing existing surfacing mechanisms (kb context, hooks, etc.)
- Automated context injection (hooks already do this)
- Agent-level introspection (out of scope)

## Success Criteria

- [ ] Orchestrator sees gap warnings when context is sparse
- [ ] Compensation patterns are tracked and visible
- [ ] At least 1 gap converts to system improvement per week (beads issue, kn entry, hook)

## Related

- Principle: `~/.kb/principles.md` - Pressure Over Compensation
- Decision: `~/.kb/decisions/2025-12-25-pressure-over-compensation.md`
- Investigation: `.kb/investigations/2025-12-25-inv-pressure-over-compensation-surfacing-mechanisms.md`
close_reason: All 3 children complete - gap detection, failure surfacing, and learning loop implemented

title: Gap Detection Layer - Detect missing context at spawn and completion time
description:
## Context

Part of epic orch-go-re8n (Pressure Visibility System).

The first layer of the Pressure Visibility System: detecting when context is missing.

## Task

Implement gap detection at key moments in the orchestration workflow:

1. **Pre-spawn gap detection:**
   - kb context returns sparse results (<3 matches) → flag
   - Task keywords don't match any knowledge → warning
   - Prior related investigations exist but weren't surfaced → gap detected

2. **Post-completion gap detection:**
   - Agent asked clarifying questions that should have been in spawn context → gap
   - SYNTHESIS.md shows agent 'had to figure out' something documented elsewhere → gap
   - Agent created kn entries that suggest missing context → gap

3. **Session gap detection:**
   - Orchestrator asks for context that kb should have → gap
   - Human pastes context (detected via hook?) → compensation detected

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Sparse kb context warning shows prominently at spawn time
- [ ] Gap detection flags are returned by spawn command
- [ ] At least 3 gap detection heuristics are implemented

## Implementation Notes

See investigation: `.kb/investigations/2025-12-25-inv-pressure-over-compensation-surfacing-mechanisms.md` Finding 5 for three-layer model.
close_reason: Implemented gap detection layer: pkg/spawn/gap.go with quality scoring (0-100), four gap types, integrated into spawn workflow. 25 tests passing.

title: Failure Surfacing Layer - Make gaps visible and painful to ignore
description:
## Context

Part of epic orch-go-re8n (Pressure Visibility System).

The second layer: once gaps are detected, surface them prominently.

## Task

Implement visible surfacing for detected gaps:

1. **Spawn-time surfacing:**
   - Show gap warnings prominently (not buried in logs)
   - Consider: block spawn if gap score is too high (gate, not reminder)
   - Show 'you compensated here last session' if pattern detected

2. **Dashboard surfacing:**
   - Add gap visibility to swarm dashboard
   - Show agents that had to compensate
   - Show recurring gap patterns

3. **Session surfacing:**
   - SessionStart hook could show recent gaps
   - 'Pressure report': what the system is missing

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Gap warnings display prominently at spawn time (not just logs)
- [ ] Dashboard shows gap patterns across agents
- [ ] Gaps are harder to ignore than to fix (friction added to compensation path)

## Implementation Notes

Reference Gate Over Remind principle: 'Enforce through gates (cannot proceed without), not reminders (easily ignored).'

Depends on: orch-go-re8n.1 (Gap Detection Layer)
close_reason: Abandoned: Stuck at Implementing phase for 13+ minutes - will respawn

title: System Learning Loop - Convert gap observations into mechanism improvements
description:
## Context

Part of epic orch-go-re8n (Pressure Visibility System).

The third layer: convert observed gaps into system improvements.

## Task

Implement the learning loop that converts gaps into improvements:

1. **Gap → Issue suggestion:**
   - Detected gap suggests beads issue creation
   - 'This gap occurred 3 times - create issue?'
   - Auto-populate issue with gap context

2. **Gap → Knowledge suggestion:**
   - Detected gap suggests kn entry
   - 'Add constraint? Add decision?'
   - Track which gaps become knowledge vs which recur

3. **Pattern analysis:**
   - Which topics frequently lack context?
   - Which skills/spawns have highest gap rates?
   - Recommend hook additions or kb entries

4. **Improvement tracking:**
   - Did the created issue/knowledge reduce gap rate?
   - Feedback loop: improvements that don't reduce gaps → review

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Recurring gaps automatically suggest beads issues
- [ ] Gap → knowledge path is one command
- [ ] System tracks improvement effectiveness

## Implementation Notes

The goal: make the system learn from gaps rather than relying on human compensation.

Depends on: orch-go-re8n.1 (Gap Detection), orch-go-re8n.2 (Failure Surfacing)
close_reason: Implemented System Learning Loop: GapTracker, RecordGap, FindRecurringGaps + orch learn subcommands (suggest/patterns/skills/effects/act). 26 tests pass.

title: Untracked agents have no phase visibility - CLI shows stalled, dashboard shows active
description:
Visibility gap for untracked spawns (--no-track):

1. CLI shows 'stalled' with phase '-' because no beads comments exist
2. Dashboard shows 'active' based on OpenCode session status, no stalled warning
3. No phase reporting mechanism without beads

Evidence: Agent orch-go-untracked-1767118797 has 45 messages, CLI shows stalled, dashboard shows active with 'Completed step'.

Fix needed (both):
1. Untracked spawns should report phase somewhere (workspace .phase file? registry metadata?)
2. Dashboard should show stalled warning like CLI does (idle time heuristic)

This breaks orchestrator visibility when using --no-track for ad-hoc work.
close_reason: Fixed untracked agent phase visibility. Dashboard now shows 'stalled' status for untracked agents with no phase after 1 min. Added workspace .phase file mechanism for alternative phase reporting. All tests pass.

title: Add timeout handling for stuck/untracked agents in orch review
description: orch review shows NEEDS_REVIEW for agents that will never complete: 1) Untracked agents (orch-go-untracked-*) with no phase status - these should be auto-filtered or shown separately 2) Agents stuck in non-Complete phases for >24h - should be flagged as 'stale' not 'needs review'. Add --stale flag to show these separately, and exclude them from default review output. Current state: 20+ untracked orphans and 8+ stuck agents cluttering the review backlog.
close_reason: Added staleness/untracked filtering to orch review. Default output now shows 134 actionable completions instead of 235 (101 stale/untracked hidden). New flags: --stale, --all. Tests pass.

title: Daemon spawns duplicate agents for same issue
description:
## Problem

Daemon spawned 4 agents for the same issue (kb-cli-0kk) during a single polling period. Each got a different workspace but same beads ID.

## Evidence

```
ses_46ad2e968ffeGBAPqVBDtVDptN | kc-feat-implement-kb-ask-06jan-3981 [kb-cli-0kk]
ses_46ad423b8ffe0ntedvPrB4ekVH | kc-feat-implement-kb-ask-06jan-b2a7 [kb-cli-0kk]
ses_46ad55b1affeES3B6QdMhokSog | kc-feat-implement-kb-ask-06jan-9f72 [kb-cli-0kk]
ses_46ada4c17ffe5LqOLHr5bdfEop | kc-feat-implement-kb-ask-06jan-ba74 [kb-cli-0kk]
```

## Expected Behavior

Daemon should check if issue already has an active agent before spawning. Either:
1. Check beads issue status (in_progress = skip)
2. Check OpenCode sessions for matching beads ID
3. Track spawned issues in daemon state

## Impact

- Wasted compute (4x work for 1 issue)
- Dashboard clutter (4 cards for same work)
- Potential race conditions if agents modify same files
close_reason: Root cause: race between daemon poll and beads status update. Fix: SpawnedIssueTracker tracks issue IDs before spawn. Tests: go test ./pkg/daemon/... - 62 passed, 0 failed

title: feat-035: Session registry for orchestrator lifecycle tracking
description:
Create lightweight session registry at ~/.orch/sessions.json for orchestrator sessions.

## Context
Decision 2026-01-05-orchestrator-lifecycle-without-beads.md establishes that orchestrators should not use beads for lifecycle tracking. This is the foundation for the new approach.

## Implementation
- Create pkg/session/registry.go
- JSON file at ~/.orch/sessions.json
- Lock file for concurrent access (~/.orch/sessions.json.lock)
- CRUD operations: Register, Update, Unregister, List, Get

## Schema
```json
{
  "sessions": [
    {
      "workspace_name": "og-orch-ship-feature-05jan",
      "session_id": "ses_abc123...",
      "project_dir": "/Users/dylan/projects/orch-go",
      "spawn_time": "2026-01-05T10:30:00Z",
      "goal": "Ship the feature",
      "status": "active"
    }
  ]
}
```

## Success Criteria
- Registry file created on first session registration
- Concurrent access safe via lock file
- Tests for all CRUD operations

## References
- Decision: .kb/decisions/2026-01-05-orchestrator-lifecycle-without-beads.md
- Investigation: .kb/investigations/2026-01-05-inv-design-orchestrator-session-lifecycle-without.md
close_reason: Created pkg/session/registry.go with CRUD operations (Register, Update, Unregister, List, Get, ListActive, ListByProject), file locking for concurrent access, and 16 passing tests. Ready for Phase 2 integration with spawn/status commands.

title: Untracked agents need cleanup path
description:
## Problem

`--no-track` spawns (for self-tracking skills like investigation) create orphan agents in `orch status` with no completion path. They appear as idle/at-risk but:
- `orch abandon` fails (no beads issue)
- `orch complete` fails (no beads issue)
- Manual registry editing fails (unexpected structure)

## Evidence (session ses_474f)

Orchestrator spent ~15 turns trying to clean up untracked agents:
- Lines 70-275 of transcript
- Multiple jq attempts to edit registry
- Eventually gave up: 'Let me just focus on what's working'

## Options

1. **Untracked agents don't appear in `orch status`** - separate list via `orch status --untracked`
2. **Add `orch clean --untracked`** - explicit cleanup command
3. **Auto-cleanup after idle timeout** - untracked sessions expire after N minutes of inactivity

## Principle

**Graceful Degradation** - `--no-track` is a valid option but creates debris that pollutes the system.
**Surfacing Over Browsing** - `orch status` surfaces agents but gives no actionable path for untracked ones.
close_reason: Fixed orch abandon and orch complete to handle untracked agents. Tests: go test ./cmd/orch/... -run 'Untracked|Abandon|Complete' - 4 passed. Smoke test: both commands now succeed for untracked agent IDs.

title: Update orchestrator skill: add dashboard troubleshooting protocol

Protocol when dashboard takes forever:

3. If running but slow: Check Network tab to identify slow endpoint
4. If specific endpoint slow: Search for existing investigation (kb context 'dashboard <endpoint> slow')

title: orch status shows agents for closed beads issues - should filter or mark as stale
description:
Two related issues:

1. **Agents going idle without Phase: Complete** - Some agents commit work but don't report final phase, causing orch complete to require --force

2. **orch status shows stale sessions** - After issues are closed, OpenCode sessions remain in registry showing as idle/AT-RISK

3. **orch complete silently fails** - Said 'Closed beads issue: 57dn' but issue remained in_progress

Root cause may be agents exhausting context before completing the phase reporting, or a race condition in orch complete's bd close call.

Evidence:
- 80tq reported Phase: Complete, orch complete worked
- 57dn did NOT report Phase: Complete, orch complete --force said it closed but didn't persist
- lxcc, 0xra, 71pa all required --force and show as stale in orch status
close_reason: Root cause found: FallbackClose and CloseIssue missing directory context - see investigation

title: orch clean --verify-opencode deletes orchestrator's own session
description:
## Problem

`orch clean --verify-opencode` can delete the orchestrator's own active session, causing OpenCode to crash with 'No user message found in stream' error.

The issue occurs in `cleanOrphanedDiskSessions()`:
1. Lists ALL disk sessions for the project directory
2. Gets session IDs tracked via workspace files (`.orch/workspace/*/SESSION_ID`)
3. Deletes any session on disk that isn't in a workspace file

The orchestrator's session is running in the same project directory, is on disk, but ISN'T tracked in a workspace (it's not a spawned agent - it's the main interactive session). So it gets flagged as 'orphaned' and deleted while still running.

After deletion, `SessionPrompt.loop()` continues running but finds no messages → crashes at `packages/opencode/src/session/prompt.ts:273`.

## Evidence

- `cmd/orch/main.go:3064-3069` - Logic that marks sessions as orphaned if not in workspace files
- `cmd/orch/main.go:3093` - `client.DeleteSession(session.ID)` deletes the orphan
- `packages/opencode/src/session/prompt.ts:273` - Crash site: `if (!lastUser) throw new Error('No user message found in stream...')`
- Prior knowledge constraint: 'OpenCode x-opencode-directory header returns ALL disk sessions'

Reproduction:
1. Start OpenCode in orch-go directory
2. Run `orch clean --verify-opencode`
3. Observe: Orchestrator's session is listed as orphaned and deleted
4. Crash: 'No user message found in stream'

## Context

This is a race condition during cleanup. The orchestrator is essentially deleting its own session out from under itself. The fix needs to skip the currently active session when cleaning orphans.

Options for fixing:
1. Skip any session that is currently 'busy' (has active loop running)
2. Pass orchestrator's current session ID to clean and exclude it
3. Check if session was updated within last N seconds (recently active)
4. Query OpenCode for sessions with active status

Recommendation: Option 1 or 4 - use OpenCode's session status to skip busy sessions.
close_reason: Batch close from orch review

title: Daemon capacity count goes stale - needs reconciliation on each poll
description:
Daemon maintains in-memory count of active agents. When agents complete or are abandoned externally (via bd close, orch clean), the count becomes stale. Daemon thinks it's at capacity (3/3) when actual count is lower.

Had to restart daemon twice this session to unblock.

Fix: Reconcile active count against actual OpenCode sessions on each poll cycle, not just trust in-memory state.
close_reason: Fixed daemon capacity stuck by filtering OpenCode sessions to only those updated within 30 min. Root cause: DefaultActiveCount() was returning all 26 sessions including stale ones from days ago, so Reconcile() never freed slots.

title: Agent stall visibility - distinguish stuck vs waiting vs rate-limited vs confused
description:
**Problem:** No visibility into WHY an agent stopped making progress. Today's example: agent had fix ready, listed next steps, then stopped. Showed as 'running/Implementing' for 5+ minutes until we manually tailed it and sent 'continue'.

**Current state:**
- We see: phase, tokens, runtime, status (running/idle/stalled)
- We don't see: last action, stall reason, waiting-for-input state

**What we need:**
1. **'Waiting for input' state** - Agent idle but not stuck, just waiting for continuation
2. **Last activity content** - Not just 'Implementing' but 'listed 6 next steps, stopped'
3. **Stall reason detection** - Rate limited? Waiting? Confused? Hit error? All show same 'running' status now
4. **Proactive alert** - 'Agent idle 2min with pending work' should surface automatically

**Evidence:** Agent orch-go-zdja had 103 messages, fix ready, next steps listed, but appeared stuck. Only discovered via manual 'orch tail'. Sent 'continue' and it resumed immediately.

**Connects to:** orch-go-jb0w (Root Cause Thinking epic) - we built symptom visibility (phase/tokens) but not behavior visibility (what is agent doing, why did it stop)

title: orch spawn should auto-start OpenCode if not running
description:
## Problem

After computer restart, `orch spawn` fails with 'connection refused' because OpenCode server isn't running. User must manually start OpenCode first.

## Solution

When `orch spawn` detects OpenCode isn't reachable (connection refused on port 4096):

1. Start OpenCode server in background: `opencode serve --port 4096 &`
2. Wait for it to be ready (poll /session endpoint)
3. Proceed with spawn

## Alternative considered

- launchd/plist for auto-start on login - more system-level, less portable
- Require manual start - current behavior, friction after restarts

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] orch spawn auto-starts OpenCode if connection refused
- [ ] Waits for OpenCode to be ready before proceeding
- [ ] Works on both macOS and Linux
- [ ] Logs that it started OpenCode (user knows what happened)
close_reason: Fixed - orch spawn now auto-starts OpenCode if not running, waits up to 10s for it to be ready

title: Diagnose investigation skill 32% completion rate
description:
**Problem:** Investigation skill has 32% completion rate per `orch stats`, well below 80% threshold.

**Evidence from event analysis:**
- 295 investigation spawn events
- 116 completed (of unique beads IDs)
- 137 unique beads IDs never completed

**Abandonment patterns seen:**
- 'Stuck at Planning for X minutes'
- 'Stalled - no phase comments'
- 'No progress for 17+ minutes'

**Analysis approach:**
1. Sample 10 failed investigation workspaces
2. Check for SYNTHESIS.md (completed but not tracked?)
3. Look for patterns in task descriptions

**Success criteria:** Identify top 2-3 failure modes with actionable fixes.
close_reason: Phase: Complete - Investigation skill 32% rate is data quality artifact. 71% failures are test spawns, 14% are skill/task mismatches. True completion rate for tracked investigation work is ~91%. Recommendations: filter test spawns from stats, add skill/task mismatch warning.

title: Implement two-threshold ghost filtering for concurrency and display
description:
## Problem

Ghost agents (idle >4h, no Phase: Complete) accumulate and cause two problems:
1. **Block new spawns** - concurrency limit counts ghosts as "active"
2. **Clutter status output** - 45 idle agents shown alongside 5 running

## Solution

Filter ghosts at query time with two different thresholds:

### Concurrency check (aggressive, 1h)
```go
func isActiveForConcurrency(agent) bool {
    if agent.Status == "running" { return true }
    if agent.Status == "idle" && agent.LastActivity > time.Now().Add(-1*time.Hour) {
        return true
    }
    return false
}
```

### Display filter (conservative, 4h + state-aware)
```go
func isVisibleByDefault(agent) bool {
    if agent.Status == "running" { return true }
    if agent.Phase == "Complete" { return true }  // Always show, needs review
    if agent.LastActivity > time.Now().Add(-4*time.Hour) { return true }
    return false
}
```

## Files to modify

- `cmd/orch/spawn.go` - use isActiveForConcurrency() in limit check
- `cmd/orch/status.go` - use isVisibleByDefault(), add --all flag
- `cmd/orch/serve_agents.go` - dashboard filtering

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Can spawn new agent when 5+ ghosts exist but <5 active (1h threshold)
- [ ] `orch status` shows only actionable agents by default
- [ ] `orch status --all` shows everything including ghosts
- [ ] Phase: Complete agents always visible regardless of age
- [ ] Dashboard respects same filtering

## References

- Decision: `.kb/decisions/2026-01-15-ghost-visibility-over-cleanup.md`
close_reason: Implemented two-threshold ghost filtering (1h for concurrency, 4h for display). Created pkg/agent/filters.go with IsActiveForConcurrency and IsVisibleByDefault functions. Updated spawn_cmd.go, status_cmd.go, and serve_agents.go to use new filtering logic. All acceptance criteria met: spawns unblocked by ghosts, status shows actionable only, --all flag works, Phase:Complete always visible, dashboard filtered. SYNTHESIS.md committed.

description:
Symptoms observed:

2. Dashboard shows 'Starting up...' for agent running 7+ minutes

4. Agents with Phase: Complete still marked status: active

API evidence:
```
curl http://127.0.0.1:3348/api/agents | jq '[.[] | select(.status == "active")]'
# Returns 5 agents with name: null, 4 have phase: Complete but status: active
```

This breaks visibility into daemon-spawned work. Likely regression in serve.go agent detection or OpenCode session querying.

title: Bug: orchestrator spawn uses wrong context filename in claude command
description:
## Problem

When spawning an orchestrator, the tmux command runs:
```
cat "...SPAWN_CONTEXT.md" | claude --dangerously-skip-permissions
```

But orchestrator workspaces create `ORCHESTRATOR_CONTEXT.md`, not `SPAWN_CONTEXT.md`.

## Impact

- Orchestrators start with NO context (claude gets empty stdin)
- All orchestrator spawns today may have been affected
- Critical: orchestrators running blind

## Expected

Command should use `ORCHESTRATOR_CONTEXT.md` for orchestrator skill spawns.

## Provenance

Meta-orchestrator session 2026-01-14, Dylan observed in tmux output.
close_reason: Both bugs were already fixed on Jan 14, 2026 in commits f074433c (context file path) and 0d968976 (tmux session selection). Investigation confirmed fixes, tests added for regression coverage.

title: Orchestrator workspaces need clear visual distinction from workers
description:
Currently orchestrator session workspaces are hard to distinguish from worker workspaces when browsing .orch/workspace/ directories.

## Problem
Example: /Users/dylanconlin/Documents/work/SendCutSend/scs-special-projects/price-watch/.orch/workspace/pw-work-test-workdir-fix-05jan is actually an orchestrator session but nothing visually distinguishes it from worker workspaces.

## Current State
- Workers: og-feat-*, og-inv-*, og-arch-*, etc.
- Orchestrators: og-work-*, pw-work-*, meta-work-*
- The 'work' prefix exists but isn't obvious enough
- No file-level indicator inside the workspace

## Possible Solutions

### Option A: Stronger naming convention
- Orchestrators: og-orch-*, pw-orch-*, meta-orch-*
- More explicit than 'work'

### Option B: Marker file in workspace
- .orchestrator or .tier file indicating 'orchestrator' vs 'worker'
- Already have .tier file - ensure it's set correctly

### Option C: Both
- Naming convention for quick visual scan
- Marker file for programmatic detection

## Context
- Related to feat-035 through feat-038 (orchestrator lifecycle without beads)
- Session registry will help for active sessions, but workspace browsing still needs distinction

## Acceptance Criteria
- Can visually distinguish orchestrator from worker workspaces at a glance
- Programmatic detection works reliably (for orch status, orch complete, etc.)
close_reason: Implemented visual distinction for orchestrator workspaces: og-orch-* naming convention and .tier file with 'orchestrator' value for programmatic detection. Tests pass, commit c3944462.

title: Add 'no silent waiting' instruction to SPAWN_CONTEXT template
description:
**From investigation:** 2025-12-30-inv-agents-stop-mid-execution-pending.md

**Root cause:** Claude's conversational pattern treats step-lists as 'proposals awaiting confirmation', not skill guidance. Agents list next steps then stop, waiting for approval that never comes.

**Evidence:** Agent orch-go-zdja had fix ready, listed 6 steps, stopped for 20 minutes until orchestrator sent 'continue'. No skill guidance says 'wait' - this is model behavior.

**Fix:** Add to SPAWN_CONTEXT template (pkg/spawn/context.go):

'Listing steps is NOT a stopping point. If you write 'Next steps:' or a numbered action list, execute them immediately. Do not wait for confirmation. Silent waiting is a bug - you are either working, explicitly blocked (Status: BLOCKED), or done (Phase: Complete).'

**Placement:** AUTHORITY or STATUS UPDATES section

**Consistency:** Matches existing AGENTS.md pattern: 'NEVER say ready to push when you are - YOU must push'
close_reason: Added EXECUTION BEHAVIOR section to SPAWN_CONTEXT template in pkg/spawn/context.go. Instruction tells agents that listing steps is NOT a stopping point and they should execute immediately. Tests pass.

title: Detect sessions that spawn but never execute (silent failures)
description:
## Problem

Agent pw-oq32.4 session was created but never executed. ZERO beads comments - agent never ran a single tool call. Failure was completely silent and only discovered during manual post-mortem.

## Evidence

From batch spawn failure analysis:
- Session created: ses_49466ea0effe1f8WB85HOwX8v7
- Workspace exists with SPAWN_CONTEXT.md
- `bd show pw-oq32.4 --json` shows no comments
- No SYNTHESIS.md, no artifacts, no progress
- Complete task failure with no indication

## Solution

Add health check after spawn to detect sessions that fail to start.

**Option A: Spawn-time check**
- After spawn, wait for first beads comment (Phase: Planning) within 30-60s
- If timeout, warn: 'Session may have failed to start - no initial activity'
- Non-blocking warning, doesn't fail spawn

**Option B: Status-time check**
- `orch status` detects sessions with 0 comments after >1 minute
- Shows 'failed-to-start' or '⚠️ NO ACTIVITY' indicator
- `orch doctor` includes this check

**Option C: Both** (recommended)
- Spawn warns if no activity within timeout
- Status surfaces stuck sessions ongoing

## Related Issues

- orch-go-c1a8: orch status should detect dead/orphaned sessions
- orch-go-l7eu: Detect stale/zombie sessions after OpenCode restart

Consider consolidating these into unified 'session health' feature.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] orch spawn warns if no beads comment within 60s of spawn
- [ ] orch status shows indicator for sessions with 0 comments
- [ ] orch doctor checks for failed-to-start sessions

## References

- Source: .kb/investigations/2025-12-29-inv-batch-spawn-failure-analysis.md
close_reason: Implemented failed-to-start detection with 3 mechanisms: (1) 60s post-spawn comment monitoring with stderr warning, (2) '⚠️ stalled' indicator in orch status for agents >1 min with no comments, (3) Session Health check in orch doctor listing stalled sessions. All changes committed, tests passing.

title: Harden flaky time-based tests
description: Some tests depend on timing and can be flaky. Add proper synchronization, increase tolerances, or use test helpers for time-dependent assertions. See .kb/investigations/2025-12-30-audit-orch-go-comprehensive-codebase.md Issue 3.
close_reason: Created internal/testutil package with WaitFor/Eventually/YieldForGoroutine helpers. Fixed 12+ time.Sleep calls across 6 test files. All tests pass consistently across multiple runs.

title: Redesign 'Needs Attention' blocked issues section - currently misleading
description:
## Problem

The 'DECISION NEEDED' section shows '3 blocked issues' with action '→ bd blocked', but:

1. **Wrong framing** - 'Decision needed' implies human action required, but sequential epic phases being blocked is expected behavior
2. **Useless action** - Clicking 'bd blocked' just shows the same info, doesn't help decide anything
3. **No context** - Doesn't explain WHY it's surfaced or WHAT to do

Current blocked issues are P2 epic phases (6uli.3 blocked by 6uli.2, etc.) - this is correct dependency behavior, not a problem.

## What would be useful

Only surface blocked issues that actually need intervention:

| Scenario | Action Needed | Suggested UI Action |
|----------|---------------|---------------------|
| Blocked by CLOSED issue | Stale dep, unblock it | 'bd dep remove X Y' |
| Blocked by ABANDONED work | Cancel or reprioritize | 'bd close' or reassign |
| Circular dependency | Fix dep graph | Show cycle, offer fix |
| Blocked >7 days | Blocker may be stuck | Surface blocker status |
| Sequential phases | **Nothing** - expected | Don't surface at all |

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Don't surface naturally-blocked sequential work (blocked by open earlier phase)
- [ ] Only surface blocked issues where intervention helps
- [ ] Show actionable command, not just 'bd blocked'
- [ ] Explain the problem: 'Blocked by closed issue' not just 'blocked'
close_reason: Zombie issue reconciled - no active agent found

title: E2E verification for orchestrator session lifecycle
description:
## Problem

Orchestrator lifecycle improvements need end-to-end verification to avoid "agents say it works" without human observation.

## Scope

Verify the complete orchestrator session lifecycle works:
1. Spawn creates workspace with pre-filled SESSION_HANDOFF.md
2. Orchestrator progressively updates handoff during work
3. `orch status` shows orchestrator sessions correctly
4. Dashboard displays orchestrator sessions with accurate status
5. `orch complete` successfully closes orchestrator sessions
6. Bidirectional communication (meta→orch, orch→worker) functions

## Approach

- Manual E2E test with real orchestrator session
- Document test scenarios and expected behavior
- Consider automated smoke test for critical paths

## Dependencies

- Progressive SESSION_HANDOFF.md (new issue)
- orch-go-0r0m (complete command fix)
- orch-go-k300.8 (dashboard visibility)
- orch-go-pn7q (bidirectional communication)

## Acceptance

- [ ] Manual E2E test executed and documented
- [ ] All lifecycle stages observed working
- [ ] Any gaps filed as issues
notes: Blocked by: orch-go-gy27l, orch-go-0r0m, orch-go-k300.8, orch-go-pn7q

title: Dashboard 'Active Agents' section conflates working and dead agents
description:
**Problem:** Dashboard showed '11 Active Agents' when only 3 were truly active. Dead agents (from rate limiting) were mixed in, making the count misleading.

**Evidence this session:** Screenshot showed 11 Active but orch status showed 3 active + 78 dead. Had to mentally filter.

**Root cause:** activeAgents store intentionally includes dead agents 'so users know they need attention' (agents.ts line 241). But this makes Active count misleading.

**Code location:** web/src/lib/stores/agents.ts:240-241
```typescript
export const activeAgents = derived(agents, ($agents) =>
  $agents.filter((a) => a.status === 'active' || a.status === 'idle' || a.status === 'dead' || a.status === 'stalled')
);
```

**Possible solutions:**
1. Split into 'Working (3)' and 'Needs Attention (8)' sections
2. Change label from 'Active Agents' to 'Agent Sessions'
3. Add visual separator between truly active and dead

**Impact:** Orchestrator can't quickly see actual active count.
close_reason: Visual verified: Dashboard now shows Working section separate from Needs Attention. npm run check passes.

title: Dashboard: Follow orchestrator context (time + project filtering)
description:
## Problem

Dashboard shows "everything about all agents ever" when it should show "what's relevant to current orchestration context."

This is a **design artifact** from when there were 20 agents - the current design doesn't match actual usage patterns.

## Root Cause

Wrong mental model baked into API design:
- Current: "Give me ALL agents, ALL beads, ALL the time"
- Correct: "Give me what's relevant to my CURRENT context"

## The "Follow the Orchestrator" Design

Dashboard should filter by three dimensions:

### 1. Time (default: 12 hours)
- Most relevant work is recent
- Dropdown to load more (24h, 48h, 7d, all)
- Historical agents are for debugging, not monitoring

### 2. Project (follow orchestrator tmux cwd)
- Dashboard auto-switches when orchestrator changes project
- Poll `tmux display-message -t orchestrator -p '#{pane_current_path}'`
- Stability threshold prevents flicker during rapid switching
- **Prior art:** beads-ui-svelte/src/server/tmux-follower.ts

### 3. Multi-project exception (orch-go special case)
- Some orchestrator sessions coordinate across repos
- orch-go needs to see agents from orch-cli, beads, kb-cli, etc.
- Config: `{ follow: "tmux", projects: ["orch-go", "orch-cli", ...] }`

## Expected Impact

| Before | After |
|--------|-------|
| 426 agents loaded | ~10-20 agents (12h + project filter) |
| All beads issues | Just current project's issues |
| O(n²) investigation scan | Only scan matching project_dir |
| 6+ second API calls | Sub-100ms (small dataset) |

## Implementation Shape

1. **Time filter API** - `/api/agents?since=12h` (default), dropdown changes param
2. **tmux-follower** - Port from beads-ui-svelte (already built!)
3. **Project filter API** - `/api/agents?project=orch-go` or `projects=orch-go,orch-cli`
4. **Project config** - Which projects to show when following a given project
5. **Beads follows same filter** - `/api/beads?project=...`

## Reference
- .kb/investigations/2026-01-07-inv-dashboard-api-agents-performance-synthesis.md
- beads-ui-svelte/src/server/tmux-follower.ts (prior art)
- serve.go is a hotspot (35 fixes) - structural issues from wrong model
close_reason: Dashboard beads stats and ready queue now follow orchestrator tmux context via project_dir parameter. API endpoints updated, cache made project-aware, frontend wired to pass context.

title: Add default_tier config option to force full tier for all spawns
description:
## Problem
Light tier spawns skip SYNTHESIS.md, which means knowledge isn't externalized. Some users may prefer all spawns produce synthesis artifacts for better knowledge capture.

## Current State
- Per-spawn override exists: `orch spawn --full`
- No global config to change default behavior
- Light tier skills: feature-impl, reliability-testing, issue-creation

## Proposed Solution
Add to `~/.orch/config.yaml`:
```yaml
default_tier: full  # or 'light' to keep current behavior
```

When set to `full`, all spawns (including light-tier skills) would require SYNTHESIS.md on completion.

## Implementation
1. Add `DefaultTier` field to userconfig
2. Check config in `DefaultTierForSkill()` before falling back to skill defaults
3. `--light` flag should still override config when explicitly passed
close_reason: Added default_tier config option to ~/.orch/config.yaml. When set to 'full', forces all spawns to require SYNTHESIS.md regardless of skill defaults. Explicit --light/--full flags still override. Tests passing.

title: Verification: Replace --force with targeted --skip-{gate} flags
description:
## Problem
55% of completions (890/1599) use --force, which bypasses ALL verification gates. This makes the entire verification system ineffective - gates exist but are routinely circumvented.

## Evidence
- 890 forced completions out of 1599 total (55%)
- Recent trend unchanged: 110/200 (55%) in last 200 completions
- Gates causing most friction: test_evidence (14), visual_verify (6), git_diff (5)
- Constraint kb-e7cfe3: 'force flags should not bypass substantive gates only procedural checks'

## Root Cause
The friction of fixing verification failures > friction of --force
So --force becomes the path of least resistance

## Solution
Replace blanket --force with targeted bypass flags:

```bash
# Instead of:
orch complete <id> --force --reason 'docs only'

# Use specific bypasses:
orch complete <id> --skip-test-evidence --reason 'docs only'
orch complete <id> --skip-visual --reason 'backend change'
orch complete <id> --skip-git-diff --reason 'cross-repo plugin'
```

Each --skip-{gate} requires --reason and is logged to events for pattern analysis.

## Benefits
1. Other gates still run (don't throw baby out with bathwater)
2. Bypass patterns become data for gate calibration
3. Forces articulation of WHY gate doesn't apply
4. Can deprecate --force or make it require --i-know-what-im-doing

## Implementation
1. Add --skip-{gate} flags to complete_cmd.go (one per gate)
2. Each requires --reason (min 10 chars)
3. Log to verification.bypassed event with gate, reason, beads_id
4. Add bypass metrics to orch stats
5. Deprecation warning on --force suggesting specific flags

## Acceptance
- Can bypass individual gates with reason
- Other gates still enforced
- Bypass events logged for analysis
- --force shows deprecation warning

## Dependencies
Should come AFTER fixing false positive gates (orch-go-f5oed, q03vm, modaz, fhfhk) - otherwise we're just adding more friction
close_reason: Implemented 10 targeted --skip-{gate} flags, --skip-reason validation (10 char min), verification.bypassed event logging, and --force deprecation warning. 23 new tests, all passing.

title: glass: daemon-based shared browser control
description:
**Status:** Functional - glass binary built and installed at ~/bin/glass

**Implemented:**
- CDP daemon with persistent Chrome connection (pkg/chrome/daemon.go)
- Tab targeting via --tab index or --url pattern
- Focus file (~/.glass-focus) for default tab persistence
- Commands: url, title, tabs, snap, actions, actions-json, focus
- Assert command for orchestrator validation (url-contains, title-contains, selector-exists, text-contains)
- MCP server (glass mcp) with tools: glass_tabs, glass_page_state, glass_elements, glass_click, glass_type, glass_navigate, glass_focus

**Current issue:** MCP tools not appearing in orchestrator session. Investigation spawned (orch-go-ixxg) to determine why playwright_browser_* tools show but glass_* tools don't.

**Prereq:** Chrome must run with --remote-debugging-port=9222 (use launch-chrome.sh)

**Next:**
1. Fix MCP tool visibility issue
2. Dogfood with orchestrator for real browser tasks
3. Decide: disable playwright MCP for orchestrator, keep glass only?

See: ~/Documents/personal/glass/CLAUDE.md for full docs
close_reason: Fixed glass MCP tool double-prefix issue (glass_tabs → tabs in registration, becomes glass_tabs via opencode). Committed fix to glass repo. Investigation recommends disabling playwright MCP (failing, not needed when glass works). kn recorded: MCP tools should not include client name prefix.

title: Epic: Orchestrator Coaching Plugin
description: Build conversational coaching plugin that prevents orchestrator frame collapse. Probing complete (3 probes validated). Ready for implementation. Architecture: Plugin streams orchestrator transcript → coach session (always-on), coach provides real-time intervention. Patterns: Behavioral variation count (3+ without pause), circular return detection (cross-document parsing). Configuration: Highly configurable (initialization, patterns, intervention style).
close_reason: Epic MVP Complete: Orchestrator Coaching Plugin operational with bidirectional pattern detection. ✅ Delivered: Phase 1 (behavioral_variation detection via semantic tool grouping), Phase 2 (circular_pattern via cross-document parsing), Phase 3 (coach session streaming via SDK client.promptAsync), Phase 3.5 (Dylan pattern detection via chat.messages.transform). Architecture: Two-tier sensing pattern (cheap pattern matching → expensive LLM investigation). Testing: Create coach session, export ORCH_COACH_SESSION_ID, trigger patterns, monitor for 1 week to validate FP rate and token economics. Phase 4 (Configuration) deferred pending MVP results.

title: Headless spawns not discoverable by beads ID after completion
description: When spawning headless (--headless), the session is discoverable while running via OpenCode API. But after completion, there's no way to find it by beads ID because: (1) No tmux window exists to search, (2) Session may be gone from OpenCode in-memory list, (3) Workspace .session_id file exists but tail/question don't search workspaces by beads ID. Potential fixes: (1) Include beads ID in session title for API lookup, (2) Have tail/question search workspace directories by beads ID pattern, (3) Store beads_id→workspace mapping somewhere. Discovered during registry removal validation testing.

title: Investigate headless spawn silent failures - agents spawn but never execute
description: When spawning 4 agents rapidly (~45 seconds), the 4th agent (orch-go-ug79) created an OpenCode session but had 0 messages - never executed. Session ID ses_48fd9f00dffe was created, beads comment posted 'Phase: Planning', but no actual work happened. Possible causes: OpenCode API rate limiting, session creation race, message delivery failure. Need telemetry to detect this pattern (spawn succeeded but no tool calls within N seconds).
close_reason: Identified race condition in headless spawns: SendPrompt called immediately after CreateSession with no delay/verification. Recommended fix: add message verification with retry (poll GetMessages until count > 0). Investigation: .kb/investigations/2025-12-30-inv-headless-spawn-silent-failures-agents.md

title: Epic children should not have blocking dependency on parent
description:
**Problem:** When creating a child issue with `--parent`, beads creates a `depends-on` relationship. This means the child is blocked until the parent closes. But for epics, the parent closes when children complete - circular.

**Current behavior:**
```
bd create "Child task" --parent epic-xyz
# Creates: child depends-on epic-xyz
# Daemon rejects: "blocked by dependencies: epic-xyz (open)"
```

**Expected behavior:**
`--parent` should create a hierarchical relationship (child-of), not a sequential one (depends-on). Children should be independently spawnable while parent epic remains open.

**Workaround:**
```
bd dep remove child-id parent-id
```

**Evidence:**
- kb-8294e7 (constraint recorded)
- orch-go-lv3yx.3 required manual dep removal to become spawnable
- This friction recurs every time epics are created with children

**Related friction:**
- Labeling epic `triage:ready` doesn't propagate to children (separate issue)
close_reason: Phase: Complete - Fixed parent-child blocking bug. GetBlockingDependencies() now NEVER blocks on parent-child deps. Tests: go test ./pkg/beads/... -run Block - 12 passed, 0 failed. go test ./pkg/daemon/... - all passed. Smoke test: orch daemon preview no longer shows children blocked by parent.

title: Test issue v2 for verifying test evidence reporting
close_reason: Tests: go test ./pkg/beads/... - 59 passed, 10 skipped, 0 failed

title: Find command is always slow - need better tooling
description: Example: find ~ -name 'opencode.jsonc' -path '*/dotfiles/*' takes forever. Options: 1) Switch to fd (rust-based, respects .gitignore), 2) Wrapper to scope searches with sane defaults, 3) Better agent guidance to avoid broad finds. This affects agent efficiency.

title: Improve orch servers init main.go detection heuristics
description:
orch servers init incorrectly detected 'go run ./cmd/gendoc' as an API server for orch-go. The gendoc command is a doc generator, not a server.

Current behavior: Finds go.mod + any main.go → assumes it's a server on port 8080

Better heuristics needed:
- Look for http.ListenAndServe or net.Listen patterns in main.go
- Check if main.go imports net/http or web frameworks (gin, echo, chi, fiber)
- Skip cmd/ subdirectories that are clearly CLI tools (gendoc, migrate, etc.)
- Maybe require explicit port in code or config

Related: pkg/servers/detect.go

title: Audit orch-ecosystem for session-transition skill traces
description:
## Context

The `session-transition` skill is no longer being used. Need to identify all references across the orch-ecosystem to understand touchpoints before deciding how to proceed (remove, deprecate, or repurpose).

## Scope

Audit these repositories for any traces:

### Primary
- `~/Documents/personal/orch-go` - CLI, daemon, spawn logic
- `~/orch-knowledge` - Skills source, SKILL.md files
- `~/.claude/skills/` - Deployed skills

### Secondary  
- `~/Documents/personal/opencode` - Fork customizations
- `~/Documents/personal/kb-cli` - Knowledge CLI
- `~/Documents/personal/beads` - Issue tracking

## What to Look For

1. **Skill definitions** - `session-transition/SKILL.md`, skill.yaml entries
2. **Spawn references** - `orch spawn session-transition`, skill inference mappings
3. **Documentation** - CLAUDE.md mentions, guides, decisions
4. **Code references** - Hardcoded skill names, switch statements
5. **Config files** - Skill registries, daemon configs
6. **Beads issues** - Open issues referencing the skill

## Deliverable

A report listing:
- All files containing references
- Type of reference (definition, invocation, documentation, etc.)
- Recommendation per touchpoint (remove, update reference, leave as-is)

## Decision Needed After Audit

- **Remove entirely** - Delete skill, remove all references
- **Deprecate** - Mark as deprecated, redirect to replacement
- **Repurpose** - Keep structure, change purpose
- **Leave dormant** - Keep but don't actively use

## Notes

This is discovery/audit work - no changes until we review findings and decide approach.
close_reason: Phase: Complete - Audited 6 repos for session-transition traces. Found skill doesn't exist but 45+ references remain. 2 kb quick entries need updating; historical investigations preserved; workspace artifacts ephemeral. Full categorized report in investigation file.

title: skillc: Allow cross-directory dependencies to compile (defer validation to spawn time)
description:
Currently skillc fails to compile skills with dependencies that exist in other directories (e.g., meta-orchestrator depends on orchestrator, worker skills depend on worker-base).

The error: 'skill X depends on Y, but Y not found'

**Current behavior:** skillc validates all dependencies exist in the current compile context. Cross-directory deps fail validation.

**Desired behavior:** Skip validation for dependencies not found in current context. These are resolved at spawn time by orch-go's LoadSkillWithDependencies.

**Evidence:** Investigation skill.yaml already documents this: 'Dependencies are resolved at spawn time by orch-go's LoadSkillWithDependencies. Note: skillc doesn't support cross-directory deps yet, so compilation warns.'

**Fix location:** pkg/graph/graph.go TopologicalSort() - instead of erroring when dep not found, skip validation for that dep (or warn instead of error).

**Test:** After fix, 'skillc build --recursive' in orch-knowledge/skills/src should compile all 16 skills, not just 5.
close_reason: Modified TopologicalSort to skip validation for cross-directory dependencies (deps not in g.Skills). These are now silently skipped at compile time and resolved by orch-go at spawn time. All tests pass. Commit: d47d070

title: orch status shows different results depending on which project you run it from
description:
When running orch status from different project directories, the phase and other beads-dependent information shows differently.

## Root Cause
The status command uses GetCommentsBatchWithProjectDirs to fetch phase comments. When project directory isn't known for a beads ID, it falls back to current working directory. This means:

1. From orch-go: looks up orch-go-* in orch-go/.beads/ → finds comments → shows phase
2. From price-watch: looks up orch-go-* in price-watch/.beads/ → no comments → shows '-'

## Expected Behavior
orch status should show the same global view regardless of which directory you run it from. The status command should:
1. Determine the correct project directory for each beads ID (from the beads ID prefix or workspace metadata)
2. Look up comments from the correct project's .beads/ database

## Affected Code
- cmd/orch/status_cmd.go - builds beadsProjectDirs map
- pkg/verify/beads_api.go - GetCommentsBatchWithProjectDirs

## Related
- Cross-project agent spawning (--workdir flag) already handles this for spawn/complete
- The beads ID prefix (e.g., 'orch-go-', 'pw-') encodes the project
close_reason: Fixed cross-project beads lookup by deriving project directory from beads ID prefix. Tests: go test ./cmd/orch/... - PASS 81.284s. Reproduction verified: orch status from snap now shows phases correctly for orch-go agents.

title: Add comprehensive orch clean --all that cleans all 4 sources
description:
## Problem

Agent cleanup requires multiple commands because status aggregates from 4 sources:
- tmux windows
- OpenCode sessions
- beads issues
- workspaces

Current cleanup commands only address subsets, so agent count doesn't decrease uniformly.

## Solution

Add `orch clean --all` that comprehensively cleans all sources:
- Archive stale workspaces
- Remove completed OpenCode sessions
- Kill orphaned tmux windows
- Close completed beads issues

## Provenance

Cleanup orchestrator session 2026-01-14, found diminishing returns from existing cleanup commands.
close_reason: Phase: Complete - Implemented orch clean --all flag that enables comprehensive cleanup of all 4 agent status sources (tmux, OpenCode, beads, workspaces). Feature tested and working correctly.

title: Implement session start focus guidance (thread grouping from beads ready)
description:
Add focus guidance to orch session start that groups bd ready issues into thematic threads.

## Context
- Dylan liked the manual thread grouping done at session start
- Helps orchestrators orient: 'Here are your active threads. What's nagging you?'
- Part of Capture at Context principle - surface context when it matters

## Design

### Thread grouping logic:
1. Load bd ready issues
2. Group by common keywords/themes in title (e.g., 'model', 'session', 'kb', 'dashboard')
3. Also consider issue type (task, bug, feature) and labels
4. Present as named threads with issue IDs

### Output format:
```
📋 Focus Guidance (10 ready issues in 5 threads):

Thread: Session tooling      → orch-go-1c5ov, orch-go-xyz
  Notes: Capture at Context implementation

Thread: Model system         → orch-go-q1spg, orch-go-kpdg2, orch-go-2y1ag  
  Notes: Template/pattern updates

Thread: orch tooling         → orch-go-vnqgd, orch-go-9xtc0
  Notes: Escape hatch tracking, doctor improvements

Thread: Cleanup              → orch-go-xbkmp
  Notes: Empty investigation templates

What's nagging you or feels most important?
```

### Files to modify:
- cmd/orch/session.go - add focusGuidance() function
- Call from runSessionStart() after resume injection
- May need pkg/focus/guidance.go for grouping logic

### Grouping heuristics:
- Extract keywords from issue titles
- Common prefixes: 'session', 'model', 'kb', 'orch', 'dashboard', 'spawn'
- Fall back to 'Misc' thread for ungrouped
- Sort threads by issue count (largest first)
- Cap at 5-7 threads for readability
close_reason: Focus guidance implemented. Tests verified: pkg/focus - 20 tests PASS (thread grouping, formatting, keyword detection)

title: Fix spawn to use standalone mode with TUI (port Python approach)
description:
## Problem

Workers spawned via orch-go don't show the OpenCode TUI - they run in headless mode with raw text output. This breaks observability and the expected worker experience.

## Root Cause

orch-go uses `opencode run --model X --title Y "prompt"` which passes prompt as CLI argument, triggering headless mode. Python orch-cli uses standalone mode which renders full TUI.

## Current (broken)
```
opencode run --model anthropic/claude-opus --title og-feat-xyz "prompt"
# Runs headless, no TUI
```

## Expected (Python's approach)
```
opencode {project_dir} --model anthropic/claude-opus
# Wait for TUI ready (poll for visual indicators)
# Wait 1.0s for input focus
tmux send-keys -l "prompt"
tmux send-keys Enter
# Full TUI rendered
```

## Implementation

Port from Python orch-cli (spawn.py:837-1127):

1. **WaitForOpenCodeReady()** - Poll tmux pane for TUI indicators:
   - `┃` (thick vertical bar - prompt box)
   - `build` or `agent` (agent selector)
   - `alt+x` or `commands` (command hints)
   - Timeout: 15s, Poll interval: 200ms
   - Extra 1.0s wait after visual ready for input focus

2. **Modify BuildRunCommand()** - Change from:
   ```go
   args := []string{"run", "--model", cfg.Model, "--title", cfg.Title, cfg.Prompt}
   ```
   To standalone mode:
   ```go
   args := []string{cfg.ProjectDir, "--model", cfg.Model}
   ```

3. **Add send-keys prompt injection** - After TUI ready:
   ```go
   tmux.SendKeysLiteral(window, prompt)  // -l flag for special chars
   tmux.SendEnter(window)
   ```

4. **Optional: DiscoverSession()** - Query API to find session by directory + spawn time for later API interactions

## Reference

- Investigation: .kb/investigations/2025-12-20-design-explore-tradeoffs-orch-opencode-integration.md
- Python impl: orch-cli/src/orch/spawn.py:959-1032 (spawn_with_opencode)
- Python impl: orch-cli/src/orch/spawn.py:1083-1127 (_wait_for_opencode_ready)

## Validation

- Spawn agent via `orch spawn investigation "test"`
- Verify TUI renders in tmux window (header, token counter, purple status bar)
- Verify agent processes prompt correctly

title: Epic: Split cmd/orch/main.go into focused subcommand files
description:
## Goal

Reduce main.go from 2705 lines to ~300 lines by extracting commands into focused files following the established spawn_cmd.go/status_cmd.go pattern.

## Problem

main.go has 49 fix commits in 28 days - it's a god file. Changes to one command risk breaking others. Hard to navigate and test.

## Design

See: .kb/investigations/2026-01-04-inv-cmd-orch-main-go-49.md

## 4-Phase Plan

**Phase 1:** complete_cmd.go (~400 lines) - High complexity, verification/UI approval/auto-rebuild
**Phase 2:** clean_cmd.go (~350 lines) - Medium complexity, workspace scanning/phantom detection  
**Phase 3:** account_cmd.go + port_cmd.go (~430 lines) - Medium complexity, sub-command groups
**Phase 4:** Small commands (~510 lines) - send, tail, question, abandon, retries

## Success Criteria

- [ ] main.go reduced to ~300 lines (root + version + init)
- [ ] Each extracted file follows spawn_cmd.go pattern
- [ ] All tests pass after each phase
- [ ] No fix commits needed post-extraction (clean split)
close_reason: main.go reduced from 2705 to 195 lines (93% reduction). All 4 phases complete, tests pass.

title: Phase 1: Extract complete_cmd.go from main.go
description: Extract completeCmd and related handlers (~400 lines) into complete_cmd.go. Includes verification logic, UI approval flow, auto-rebuild. Follow spawn_cmd.go pattern.

title: Epic: Orch Ecosystem Consolidation (6-month)
description:
Consolidate 8 repos to 4 functional units. See .kb/investigations/2025-12-24-inv-full-ecosystem-audit-scope-simplify.md for full analysis.

## Phases

### Phase 1 (Month 1): kb absorbs kn
- Add kb quick subcommand with kn semantics
- Migrate .kn entries to .kb format  
- Deprecate kn binary

### Phase 2 (Month 2-3): orch-go reaches Python parity
- Port remaining Python features (synthesis, transcript, friction, stale, doc-check, lint, logs, history)
- Add deprecation warnings to Python orch-cli
- Update skill references

### Phase 3 (Month 4): beads abstraction
- Create pkg/beads/client.go interface
- Reduce direct bd CLI calls
- Enable mock testing

### Phase 4 (Month 5-6): Final cleanup
- Archive Python orch-cli
- Evaluate agentlog (archive or integrate)
- Consider skillc integration into orch-go

## Success Criteria
- Single knowledge CLI (kb) instead of two
- orch-go as only orchestration CLI
- All skill references point to valid tools
- beads interaction via abstraction layer

## Evidence
- orch-go: 37k LoC, 334 skill refs
- kb+kn: 231 combined skill refs, conceptual overlap
- agentlog: only 14 skill refs (orphaned)
- Python orch-cli: ~80% parity achieved
close_reason: Created epic orch-go-6uli with 5 child issues for 6-month ecosystem consolidation. Investigation at .kb/investigations/2025-12-27-inv-epic-orch-ecosystem-consolidation-month.md. All phases have triage:review label for orchestrator prioritization.
